MawaNet (our proposed model) has been compared with the most popular machine learning models, such as AdaBoost Classifier, Decision Tree Classifier, Gaussian Naïve Bayes, Gradient Boosting Classifier, Logistic Regression, Passive Aggressive Classifier, Quadratic Discriminant Analysis, Ridge Classifier, Self-Training Classifier, and SGD Classifier. The performance has been evaluated through precision, recall, F1-score, and accuracy. MawaNet achieves the highest score <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(99.99 \%)$</tex> in all performance evaluation metrics. The proposed architecture outperforms other traditional methods. The Decision Tree Classifier performs well with the score of 99.78 % across all metrics. These results demonstrate that the deep learning architecture is able to capture the non-linear and obscured patterns in transactional data, offering financial institutions a strong candidate for use in real-time systems to detect fraud.

title: Towards a Data-Driven Approach for Early Identification of at-Risk Students in Higher Education
authors: Ahmed Abrar Zayad, Ashim Dey, Annesha Das
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Predicting students attrition is key for higher education institutions, as it allows for timely and proper intervention for those identified as dropout students. This study proposes a machine learning procedure to assess the likelihood of student attrition using academic records, demographic features, and macroeconomic indicators (i.e., GDP and inflation) at the time of enrollment. In contrast to prior work that predicts Graduate, Enrolled, or Dropout outcomes, this study focuses on distinguishing the pupils who actually discontinued their studies from the students who graduated instead. Predictions were made after the first two semesters to facilitate early intervention. Since the dataset is highly imbalanced, SMOTE was applied for class balancing. Hyperparameter tuning using RandomSearchCV was performed, which helped improve the model's performance. Several classification algorithms, including XGBoost, Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and a Voting Classifier, were evaluated using multiple performance metrics. The tuned Voting Classifier attained the maximum level of accuracy of 92.15 %, demonstrating the effectiveness of machine learning algorithms with optimized parameters. The feature importance chart generated according to the voting classifier also shows that the average approval rate and whether a student is aware of their tuition payments are some of the most important factors for a student's outcome. These results demonstrate the possibilities of data-driven approaches in effectively helping students at risk and using resources.

title: Genetic Algorithm with Optimised Extra Tree Ensemble for Enhanced Intrusion Detection in IoMT Networks
authors: Rubiat Jahan Momtahina, Abdullah Rajib, Ashraful Goni, Fuad Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Clinical efficiency has been remarkably enhanced by the integration of Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) into the healthcare system. But at the same time, due to the integration of IoMT, the attack surface for cyber threats is also increased. In the case of domain-specific environments, current machine learning and deep learning models have shown a deficiency in computational scalability and adaptability. To reinforce intrusion detection in IoMT networks, a Genetic Algorithm with Optimised Extra Tree Ensemble (GA-ExtraTree) is developed in this work. For selecting discriminative features from the MedSec-2025 dataset, the Genetic Algorithm is used, and this also reduces dimensionality and redundancy. On the other hand, highly accurate classification with decreased computational load has been guaranteed by the optimised Extra Tree ensemble. Compared to the current baseline approaches, the proposed model performs better by achieving 99.35% detection accuracy. According to experimental outcomes, GAExtraTree is identified as a high-performing, low-overhead, and domain-transferable architecture for intrusion detection in realtime IoMT, and it will strengthen the robustness of the security infrastructure of modern medical network systems.

title: A Hyper-Heuristic Framework for Human-In-The-Loop Combinatorial Optimization
authors: Kassem Danach, Jomana Al Haj Hassan, Samir Haddad, Jinane Sayah, Merhej Joseph, Kallab Chadi
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Human-in-the-loop combinatorial optimization problems are characterized by dynamic objectives, evolving constraints, and frequent human intervention, posing significant challenges to classical automated optimization methods. This paper proposes a categorized selection hyper-heuristic framework designed to explicitly incorporate human feedback into the optimization process. Low-level heuristics are organized into constructive, improvement, perturbation, and reconstructive categories, enabling adaptive control of search behavior in response to both algorithmic performance and human acceptance signals. The framework integrates a two-stage heuristic selection mechanism, interaction-aware acceptance criteria, and termination conditions tailored to non-stationary optimization environments. The proposed approach is evaluated on a nurse rostering problem, a representative NP-hard combinatorial optimization task. Experimental results indicate that the proposed framework reduces the average number of human interventions by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 5 \%}$</tex> compared to a noncategorized hyper-heuristic, while improving solution stability by more than 50 % and maintaining competitive objective values. These results demonstrate that explicit heuristic categorization and acceptance-aware control provide effective mechanisms for addressing the challenges of human-in-the-loop combinatorial optimization.

title: Stomata-Inspired Reconfigurable Square Ring Slot Antennas for 5G Applications
authors: Buthaina Al Siyabi, Hassan Al-Lawati, Mohammed Bait-Suwailam, Joseph A. Jervase
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: This paper presents two antennas of a bioinspired reconfigurable square-ring slot for sub- 6 GHz 5 G applications, functionally motivated by the dynamic opening and closing behavior of plant stomata. By employing a single PIN diode, the proposed antennas simultaneously enable frequency and polarization reconfiguration, thereby reducing circuit complexity, biasing overhead, and power consumption. The antennas are fabricated on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 6 - m m}$</tex> thick FR4 substrate with a relative permittivity of 4.4 and a loss tangent of 0.02. The first antenna is for frequency-reconfigurable mode, the antenna operates in a triple-band state at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.6 \text{GHz}, 4.95 \text{GHz}$</tex>, and 6.85 GHz when the diode is ON, and in a dual-band state at 3.55 GHz and 6.8 GHz when the diode is OFF. Furthermore, the second antenna is for polarization reconfiguration, which is achieved by switching between linear (ON state) and circular polarization (OFF state), with a minimum axial ratio of 0.62 dB at 3.5 GHz. Owing to their compact geometry, low implementation complexity, and multifunctional reconfigurability, the proposed biomimetic antennas represent an efficient and cost-effective solution for adaptive sub-6 GHz 5G and Internet-of-Things (IoT) wireless systems.

title: DDoS Attack Detection in Cloud Systems Using a Hybrid Stacked Ensemble
authors: Nematullah Khan, Raad Alharthi, Fahad Alzahrani, Mourad Benmalek, Abdelouahid Derhab
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks remain a major threat to cloud infrastructure, while many existing defenses provide only coarse protocol-level detection and struggle to adapt to evolving attack patterns. This paper proposes an AI-driven stacked ensemble for cloud DDoS detection and finegrained TCP-based attack typing. The framework uses two stages: (i) an SVM for binary flow classification (benign vs. non-benign) and (ii) three deep meta-learners (LSTM, CNN, and Autoencoder) to classify 17 TCP-based DDoS variants and suspicious traffic, with majority voting for the final decision. Experiments on the AWS-collected BCCC-cPacket-Cloud-DDoS2024 dataset show 94.28% accuracy for the SVM, 94.20% for the best meta-learner, and 92.72% accuracy for the ensemble, achieving a weighted F1-score of 0.92. Results indicate strong performance on volumetric attacks and stable behavior across diverse categories, supporting the practicality of heterogeneous ensembles for cloud DDoS defense.

title: Adaptive First-Person Shooter: Real-Time Game Difficulty Adjustment Based on Player Stress Levels
authors: Georges Chaiban, Nabil Moussa, Bilal Zahraman, Joseph Azar
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Video games, especially first-person shooter (FPS) games, often involve intense gameplay that can elicit aggressive responses. This paper proposes a stress-adaptive FPS game that dynamically adjusts gameplay based on the player's real-time emotional state. The system combines speech analysis via a large language model (LLM), heart rate monitoring, and in-game performance metrics to compute an aggression score using a weighted algorithm. Based on this score, the game applies inverse difficulty scaling and, when extreme aggression is detected, transitions the player to a calming environment with relaxation tasks. Grounded in established psychological frameworks including the General Aggression Model and Flow Theory, this work demonstrates how real-time multi-modal emotion monitoring can be integrated into the gaming domain to create an adaptive, personalized experience focused on player well-being.

title: Machine Learning Approaches for RADAR-Based Warhead Target Classification Under Clutter Conditions
authors: Md. Abdur Rouf, Md. Selim Hossain, Mst. Azmira Khanam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: This study presents a machine learning method that effectively classifies warhead-type targets from non-warhead-type targets using a simulated radar dataset. Key radar characteristics, including Radar Cross Section (RCS), Doppler shift, speed, angle, frequency, micro-Doppler signatures, and range profiles, were incorporated into a synthetic dataset. The dataset is generated in two different scenarios in realistic conditions with additional environmental clutter, such as rain and urban reflections, and another one in ideal conditions without clutter. A Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier showed notable performance degradation 53.75 % when trained on cluttered data, but it achieved nearperfect accuracy of 99.5% on the clean dataset. The Principal Component Analysis was used as a feature optimization method to suppress the clutter, including variance, and reduce feature redundancy. The classification accuracy for the clutter condition increased to 85.7 % with the incorporation of PCA. These findings underscore the importance of feature optimization in enhancing radar-based classification performance in challenging operating environments.

title: Integrating Pathway Enrichment with Graph Attention Networks for Pan-Cancer Classification
authors: Anika Tasnim, Propa Punam, Md. Salahuddin Fahad, Tasnim Ahmed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Large-scale pan-cancer transcriptomic resources such as TCGA provide unprecedented opportunities to identify features that are both predictive and biologically meaningful. However, the high dimensionality and heterogeneity of RNA-seq data pose challenges for robust feature selection and generalizable classification. In this study, we present a hybrid computational framework that integrates mutual information and LASSO filtering with pathway-guided Graph Attention Networks (GATs) to extract a compact set of significant features from TCGA PanCancer RNA-seq data spanning 33 tumor types (n = 10,826 samples). Our pipeline reduced more than 60,000 genes to 32 key features while preserving predictive power. Using SMOTEbalanced training and multiple classifiers (Random Forest, Light-GBM, XGBoost, DNN, and an ensemble model), we achieved 96.21% overall classification accuracy and consistently high precision, recall, and F1-scores across cancer types. Functional enrichment analysis of the selected features revealed biologically coherent pathways associated with tumorigenesis, highlighting the potential of our approach to identify functionally relevant and highly discriminative transcriptomic signatures in pan-cancer studies.

title: Design and Characterization of a Tendon-Driven Soft Robotic Gripper for Lightweight Manipulation
authors: Matthew Lee, Indie Heinemann-Rueda, Yen-Lin Han
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: This paper presents the design, fabrication, computational, and experimental validation of a tendon-driven soft robotic gripper for lightweight manipulation tasks with relevance to assistive hand function. The gripper features a bio-inspired three-joint finger design fabricated as a single monolithic piece from thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) via fused deposition modeling (FDM) 3D printing, eliminating assembly requirements and potential failure points. Comprehensive finite element analysis using hyperelastic Mooney-Rivlin material models validates the mechanical design through full-finger and individual joint simulations. Full-finger analysis under 1 N tensile load characterizes overall bending behavior, while individual joint simulations under a 0.1 N load reveal detailed stress distributions. The gripper integrates with a HiWonder xArm robotic arm platform in a three-finger radial configuration, actuated by a single servo motor via fishing line tendons. Preprogrammed motion sequences enable execution of manipulation tasks without requiring low-level servo control. Experimental pick-and-place testing across objects of varying weights and geometries demonstrates the gripper's functional manipulation capabilities, with qualitative observations confirming passive conformability to object contours and stable grasping for cylindrical, spherical, cubic, and irregular objects. The computational and experimental findings validate the design's fundamental grasping mechanics and establish the viability of single-piece 3D-printed tendon-driven grippers as an accessible platform for future soft robotic assistive technology research.

title: A Vision-Based Four-Cable-Driven Positioning System with Cascade Control: Experimental Evaluation
authors: Gianmarco Humpiri, Piero Reyes, Medard Quispe, Elvis J. Alegria
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: Effective water quality monitoring in large-scale aquaculture remains a challenge due to the limited spatial coverage of fixed sensors and the operational constraints of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs). To address this, this paper presents the design and validation of a scalable four-cable-driven parallel robot (CDPR) with a four-corner architecture tailored for autonomous sensor deployment. The system implements a cascade control strategy, integrating a low-level motor current loop with a high-level vision-based kinematic loop to ensure precise end-effector positioning under dynamic uncertainties. Although the mechanical design supports five degrees of freedom (5-DoF), this study focuses on validating the translational performance required for environmental sensing. Experimental validation was conducted using square and circular trajectories under varying payloads. Results demonstrate that the system maintains a consistently low normalized tracking error even under maximum load conditions, confirming the robustness and feasibility of the proposed architecture as a cost-effective solution for smart aquaculture monitoring.

title: Analysis and Optimization of Fluid Antenna-Aided Cell-Free Massive MIMO With Imperfect CSI
authors: Feiyang Li, Qiang Sun, Dong Li, Jiayi Zhang, Chan-Byoung Chae, Kai-Kit Wong
abstract: Cell-free massive multiple-input multiple-output (CF massive MIMO) is regarded as a promising technology for next-generation wireless communication systems. However, conventional CF massive MIMO systems typically employ fixed-position antennas (FPAs) at access points (APs), which limits the exploitation of spatial degrees of freedom (DoFs) for antenna position optimization. To address this issue, we propose the use of fluid antennas (FAs) in place of FPAs, enabling more DoFs at APs and leading to a novel FA-aided CF massive MIMO (FA-CF) architecture. In this paper, we investigate the uplink spectral efficiency (SE) of FA-CF systems with imperfect channel state information (CSI). We design a minimum mean-square error (MMSE)-based channel estimation scheme to estimate the aggregated channels between APs and user equipments (UEs). We further derive achievable SE expressions for both centralized and distributed processing schemes, including fully centralized processing (FCP), large-scale fading decoding (LSFD), and equal-gain decoding processing (EGDP). Moreover, we formulate a mean-square error (MSE) minimization problem based on the signal transmission model. To solve this problem, we develop an efficient algorithm that combines orthogonal matching pursuit (OMP) with binary search to jointly optimize FA positions and the combining matrix. In addition, we propose a protective weak-ordering (PWO) strategy to enhance the SE of the FCP scheme. Numerical results demonstrate that FA-CF significantly outperforms conventional CF systems in terms of SE, even with a limited number of antennas, and maintains strong robustness under imperfect CSI or heavy UE loads by adaptively adjusting antenna positions. These results highlight FA-CF as a promising architecture offering enhanced SE and robustness for future wireless systems, particularly in scenarios where large-scale AP deployment is infeasible or cost-constrained.

title: Adaptive IR-HARQ System With Deep Learning-Based Early Prediction for URLLC Service
authors: Narayan Prasad Kusi, Dong Ho Kim
abstract: Ultra-high reliability and low latency communication (URLLC) is a crucial concept in the realm of 5G and beyond. URLLC has a demand for short packets with an end-to-end latency of 1 ms with a success probability of 99.999%. The stringent reliability and latency requirements of URLLC significantly affect the hybrid automatic repeat request (HARQ) mechanism at the PHY/MAC layer. In this paper, we propose an adaptive incremental redundancy HARQ (IR-HARQ) scheme that leverages deep learning techniques to predict the decoding reliability of a received codeword before the actual decoding process. Additionally, we propose novel packet transmission schemes suitable for IR-HARQ. In particular, the proposed concept enables the transmitter to respond more quickly by receiving early feedback (FB) on decoding reliability and simultaneously transmitting the required incremental redundancy versions (RVs) using the proposed transmission schemes. We also propose an early prediction method based on long short-term memory (LSTM) using novel features to accurately predict decoding reliability. This method achieves more than 98% prediction accuracy for a code rate of 2/5, and over 96% for code rates of 5/6 and 3/4 in quasi-static fading channel environment. The proposed adaptive IR-HARQ, combined with the packet transmission schemes, significantly improves throughput and bandwidth efficiency compared to conventional IR-HARQ, while meeting the latency requirements of URLLC.

title: Pixels to Pursuit: Reinforcement Learning-Based Control Strategies for UAV-AGV Collaborative Systems
authors: Ankit Mehra, Darshankumar Prajapati, Pushkar Kumar, Ashish Rana, Urvashi Goswami, Amit Shukla
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: This paper presents reinforcement learning-based control strategies for vision-based UAV tracking by AGVs. We developed custom YOLOv8 object detection models achieving 98 % accuracy and implemented DQN and DDPG controllers trained in simulation environments. Experimental validation demonstrates DDPG outperforms PID, SMC, and DQN controllers with superior tracking accuracy and reduced settling time. The approach enables robust UAV-AGV collaboration in GPS-denied environments with potential applications in agriculture and search-and-rescue operations.

title: GALA: Guided Attention with Language Alignment for Open Vocabulary Gaussian Splatting
authors: Elena Alegret, Kunyi Li, Sen Wang, Siyun Liang, Michael Niemeyer, Stefano Gasperini, Nassir Navab, Federico Tombari
abstract: 3D scene reconstruction and understanding have gained increasing popularity, yet existing methods struggle to capture fine-grained, language-aware 3D representations from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 D$</tex> images. In this paper, we present GALA, a novel framework for open-vocabulary 3D scene understanding with 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS). GALA distills a scenespecific 3D instance feature field via self-supervised contrastive learning. To further extend this to generalized language feature fields, we introduce a core contribution of GALA, a cross-attention module with two learnable codebooks that encode view-independent semantic embeddings. This design not only ensures intra-instance feature similarity but also supports seamless 2D and 3D open-vocabulary queries. It reduces memory consumption by avoiding perGaussian high-dimensional feature learning. Extensive experiments on real-world datasets demonstrate GALA's remarkable open-vocabulary performance on both <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 D$</tex> and 3D.

title: SCIGaussian-D: Dynamic Scene Reconstruction from a Single Snapshot Compressive Image
authors: Yunhao Li, Yuze Yang, Yanan Hu, Xiaoyue Li, Yong Tang, Xin Yuan, Peidong Liu
abstract: In this paper, we explore the potential of snapshot compressive imaging (SCI) for dynamic 3D scene reconstruction from a single temporal compressed image. SCI is a low-cost imaging technique that captures high-dimensional information-such as temporal data-using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 D$</tex> sensors and coded masks, significantly reducing data bandwidth while offering inherent privacy advantages. While recent advances in Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF) and 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) have enabled 3D reconstruction from SCI measurements, these methods are fundamentally limited to static scenes and fail to generalize to dynamic content. To address this, we propose SCIGaussian-D, a novel framework that enables dynamic 3D reconstruction from a single SCI image. Our method represents the scene with 3D Gaussians defined in a canonical space and models motion using learnable deformation fields. By incorporating the SCI imaging model into the training loop, SCIGaussian-D directly reconstructs the dynamic 3D scene and recovers the corresponding camera motion from a single SCI. We evaluate our method on both synthetic and real SCI datasets, demonstrating significant improvements in reconstruction quality over existing baselines. Our results establish a new state of the art for dynamic scene reconstruction within the SCI framework, paving the way for practical applications in high-speed imaging and real-time scene rendering.

title: Stability Switching and Oscillation Regulation Strategies for Large-Scale Fractional-Order Neural Networks With Double Hubs and Multiple Delays
authors: Jing Chen, Min Xiao, Xiaoqun Wu, Zhengxin Wang, Wenwu Yu, Wei Xing Zheng, Tingwen Huang
abstract: Abnormal neuronal oscillations underlie various brain disorders; however, effective regulation remains challenging due to the inherent complexity of neural network (NN) dynamics. This article proposes a hub-targeted state-feedback control strategy acting exclusively on a single hub node to suppress Hopf bifurcation in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(n{\,}+{\,}m)$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-dimensional dual-hub coupled NNs governed by fractional-order differential equations with multiple time delays. Unlike conventional full-dimensional controllers requiring state measurements from all nodes, the proposed low-dimensional controller significantly reduces implementation complexity and sensing overhead. The characteristic equation of the high-order multidelay system is derived via the Coates flow graph method, and rigorous delay-dependent bifurcation criteria are established based on fractional stability theory and the Hopf bifurcation theorem. Numerical simulations validate the theoretical predictions, demonstrating that an appropriately tuned control gain effectively postpones oscillation onset and enhances robustness against parameter perturbations. Furthermore, the bifurcation threshold is shown to be highly sensitive to fractional-order variations, while larger network scales promote high-frequency oscillations. Notably, interhub connectivity is identified as a critical trigger for periodic oscillations, and hub-node failure is found to enlarge the stability region by degrading the dual-hub topology to a single-hub configuration.

title: Conditional Distributionally Robust Optimization for Look-Ahead Power Dispatch With Heterogeneous Uncertainties
authors: Kaiping Qu, Yue Chen, Heng Liang, Changhong Zhao
abstract: This paper presents a look-ahead environmental economic power dispatch model incorporating heterogeneous uncertainties. A novel distributionally robust dispersion control is integrated in the model for managing air pollutant emissions. This control approach captures the spatial and temporal distribution of emissions, aiming to minimize their contribution to air pollutant concentrations. Furthermore, the dispersion condition uncertainty is modeled on a finite support to enhance control robustness. As a result, the traditional infinite-support renewable energy uncertainty is combined with the proposed finite-support dispersion condition uncertainty in a distributionally robust optimization framework. A joint ambiguity set is thus formulated for the two heterogeneous and correlative uncertainties, which addresses the heterogeneity through conditional probability theory and captures the correlation by conditional expectation constraints. To solve the distributionally robust dispatch model, it is first converted into a tractable form that incorporates robust cost constraints, utilizing dual theory of infinite dimensional linear programming. A conditional column and constraint generation method is then developed to tackle the reformulated problem. Numerical simulations demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed dispersion control in improving air quality, and the computational efficiency of our solution methods.

title: LVCNet: Embedded Label-Vector Co-occurrence for Instance Segmentation of Concrete Bridge Surface Defects
authors: Shixin Jiang, Yilan Huang, Jianxi Yang, Ren Li, Hao Li, Shuangxin Zhao
abstract: Bridge surface defect detection is essential for structural health monitoring and safety assurance, serving as a key step toward bridge industrialization and intelligent development. In complex inspection scenarios, defects often exhibit blurred boundaries, multiscale variations, and multiclass co-occurrence, while existing methods rarely exploit domain priors implied by these relationships. To address this issue, this article proposes LVCNet, a co-occurrence-aware instance segmentation framework for concrete bridge surface defects. LVCNet incorporates label vector co-occurrence priors into feature learning to enhance defect discrimination. Specifically, a Convolutional Gated Linear Units-Former is designed to strengthen local spatial representation, an Adaptive Feature Fusion Module enables dynamic multiscale integration, and a Relation Efficient Channel Attention module injects label co-occurrence knowledge into the channel space. Experiments on the self-constructed bridge defect dataset demonstrate that LVCNet consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving up to 2.5% improvement in mask mAP and 1.8% improvement in box mAP over the YOLOv11 baseline.

title: CVGD: Cross-View Guided Disentangler for Multi-View Radar Semantic Segmentation_supp1-3696605.pdf
authors: Yuan Feng
abstract: Neural networks continue to face challenges in effectively extracting information from the high-dimensional and sparse range-azimuth-Doppler (RAD) tensor. While multi-view architectures using three-axis projections of the RAD tensor improve efficiency and maintain accuracy, existing designs suffer from inefficient inter-view information interaction. To address this issue, this letter proposes a new multi-view information fusion viewpoint: instead of directly blending feature maps, the orthogonal relationships among the RAD coordinate axes are leveraged to guide feature decomposition across views, thereby disentangling the mixed semantics introduced during view compression. Based on this idea, a new module, termed Cross-View Guided Disentangler (CVGD), is introduced to enable inter-view information interaction in multi-view architectures. Extensive experiments on the public CARRADA dataset demonstrate that networks incorporating the proposed module achieve performance comparable to state-of-the-art methods, while utilizing 50% fewer parameters and attaining a 6-fold increase in inference speed. Additionally, promising results are also observed on RADIal, achieving near-SOTA performance with higher efficiency.

title: Conference Committee
authors: N/A

title: Computational Investigation of Magnetophoretic Separation of Blood-Cell-Inspired Particles in Serpentine Microchannels_supp2-3695600.docx
authors: Mondal Pranab Kumar
abstract: We computationally investigate magnetophoretic separation characteristics of blood-cell-inspired particles representing white blood cells (WBCs) and red blood cells (RBCs) in serpentine microchannels under saline-water diluted conditions. The flow field inside the microchannel and the induced magnetic field distribution are numerically simulated using a finite-element-based framework, while particle trajectories are obtained through Newtonian particle tracking. The study systematically examines the influence of magnetic configuration, channel geometry, and flow conditions on magnetic field gradients, particle trajectories, recovery, purity, and separation efficiency. The results demonstrate that serpentine microchannels generate stronger and more non-uniform magnetic field gradients compared to planar configurations, thereby enhancing magnetophoretic manipulation and particle separation. The influence of magnet geometry is also analyzed, revealing that square-shaped magnets provide the most favorable separation characteristics among the investigated configurations due to stronger localized magnetic field gradients. Furthermore, the combined effects of Reynolds number, serpentine channel amplitude, and magnet position are shown to govern the balance between magnetophoretic and hydrodynamic drag forces, leading to distinct operating windows for enhanced separation performance. The present work should be interpreted as a first-order computational design study employing simplified particle surrogates under low-Reynolds-number conditions. The findings provide physical insights and computational design guidelines for the development and optimization of magnetophoretic microfluidic separators for blood-cell-inspired particles in lab-on-chip applications.

title: Characteristics of a Repetitive Pulsed Capillary Discharge Helium Plasma Source and Its Interaction With Tungsten-Based Materials
authors: Shilong Zhang, Li Chen, Jiajing Zhou, Huantong Shi, Xingwen Li, Weihao Li, Shiyu Hao, Chengcheng Li
abstract: The divertor material will be subjected to a high heat flux helium plasma with a repetition rate of 10 Hz. This work investigates a repetitive helium plasma source based on pulsed capillary discharge, where helium is ionized and Joule-heated by high-voltage pulses, then confined by the capillary to form a transient high-density plasma jet (duration: 1 ms, density: ~<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{22}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup>). Plasma parameters were measured using a combined spectroscopic and high-speed imaging diagnostic system. The variation of plasma parameters with capillary peak current and pressure was further investigated to accurately control plasma parameters. Subsequently, the ablation behavior of tungsten under the generated repetitive-frequency heat flux was analyzed. The experimental results demonstrate that the heat flux ranged from 0.13 to 1.37 GW<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−2</sup> under different peak current and pressure conditions. Under the high heat flux with helium, tungsten surfaces exhibited rapid helium-induced modifications, including bubble formation, pore development, and nanostructure evolution within a millisecond timescale. The observed correlation between incident heat flux magnitude and surface degradation rate suggests that thermal loading serves as a critical accelerator for helium-induced material modification in fusion-relevant conditions.

title: Robust Sim2Real Deep Reinforcement Learning for Tracking Control of Continuum Robots
authors: Sihan Zhao, Yunkai Ma, Guanglin Ji, Rui Tao, Junfeng Fan, Fengshui Jing, Gui-Bin Bian, Min Tan
abstract: To achieve effective control of continuum robots (CRs), a robust Sim2Real deep reinforcement learning paradigm is presented in this article. The paradigm consists of a model-informed reinforcement learning (RL) control architecture, a domain-randomized training framework, and a composite reward function. The control architecture adopts a configuration-space action representation informed by kinematics, within which the control policy is learned using RL. Considering the high cost of real-world data collection for training, the domain-randomized training framework is proposed based on model error analysis. This framework enables the controller to learn a robust policy in simulation that can be directly transferred to real robots. Furthermore, the reward function is designed to simultaneously evaluate tracking accuracy and motion safety, promoting convergence of the control policy. Experimental results demonstrate that the controller achieves an average accuracy of 0.21 mm in the target tracking, while the average tracking accuracy for different trajectories ranged from 0.25 to 0.35 mm, validating the effectiveness and robustness of the paradigm.

title: An LTD-Based Unipolar Pulse Generator With Active Resonant Magnetic Reset for Precision Microfluidic Electroporation
authors: Jiaxin Qian, Xiaorun Li, Zhiwei Wang
abstract: Driven by the demand for high-throughput microfluidic electroporation requiring unipolar pulses, LTD-based unipolar pulse generators (UPGs) have emerged as a promising architecture. However, repetitive unipolar excitation leads to residual flux accumulation in the transformer core, which may eventually cause magnetic saturation and waveform degradation. To address this issue, this article proposes an active resonant magnetic reset (ARMR) strategy tailored for LTD-based UPGs. By introducing an auxiliary resonant branch with coordinated switching control, the proposed strategy actively extracts and reutilizes magnetizing energy to generate a controlled reverse current for deterministic magnetic reset. In this way, reliable flux resetting is achieved without dedicated external bias circuits, while reducing reset-related power loss and implementation overhead. A comprehensive theoretical analysis is conducted by dividing the operating cycle into five distinct modes, which provides the basis for parameter design and timing optimization. The proposed strategy is experimentally validated using a two-stage LTD-based UPG prototype with a 100 V input and a 200 V pulse output. Comparative results show that the ARMR strategy effectively suppresses flux accumulation, maintaining a stable leakage-inductance current peak of 56.7 A with an output-voltage droop of approximately 1<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\bm{\%}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. In addition, the reset-related power loss is reduced by up to 30.5<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\bm{\%}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. compared with conventional methods, indicating lower thermal stress during repetitive operation. These results demonstrate that the ARMR strategy provides a favorable tradeoff among reset reliability, loss performance, and implementation compactness, making it a promising solution for LTD-based UPGs in microfluidic electroporation applications.

title: Grand Challenges in Cross Reality_supp1-3695303.pdf
authors: Christoph Anthes

title: Attention-Guided Spatiotemporal Information Fusion of GB-SAR Data for Landslide Displacement Prediction
authors: Xiaodong Gan, Yingchao Dai, Yu Wang, Reza Malekian, Zhou Wu
abstract: Accurate landslide displacement prediction is a key component of IoT-enabled landslide monitoring and warning support frameworks, supporting risk-informed warning analysis and risk-informed decision-making. However, precise forecasting remains challenging due to the non-stationary characteristics of displacement data and the complex spatiotemporal correlations among monitoring points. To this end, this article proposes a novel attention-guided spatiotemporal fusion framework, named VMAG (Variational Mode Decomposition and Multi-head Attention-based GRU), for accurate multi-step landslide displacement prediction. Specifically, a differential fluctuation sequence is first constructed using Ground-Based Synthetic Aperture Radar (GB-SAR) displacement observations to enhance the perceptibility of displacement mutations. Then, based on the inherent characteristics of the time series, the Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) algorithm is applied to decompose the sequence into multi-scale components to mitigate non-stationarity. Subsequently, a multi-head attention mechanism is employed to dynamically extract spatial dependencies between the target node and reference monitoring nodes, which are then fed into a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) network to capture temporal evolutions. Experimental results on datasets from the Moshi Gully (MSG) landslide demonstrate that the proposed VMAG significantly outperforms mainstream benchmarks. For instance, the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) is reduced to 1.6960 mm, and the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) achieves 0.63%, showing superior accuracy compared to ANN, LSTM, and standard GRU models.

title: PCH Theory-Based Energy Reshaping Control for Transient Stability Enhancement of Grid-Connected Grid-Forming Inverters
authors: Liang Yuan, Mingxin Hu, Mei Su, Zhangjie Liu, Yao Sun, Zhao Yang Dong
abstract: Grid-forming (GFM) inverters are pivotal for enhancing the stability and resilience of power systems with high penetration of renewable energy, positioning them as a key technology for future grids. Consequently, the transient stability of GFM inverters in grid-connected systems has garnered significant research interest. Although numerous strategies have been proposed to enhance transient stability, most of them are designed for single-input single-output (SISO) systems. When resistance and reactive power control are taken into account, the GFM system exhibits strong coupling between active and reactive power, thereby becoming a multiinput multioutput (MIMO) system. Consequently, these strategies are difficult to apply effectively to such MIMO system scenarios. Moreover, some strategies rely on real-time grid voltage measurements during faults, which limits their practical applicability. To address these limitations, this article proposes a multiinput transient stability enhancement strategy for GFM inverters based on port-controlled Hamiltonian (PCH) theory, which explicitly incorporates line resistance and RPC dynamics. First, a PCH model of the grid-connected GFM system is established for a resistive-inductive network, integrating the dynamics of RPC. In contrast to conventional Lyapunov energy functions, the proposed Hamiltonian function provides a more accurate estimation of the attraction region, offering a more reliable tool for transient stability assessment for MIMO GFM system. Then, based on energy reshaping method, a control strategy is developed to enhance transient stability by adjusting both active and reactive power references during faults via state-variable feedback. Rigorous stability analysis demonstrates that the closed-loop system is asymptotically stable at the desired equilibrium point, even under severe voltage sags, without grid voltage information. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy and the theoretical analyses are validated through hardware experiments.

title: Multi-Modal Deep Learning for Single-Cell Mapping of Somatic-to-Germline Reprogramming in Infertility
authors: Tanvinur Rahman Siam, Tanjina Pioush Bhuiyan, Mejbah Uddin Bhuiyan, Tahmidur Rahman Sitam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Infertility is a complex disease commonly linked with the disturbed germline development and oocyte maturation process. Here, we adopted a multimodal deep learning platform coupled with single-cell transcriptomic analysis to reveal the transcriptional variation and developmental differentiation trajectory of infertility. Public datasets, including three disease states with more than 25,000 cells and 3,000 expressed features, were harmonized using PCA and Harmony for batch effect correction, followed by UMAP visualization and Leiden clustering, which yielded seven transcriptionally distinct clusters. Diffusion pseudotime analysis inferred a developmental continuum between early somatic-like and late germline-enriched populations, identifying progressions in the absence of defined panels of canonical oocyte and germline markers. Differentially expressed top 10 marker genes per cluster indicated candidate control factors for reproductive failure. Functional enrichment analysis validated the remarkable participation of pathways in meiotic recombination, germline stem cell maintenance, and oocyte maturation. Predictive modeling with Logistic Regression and Random Forest classifiers fitted on integrated embeddings achieved a maximum AUROC of 0.998 and AUPRC of 0.996 using Random Forest and MLP models, confirming strong discriminative performance. Together, our findings show that the combination of deep learning and single-cell multi-omics offers a potent approach to map cellular heterogeneity in infertility, reconstruct developmental dynamics, and discover new biomarkers and therapeutic targets.

title: Predictive Modeling of Women's Mental Health: Financial Confidence as a Humanitarian Determinant in Bangladesh
authors: Asma Binte Afzal, Muhammad Hassan Bin Afzal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Women's ability to secure money for medical care is a central yet understudied factor that shapes mental health in low-resource settings. This study examines how perceived financial confidence relates to anxiety and depression among Bangladeshi women using nationally representative BDHS 2022 data. Multilevel ordinal logistic models show that women who see treatment costs as not a big problem are less likely to report anxiety or depression, even after adjusting for education, wealth, digital use, age, and work status. Community context remains important, with intraclass correlations near twelve percent. The findings support the Capability Approach, the Social Determinants of Health, and Ecological Systems Theory by linking financial access to both personal perception and structural conditions. The results point to the value of lowering cost-related barriers and improving community resources to support women's mental well-being in Bangladesh.

title: Multimodal Sentiment Analysis Using Late Fusion, Late Fusion Transformer, and Multimodal Transformer
authors: Farhana Alam Arin, Rakibul Islam, Sohrab Hossain, Abu Saleh Musa Miah, Md Maruf Al Hossain Prince, Jungpil Shin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis is an analysis of the sentiment of human behavior where multiple data sources are analyzed, such as facial expressions, audio-video recordings, and text data. Multimodal sentiment analysis is an emerging technology that aims to analyze sentiments expressed through multiple data modalities. Using this technology, a field and scenario fabric that can be used for risk management, psychological treatment, public opinion, and product review analysis can be created. Currently, a popular sentiment analysis method is text-based and performs sentiment identification from sentences. Nonetheless, it is untenable to express special expressions, such as irony and exaggeration, through text. Furthermore, by incorporating audio and video modalities, multimodal sentiment analysis incorporates audio and visual data, in addition to text. For more accurate results, the fusion analysis approach is an effective sentiment analysis technique. Maintaining cross-modal sentiment data is a primary challenge in multimodal sentiment analysis. As a result, we concentrate on the fusion process, the methods employed, and different approaches and techniques. In addition, we will review the history of sentiment analysis, popular datasets, feature extraction techniques, intriguing applications for this approach, and challenging problems that may arise. It is expected that the outcome of our work is the realization by researchers that multimodal sentiment analysis evolves more every day.

title: HCT-Net: A Hybrid Convolutional-Transformer Network with Generative Balancing for Skin Cancer Classification
authors: Badrun Nahar Luna, Nazifa Jerin Reshni, Adib Mahmud, Md. Fazle Rabbi Emon, Md Saifur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: We have a proposal of a Hybrid Convolutional Transformer Network that incorporates generative balancing to categorize dermoscopic pictures belonging to the ISIC-2019 dataset. The framework has solved significant issues, such as excessive class imbalance and the necessity to collectively model the local texture indicators alongside global structure. A conditional GAN is used to augment minority-class samples and EfficientNet-B3 and DeiT-S extract local and global features respectively. The fused representation is categorized with a fully connected head that is obtained by using focal loss and optimised with AdamW. The experimental findings demonstrate that the proposed model is better when compared to the previous baseline networks, including VGG16, ResNet50, EfficientNet-B3, and DeiT-S, as it provides a higher accuracy of 96.2 % in addition to higher balanced accuracy, precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> scores, especially in case of rare lesion types. These results indicate that when generative balancing is used with a hybrid CNN-Transformer backbone, a powerful and the state-of-the-art solution to automated skin cancer classification is attained.

title: Smart Waste Management Using IoT and Artificial Intelligence with QR Code Reward System
authors: Md Abu Said, Md. Abul Basar, Md. Abir Habib, Rezwanul Haque, Md. Torikur Rahman, Sadia Sazzad, Mohammad Amanul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Rapid urbanization has intensified waste generation, creating pressing challenges for sustainable management. This paper presents a unified Smart Waste Management System that combines Internet of Things (IoT) monitoring, Artificial Intelligence (AI)–based classification, and a QR code–driven reward mechanism to encourage citizen participation. The methodology involves system architecture design, sensor hardware integration, convolutional neural network training for waste classification, cloud-based database development, and real-time prototype testing. Ultrasonic sensors detect bin capacity in real time, preventing overflow through automated lid locking. A convolutional neural network classifies waste into recyclable, organic, hazardous, and e-waste categories, while hazardous items such as batteries and syringes are automatically redirected to specialized bins for safe handling. A QR-based interface links disposal events to a reward system, granting redeemable points for responsible behavior. A prototype implementation achieved over 94% accuracy across multiple waste categories, with reliable real-time operation and consistent reward tracking. Unlike prior works that address IoT monitoring, AI classification, or reward systems separately, this study introduces an integrated framework that unites automation, intelligence, and behavioral incentives. The results demonstrate the system's potential to reduce landfill pressure, improve recycling efficiency, and foster active community engagement in sustainable urban living.

title: Adversarial Defense Strategies for 6G PHY: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Tarek Ali, Mohammed Al-Khalidi, Ali Kashif Bashir, Amna Eleyan, Tarek Bejaoui, Rabab Al-Zaidi
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Sixth-generation (6 G) networks will increasingly rely on artificial intelligence (AI)/machine learning (ML) at the physical layer (PHY) to enable ultra-low latency, massive connectivity, and intelligent radio operation, but this shift also exposes new attack surfaces to adversarial manipulation. This survey synthesizes 30 recent studies (2021-2025) on adversarial threats and defenses in AI-enabled <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 G ~ P H Y}$</tex>, covering gradientbased attacks (e.g., Fast Gradient Sign Method (FGSM) and Projected Gradient Descent (PGD)) alongside spoofing, jamming, and man-in-the-middle (MitM) adversaries targeting millimeterwave (mmWave) beam prediction, channel estimation, reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS), semantic communications, and Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) intelligence. We organize defenses into adversarial training (up to 57.90 % robustness improvement), privacy-preserving federated learning (FL) (up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 7. 4 5 \%}$</tex> defense gain), defensive distillation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 3 \%}$</tex> accuracy under attack), cryptographic/authentication mechanisms (up to 100 % verification accuracy), and reinforcement learning (RL)based adaptive strategies. While these approaches are effective in specific settings, open challenges remain in achieving crosstechnology generalization, mitigating heterogeneous concurrent attacks, and meeting strict real-time and resource constraints. The survey concludes with research directions toward unified, efficient, and deployable PHY defense frameworks for trustworthy 6 G.

title: Evaluating Multi-Agent Path Finding Algorithms for Modular Robot Reconfiguration
authors: Omar Khalil, Jad Bassil, Perla Tannoury, Benoît Piranda, Abdallah Makhoul
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Modular self-reconfigurable robots are composed of many connected modules that must move in coordination to transform from one shape to another. Effective reconfiguration requires Multi-Agent Path Finding (MAPF) methods that manage both spatial and temporal conflicts; in this paper, we compare three such coordination strategies: Cooperative A* (CA*), Conflict-Based Search (CBS), and a flow-based pairing and streamline construction method. Our results show that CA* provides fast and lightweight planning suitable for sequential or limited-parallel motion. CBS produces lower-cost solutions and stronger conflict handling but requires more computation as the number of conflicts increases. The MaxFlow approach offers an efficient way to assign targets and organize movement, reducing ambiguity and improving coordination during structured reconfiguration stages.

title: Task-Aware Semantic Communications for 6G Under Finite Blocklength: Joint Reliability, Blocklength, Power and Compute Optimisation
authors: Iacovos Ioannou, Ala Khalifeh, Prabagarane Nagaradjane, Vitawat Sittakul, Vasos Vassiliou
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: A task-aware semantic communication framework for 6 G short packet transmission is presented in this paper. The limitations of single-variable power optimisation are addressed through a joint design in which reliability, blocklength, power and compute level are optimised together. A two-stream source model is considered so that critical and auxiliary semantic content are protected differently under finite-blocklength constraints. A task distortion model is adopted in which the semantic distortion is reduced both by the transmitted semantic bits and by the available computing resources. The wireless transmission energy and computation energy are then minimised under a target semantic distortion requirement. Both additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN) and Rayleigh fading settings are examined. The reported results show that the proposed joint method achieves the lowest energy among the comparable two-stream schemes over the whole tested distortion range from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$D_{0}=0.12$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$D_{0}=0.35$</tex>. At the representative operating point <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$D_{0}=0.2186$</tex>, the proposed method requires a total energy of 106.47, the equal protection baseline requires 138.79 and the fixed blocklength baseline requires 120.88. These values correspond to energy reductions of 23.3 % and 11.9 %, respectively. It is also shown that the best compute level is usually obtained at a moderate value rather than at the smallest or the largest value. Under Rayleigh fading with outage constraint <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\delta=10^{-2}$</tex>, the required energy is more than 80 times higher than in the AWGN case. These results demonstrate that semantic short packet transmission should be designed using a joint cross-layer optimisation framework rather than power control alone.

title: Exploring Vision-Language Models for Weakly Supervised Video Anomaly Detection: Experiments and Analysis
authors: Olfa Saket, Anis Ben Aicha, Habib Fathallah
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Video anomaly detection (VAD) is an automated task that identifies deviations from normal behavior in video streams. Since 2023, several CLIP-based VAD methods have been proposed. These methods can be categorized into monomodal approaches, employing only visual features from CLIP, and bi-modal approaches, leveraging both visual and textual representations. However, most of these approaches have been evaluated under specific experimental settings, making it challenging to select the appropriate model for a particular application. Therefore, a comparative study that evaluates CLIP-based VAD methods under unified experimental settings is needed. This paper presents a comparative study of three CLIP-based approaches for VAD: CLIP-TSA, VadCLIP, and AnomalyCLIP. We specifically compare their performance on two publicly available datasets, XD-Violence and UCF-Crime, using the same evaluation metrics. The experimental results show that the bimodal approaches VadCLIP and AnomalyCLIP achieve similar performance, outperforming the mono-modal CLIP-TSA. Additionally, all evaluated methods perform better on XD-Violence than on UCF-Crime.

title: A Novel Probabilistic Relay Selection Protocol for Buffer-Aided Cooperative Networks
authors: Chadi Abou-Rjeily, Naim Aoun
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: This paper investigates buffer-aided (BA) relaying in decode-and-forward (DF) cooperative networks with an arbitrary number of relays. A novel probabilistic relay selection protocol is proposed incorporating randomness into the selection of the source-relay (S-R) or relay-destination (R-D) link to be activated. The relaying strategy assigns a probability distribution to all S-R and R-D links that are not in outage while capturing the level of emptiness of the buffers for the S-R links and the level of fullness of these buffers for the R-D links. This design balances the buffer loads across relays, thereby mitigating buffer congestion and starvation and enhancing the availability of the S-R and R-D links, respectively. The performance of the proposed scheme is analyzed using a Markov chain framework to evaluate the outage probability and the average packet delay. Simulations demonstrate improved performance levels compared to the benchmark BA relaying strategies.

title: A Controller-Centric ML-Driven Security & Knowledge Plane for SDN–NFV Architectures
authors: Zakiya Alfughi, Abdelkader Ouda
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: This paper proposes a modernized, ML-driven security architecture for multi-layered programmable networks that include software-defined networking (SDN) and network function virtualization (NFV). Building on a systematic analysis of crosslayer threats-such as man-in-the-middle attacks on control channels, hypervisor compromise in NFV, and inherited vulnerabilities in legacy protocols-the paper argues that perimeter, rule- and signature-based intrusion detection is fundamentally insufficient for these environments. To address this gap, it introduces an integrated security/knowledge plane anchored at the SDN/NFV control layer, which aggregates telemetry across SDN controllers, and NFV orchestrators into a unified event and graph representation. On top of this substrate, a controller-centric deep learning and ensemble-based IDS is designed to perform multi-stage detection, while graph neural networks operate on a dynamic, attributed network graph to expose structural and multi-tenant attacks that traverse both virtual and physical boundaries. A constrained deep reinforcement learning agent is further specified to close the loop by selecting context-aware mitigation actions, such as adaptive rerouting, VNF isolation or migration, and policy tightening-under explicit service-level and safety constraints. The contribution is an architectural framework that integrates these components into a controllerembedded security/knowledge plane; quantitative implementation and evaluation on realistic SDN/NFV testbeds are identified as future work.

title: From Prediction to Insight: Visual Analytics for Understanding Compound Potency Models
authors: Bahavathy Kathirgamanathan, Tiago Janela, Elena Xerxa, Gennady Andrienko, Jürgen Bajorath, Natalia Andrienko
abstract: Machine learning (ML) is widely used in medicinal chemistry, but accurate predictions alone are insufficient. Researchers need insight into which molecular features determine compound properties. We present an application-oriented case study that analyzes a trained model for compound potency as a source of domain knowledge. The model is converted into decision rules, and topic-guided visual analytics is used to identify co-occurring feature conditions associated with high predicted potency. These patterns are then mapped back to molecular substructures, yielding chemically interpretable motifs and testable hypotheses about structure–activity relationships. The study demonstrates how combining rule-based representations, topic modeling, and visual exploration can turn potency predictions into mechanistic insight, and outlines a reusable workflow for interpreting ML models of molecular properties.

title: From Blurry to Believable: Enhancing Low-Quality Talking Heads with 3D Generative Priors
authors: Ding-Jiun Huang, Yuanhao Wang, Shao-Ji Yuan, Albert Mosella-Montoro, Francisco Vicente Carrasco, Cheng Zhang, Fernando De la Torre
abstract: Creating high-fidelity, animatable 3D talking heads is crucial for immersive applications, yet often hindered by the prevalence of low-quality image or video sources, which yield poor 3D reconstructions. In this paper, we introduce SuperHead, a novel framework for enhancing lowresolution, animatable 3D head avatars. The core challenge lies in synthesizing high-quality geometry and textures, while ensuring both 3D and temporal consistency during animation and preserving subject identity. Despite recent progress in image, video and 3D-based superresolution (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S R$</tex>), existing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S R$</tex> techniques are ill-equipped to handle dynamic 3D inputs. To address this, SuperHead leverages the rich priors from pre-trained 3D generative models via a novel dynamics-aware 3D inversion scheme. This process optimizes the latent representation of the generative model to produce a super-resolved 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) head model, which is subsequently rigged to an underlying parametric head model (e.g., FLAME) for animation. The inversion is jointly supervised using a sparse collection of upscaled 2D face renderings and corresponding depth maps, captured from diverse facial expressions and camera viewpoints, to ensure realism under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d y$</tex> namic facial motions. Experiments demonstrate that SuperHead generates avatars with fine-grained facial details under dynamic motions, significantly outperforming baseline methods in visual quality.

title: RaCo: Ranking and Covariance for Practical Learned Keypoints
authors: Abhiram Shenoi, Philipp Lindenberger, Paul-Edouard Sarlin, Marc Pollefeys
abstract: This paper introduces RaCo, a lightweight neural network designed to learn robust and versatile keypoints suitable for a variety of 3D computer vision tasks. The model integrates three key components: the repeatable keypoint detector, a differentiable ranker to maximize matches with a limited number of keypoints, and a covariance estimator to quantify spatial uncertainty in metric scale. Trained on perspective image crops only, RaCo operates without the need for covisible image pairs. It achieves strong rotational robustness through extensive data augmentation, even without the use of computationally expensive equivariant network architectures. The method is evaluated on several challenging datasets, where it demonstrates state-of-the-art performance in keypoint repeatability and two-view matching, particularly under large in-plane rotations. Ultimately, RaCo provides an effective and simple strategy to independently estimate keypoint ranking and metric covariance without additional labels, detecting interpretable and repeatable interest points. The code is available at: https://github.com/cvg/RaCo

title: 1000FPS+ Novel View Synthesis from End-To-End Opaque Triangle Optimization
authors: Zhiwen Yan, Weng Fei Low, Tianxin Huang, Gim Hee Lee
abstract: Recent implicit and primitive-based radiance field methods, such as NeRF and 3DGS, have demonstrated impressive capabilities in novel view synthesis from multi-view images. However, their custom representations are often incompatible with conventional graphics pipelines, limiting their application in areas like editing, relighting, physics simulation, and particle effects. Additionally, their volume rendering approach requires alpha-blending multiple colors per pixel, which slows down rendering. Traditional differentiable rendering methods, while offering higher compatibility and speed, rely on known mesh topology, making them unsuitable for complex scene-level reconstruction. To address these limitations, we introduce a novel end-toend optimization process of disjoint opaque triangles, natively compatible with standard graphics engines for a wide range of applications. To enable gradient-based optimization over the highly non-differentiable rasterization process, we employ a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 D$</tex> SDF approximation and a two-layer occlusion approximation. We also incorporate density controls to ensure detailed and complete scene reconstructions. Our paper tackles the challenging end-to-end optimization of scene-level novel view synthesis with opaque representation only. Our approach achieves over 1000 FPS rendering on a single desktop GPU, providing high compatibility and similar novel view synthesis quality to existing methods.

title: A Framework for Reducing the Complexity of Geometric Vision Problems and its Application to Two-View Triangulation with Approximation Bounds
authors: Felix Rydell, Georg Bökman
abstract: In this paper, we present a new framework for reducing the computational complexity of geometric vision problems through targeted reweighting of the cost functions used to minimize reprojection errors. Triangulation - the task of estimating a 3D point from noisy 2D projections across multiple images - is a fundamental problem in multiview geometry and Structure-from-Motion (SfM) pipelines. We apply our framework to the two-view case and demonstrate that optimal triangulation, which requires solving a univariate polynomial of degree six, can be simplified through cost function reweighting reducing the polynomial degree to two. This reweighting yields a closed-form solution while preserving strong geometric accuracy. We derive optimal weighting strategies, establish theoretical bounds on the approximation error, and provide experimental results on real data demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed approach compared to standard methods. Although this work focuses on two-view triangulation, the framework generalizes to other geometric vision problems.

title: BodyContact4D: A Multi-view Video Dataset for Understanding Human and Environment Interactions
authors: Soyong Shin, Chaeeun Lee, Holly Chen, Jyun-Ting Song, Eni Halilaj, Kris Kitani
abstract: To improve vision-based methods for understanding how people interact with their physical environment, we introduce a multi-view video and body-contact sensing dataset designed to capture dynamic human activities that involve interactions with the physical environment. The dataset includes activities such as parkour, physical training, and gym exercises, characterized by frequent body-environment contact. The proposed dataset includes 780 K images across 120 K pose sequences from 7 subjects. Each subject is captured by 6 synchronized third-person cameras, a single egocentric camera, and multiple contact sensors worn on the body. Using our proposed dataset, we benchmark state-of-the-art vision-based body contact models and show significant limitations in existing methods. Furthermore, we benchmark existing human pose estimation methods on our dataset and show that they fail under significant occlusion caused by close interactions with the environment, which indicates that our dataset can also be used to further develop pose estimation models to be more robust during interaction with the environment. To facilitate better human pose estimation from video, we introduce and evaluate a video-based human contact detection model that outperforms existing imagebased methods, underscoring the potential improvements from integrating contact information into pose estimation models. See the project page at: https://yohanshin.github.io/bodycontact4d.github.io/

title: Unlocking the Video Prior for High-Fidelity Sparse Multi-View Image Synthesis
authors: Fan Yang, Jianfeng Zhang, Jun Hao Liew, Chaoyue Song, Zhongcong Xu, Jiashi Feng, Guosheng Lin
abstract: The development of multi-view image synthesis is constrained by the scarcity of training data. One promising solution is to finetune well-trained video generative models to synthesize 360-degree videos of objects. While these methods benefit from the strong generative priors inherited from the pretrained knowledge, they are limited by the high computational costs incurred by the large number of viewpoints. Existing methods commonly adopt temporal attention mechanism to address this. However, these methods suffer from undesirable artifacts such as 3D inconsistency and over-smoothing in the generated results. In this paper, we introduce a novel approach to unlock the video priors for multi-view synthesis by reducing generation into a sparser yet more precise process. Specifically, we introduce two strategies to achieve this: i) Condensing the video diffusion model to synthesize highly consistent sparse multiview images. ii) Extracting dense geometrical priors from the pretrained video diffusion models to enhance the generation stability. The combination of these two strategies formulates a novel framework for multi-view synthesis, which is capable of synthesizing highly consistent sparse multiview images with strong generalization ability. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach achieves superior efficiency, generalization, and consistency, outperforming state-of-the-art multi-view synthesis methods.

title: Video Depth Propagation
authors: Luigi Piccinelli, Thiemo Wandel, Christos Sakaridis, Wim Abbeloos, Luc Van Gool
abstract: Depth estimation in videos is essential for visual perception in real-world applications. However, existing methods either rely on simple frame-by-frame monocular models, leading to temporal inconsistencies and inaccuracies, or use computationally demanding temporal modeling, unsuitable for real-time applications. These limitations significantly restrict general applicability and performance in practical settings. To address this, we propose VeloDepth, an efficient and robust online video depth estimation pipeline that effectively leverages spatiotemporal priors from previous depth predictions and performs deep feature propagation. Our method introduces a novel Propagation Module that refines and propagates depth features and predictions using flow-based warping coupled with learned residual corrections. In addition, our design structurally enforces temporal consistency, resulting in stable depth predictions across consecutive frames with improved efficiency. Comprehensive zero-shot evaluation on multiple benchmarks demonstrates the state-of-the-art temporal consistency and competitive accuracy of VeloDepth, alongside its significantly faster inference compared to existing video-based depth estimators. VeloDepth thus provides a practical, efficient, and accurate solution for real-time depth estimation suitable for diverse perception tasks. Code and models are available at github.com/lpiccinelli-eth/velodepth.

title: DDAD: Diffusion in Dimensionless Domain for Generalized Hyperspectral Anomaly Detection
authors: Xiangrong Zhang, Jieqiong Du, Guanchun Wang, Shiqi Wu, Chaowei Fang, Xu Tang, Licheng Jiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral anomaly detection (HAD) aims to distinguish background and anomalous targets in hyperspectral images (HSIs). In real-world applications, HSIs are often acquired under varying conditions, such as different sensors, times, and locations. This makes generalized HAD particularly important for practical deployment. Most existing methods follow an unsupervised reconstruction-based framework (e.g., autoencoders, GANs) that detects anomalies by measuring reconstruction errors. However, two spectral barriers remain: Spectral Divergence and Spectral Confusion, that hinder the realization of generalized HAD: 1) cross-scene HSIs exhibit diverse spectral characteristics. The spectral divergence in the original spectral space leads to degraded generalized detection performance; 2) most methods rely on pixel-level detection in the original space, which is prone to mixed-pixel effects that hinder accurate background reconstruction. To break these spectral barriers, we propose a novel Dimensionless Diffusion framework for generalized HAD (DDAD). First, we introduce a dimensionless domain transformation module that maps HSIs from different sources into a unified, spectral-invariant dimensionless domain, which effectively eliminates spectral divergence and confusion across scenes. Next, we propose a controllable dimensionless diffusion reconstruction module that achieves balanced background reconstruction and anomaly suppression through controllable explicit background modeling. Additionally, to further enhance generalization, we design a dynamic spatial-spectral perturbation mechanism to mitigate the model’s reliance on single-source characteristics. Extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets demonstrate the superior cross-scene generalized performance of DDAD.

title: Short-Window Gamma-Gamma Parameter Estimation With SW-GGNet for Satellite-to-Ground Laser Communication
authors: Tongfei Chen, Zhiyi Hou, Zhi Liu, Yanyan Li, Changhao Zhou
abstract: Satellite-to-ground laser communication requires short-window channel estimation because atmospheric turbulence can change over short time scales and render long-window estimates unreliable for adaptive transmission. This letter proposes a Short-Window Gamma-Gamma Network (SW-GGNet), a lightweight one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN), to estimate the Gamma-Gamma fading parameters (α, β) from short irradiance sequences. SW-GGNet uses an auxiliary scintillation-index head for physics-informed regularization.With 512 samples, it reduces normalized mean squared error (NMSE) by 43%, 42%, 30%, and 48% relative to maximum likelihood estimation (MLE), the generalized method of moments (GMM), long short-term memory (LSTM), and a histogram-based CNN, respectively, and achieves a 70% reduction relative to MLE at SNR = 5 dB.

title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning for Fraud Detection in Distributed Banking Networks
authors: Ifeanyi Bryan Uzoatu, Olamide Jogunola, Ahmed Danladi Abdullahi, Bamidele Adebisi, Tooska Dargahi
abstract: Credit card fraud poses a critical threat to global financial systems, with annual losses exceeding $485 billion, necessitating robust detection mechanisms that balance predictive accuracy with stringent data privacy requirements. Existing approaches face fundamental limitations: Centralised machine learning models compromise regulatory compliance due to sharing the training data and model with third parties, while standard federated learning (FL) remains vulnerable to gradient-based privacy attacks. This paper presents a novel privacy-preserving framework integrating Conditional Tabular Generative Adversarial Networks (CTGAN) with Differentially Private FL. Unlike existing federated GAN approaches that suffer from training instability, our framework maintains local CTGAN synthesis whilst federating only the downstream classifier, achieving superior synthetic data fidelity. The framework employs local synthetic data generation with formal differential privacy (DP) guarantees at both the generation and the federated training stages. Extensive evaluation on two benchmark fraud datasets partitioned across three simulated banking institutions demonstrates an F1-score of 0.9485 ± 0.0014 across 10 independent runs, retaining 95.2% of the centralised-real baseline whilst providing formal (ε, δ)-DP at privacy budget (ε = 3.0) and failure probability (δ = 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">–5</sup>). Privacy evaluation against five state-of-the-art defences, confirms that membership-inference accuracy remains at chance level (0.48–0.51) across all tested configurations, and that model-inversion reconstruction error increases under DP. This work advances privacy-preserving financial analytics by providing a rigorously validated, regulatory-compliant framework enabling collaborative fraud detection across distributed banking networks without compromising institutional data sovereignty.

title: Physics-Guided Surface Current Reconstruction of 3D PEC Targets from a Single Image via Cascaded Neural Networks
authors: Xiangwei Liu, Te Shi, Kuisong Zheng, Haixuan Zhang, Chaoqun Fan, Jianzhou Li, Gao Wei, Changying Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Accurate reconstruction of electromagnetic surface currents is essential for radar signature prediction, scattering-mechanism interpretation, and electromagnetic scene understanding. However, traditional computational electromagnetic methods require complete geometric models and fine surface meshing, resulting in high computational costs and limiting applicability in vision-based scenarios where only single-view images are available. This paper proposes a physics-guided cascaded neural network to infer the surface-current distribution of a target directly from a single optical image.
