Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed STIGNN model outperforms existing methods across multiple evaluation metrics. Furthermore, it enables an in-depth analysis of individual target contributions to the overall threat situation, providing interpretable and targeted decision support.

title: Current Adaptive Terminal Sliding Mode Control and Neutral Point Clamping Control Strategy for Two-Three-Level DAB Converters
authors: Sen Yang, Xiangyang Xing, Yang Liu, Rui Zhang, Wenlong Ding, Yan Li, Xi Wen
abstract: Two-three-level (2/3L) dual active bridge (DAB) converters with neutral point clamping (NPC) structures are applicable to low- and medium-voltage DC conversion systems. Maintaining stable output voltage and current of the 2/3L DAB converter is a primary challenge. Under uncertain conditions and disturbances, the response speed and robustness of the DAB converter’s output are significantly reduced. Traditional sliding mode control suffers from issues such as chattering and parameter design challenges. Additionally, capacitor-voltage imbalance on the output side increases voltage stress, thereby compromising converter stability. While the fixed-switching-state (FSS) strategy effectively balances output voltages, it offers only a single balancing mode and exhibits limitations under varying voltage error conditions. This article proposes a novel high-performance control strategy for a 2/3L NPC DAB converter under transient conditions. The strategy incorporates two control loops, i.e., a current tracking loop and a voltage balancing loop. The current tracking loop employs a frequency-domain-based adaptive terminal sliding mode controller for output-side current, effectively addressing output uncertainties and disturbances while ensuring dynamic response speed and steady-state performance. For the voltage-balancing loop, this article proposes an extended-fixed-switching-state (EFSS) method. By selecting different modes under varying voltage error conditions, it optimizes voltage balancing, effectively reducing voltage error ripple and enhancing balancing performance. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed scheme is verified experimentally.

title: Prescribed-Time Control of Disturbed Unicycle Mobile Robots Under Output Constraints
authors: Jinyu Ni, Chengyi He, Xiucai Huang, Kai Zhao, Yongduan Song
abstract: In this article, we study the prescribed-time regulation problem for unicycle mobile robots subject to unknown external disturbances, with bounded one- and two-sided time-varying output constraints being considered. We initially transform the under-actuated robot model into a square system relative to the hand position, and again convert the latter into an integrator with unknown disturbances via control gain matrix feedback. For such an integrator, we design a nominal controller in the absence of constraints to regulate the output to its corresponding constraint boundary at an arbitrarily prescribed time, which is able to guarantee the boundedness of all closed-loop signals. Then, by resorting to a family of time-explicit barrier functions (BFs) and some sufficient barrier conditions, we redesign a continuous constrained controller to override the nominal one whenever the constraint violation is imminent, which solves the prescribed-time regulation problem under constraints and maintains the uniform boundedness of all closed-loop signals. Finally, numerical simulations and practical experiments are conducted to validate the efficacy of our methodology.

title: Directly Encrypting DNA Sequences for Secure DNA Storage via Automata Cryptography_supp1-3695368.pdf
authors: Ben Cao
abstract: DNA data storage is an emerging data storage method with an extremely long storage duration and high density, yet it currently lacks adequate consideration for data security. Existing encryption methods for DNA storage are based on bit-level encryption, which makes them incompatible with DNA encoding methods based on data characteristics. In this study, we propose a method for directly encrypting DNA sequences via automata cryptography for secure DNA storage (DNA-AC) to solve the above challenges. In DNA-AC, sequence-level encryption is achieved through diffusion and rotation of bases, making the base distribution more uniform and random, improving the security of DNA storage data. Security analysis demonstrates that DNA-AC exhibits a high key space, an information entropy close to 2, a Number of Bases Change Rate (NBCR) of 75% and Bases Average Changing Intensity (BACI) of 42%, showing strong resistance to malicious attacks. Compared to representative works, DNA-AC supports parallel processing to accommodate high-throughput parallel DNA synthesis and sequencing. In general, DNA-AC offers a secure, high-performance, and scalable solution for DNA storage encryption, with the potential to be an ideal data encryption approach for DNA storage devices.

title: SDPT: Synchronous Dual Prompt Tuning for Visual-Language Pre-trained Models_supp1-3692711.pdf
authors: Yan Xu
abstract: Prompt tuning methods use learnable tokens for parameter-efficient downstream adaptation on large pre-trained models. However, for dual-modal visual-language pre-trained models (VLPMs), existing prompt tuning methods overlook the preservation of pre-trained text-image alignment during fine-tuning. To address this issue, we propose Synchronous Dual Prompt Tuning (SDPT). SDPT initializes a single set of learnable unified prototype tokens in the established modal aligning space to represent the aligned semantics of text and image modalities for downstream tasks. Furthermore, SDPT establishes inverse linear projections, whose projection matrices need no training, to embed the information of learnable unified prototype tokens into the input space of different modalities. The inverse linear projections allow the unified prototype token to synchronously represent the two modalities and enable SDPT to share the unified semantics of text and image for downstream tasks across different modal prompts. Experimental results demonstrate that SDPT assists VLPMs to achieve superior outcomes with only 0.04% of model parameters for training across various scenarios, outperforming other single- or dual-modal methods. The code is released at github/SDPT.

title: Flow-Opt: Scalable Centralized Multi-Robot Trajectory Optimization with Flow Matching and Differentiable Optimization
authors: Simon Idoko, Prajyot Jadhav, Arun Kumar Singh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Centralized trajectory optimization in the joint space of multiple robots allows access to a larger feasible space that can result in smoother trajectories, especially while planning in tight spaces. Unfortunately, it is often computationally intractable beyond a very small swarm size. In this paper, we propose Flow-Opt, a fast learning-based approach for providing high-quality approximations of centralized multi-robot trajectory optimization. Specifically, we reduce the problem to first learning a generative model to sample different candidate trajectories and then using a learned Safety-Filter(SF) to ensure fast inference-time constraint satisfaction. We propose a flow-matching model based on a diffusion transformer (DiT) augmented with state and map encoders, as the generative model. We develop a custom solver for our SF and equip it with a neural network that predicts context-specific initialization. The initialization network is trained in a self-supervised manner, taking advantage of the differentiability of the SF solver. We advance the state-of-the-art in the following respects. First, we show that we can generate trajectories for tens of robots in cluttered environments in a few tens of milliseconds. This is several times faster than existing centralized optimization approaches. Moreover, our approach generates smoother trajectories orders of magnitude faster than competing baselines based on diffusion models. Second, each component of our approach can be batched, allowing us to solve a few tens of problem instances in a fraction of a second. We believe this is the first such result; no existing approach provides such capabilities. Finally, our approach can generate a diverse set of trajectories between a given set of start and goal locations, which can capture different collision-avoidance behaviors.

title: Preface: Committees ICMRE 2026
authors: N/A

title: The Influence of Series Chip Number on the Performance of High-Voltage Deep-Ultraviolet LED With a Long Stripe Array
authors: Yuling Wu, Linhao Wang, Chong Wang, Linbo Hao, Kai Guo, Xiaona Zhang, Chunshuang Chu, Kangkai Tian, Haoyan Liu, Naixin Liu, Yonghui Zhang, Zi-Hui Zhang, Jianchang Yan
abstract: In this work, high-voltage (HV) deep-ultraviolet (DUV) light-emitting diodes (LEDs) with a long-stripe array are designed and fabricated with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10\times 18$</tex-math> </inline-formula>mil<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> commercial-grade chips as basic units. Unlike conventional designs that fix the total active area in a square arrangement, this work adopts a fixed single-chip-area strategy and a long-stripe array to enhance heat dissipation. As the number of series-connected chips increases, the light output power (LOP) improves while showing a clear saturation trend beyond three chips. At an input power of 0.5 W, the four-chip device achieves an external quantum efficiency (EQE) that is 19.6% higher than that of the single-chip device. However, increasing the chip number from three to four yields a marginal gain of only 1.8%, indicating rapid saturation. This saturation originates from two competing mechanisms. The light extraction efficiency (LEE) continuously decreases due to the elongated light propagation path. Meanwhile, the self-heating effect intensifies nonlinearly with the chip number, as evidenced by the increasing peak wavelength redshift from 0 to 1.3 nm from LED I to LED IV at 80 mA. Beyond four chips, the negative impact of self-heating outweighs the benefit of adding more chips. Furthermore, it has been found that the ideality factor decreases more rapidly for HV DUV LEDs with more series chips due to the more rapid reduction in series resistance caused by the increased chip temperature. This study provides a quantitative design basis for optimizing the number of series chips in high-power HV DUV LEDs and reveals the competing roles of optical loss and thermal effects in determining the performance saturation limit.

title: Green Synthesis of Multiphase Gd/Gd&#x2082;O&#x2083; Nanoparticles Using Hazelnut Husk Extract for Biomedical Applications CR-16_supp1-3696538.pdf
authors: Ozan Akdogan
abstract: Magnetic nanoparticles (MNPs) have attracted considerable attention in recent years due to their potential applications in magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), targeted drug delivery, and cancer treatment. In particular, gadolinium-based nanoparticles are widely investigated as MRI contrast agents due to their high magnetic features and paramagnetic characteristics. In this work, Gd/Gd₂O₃ nanoparticles were synthesized by an environmentally friendly green synthesis approach, in which hazelnut husk extract was used as a natural reducing and stabilizing agent. During the synthesis, the hazelnut (Corylus colurna) husk, the outer leaf of the hazelnut shell, and a bio-waste extract were used as a reducing and stabilizing agent. The formation of a dual-phase crystalline structure consisting of Gd and cubic Gd₂O₃ with an average crystal size of approximately 21 nm was confirmed. The coexistence of metallic Gd and Gd₂O₃ phases effectively improves the magnetic response of NPs. FTIR spectra showed functional groups obtained from plant-derived biomolecules that play a role in NPs stabilization, and the formation of Gd₂O₃ was supported by Gd–O vibration bands. TGA and VSM results, respectively, showed the presence of organic residues from the green synthesis process and weak ferromagnetic behavior with low coercivity, supporting the soft magnetic properties of the synthesized Gd/Gd₂O₃ NPs. The cytotoxicity of Polyethylene glycol (PEG)-coated Gd/Gd₂O₃ NPs were evaluated on SH-SY5Y human neuroblastoma cells using MTT assay, and a concentration-dependent decrease in cell viability between 10–100 μg/mL was observed, revealing moderate cytotoxic effects at higher concentrations. These results indicate that the synthesized NPs exhibit promising potential for biomedical applications, including magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), targeted drug delivery, and other nanomedicine-related applications.

title: Learning From Both Eyes: A Bilateral Ensemble Approach for Retinal Disease Classification
authors: Sadia Alam Kotha, Abdullah Al Alam Shanto, Mumtahina Esha, Siam Tahsin Bhuiyan, Halima Khatun, Rashedur Rahman, Ashraful Islam, Saadia Binte Alam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Automated detection of retinal diseases from retinal color fundus images is crucial for early diagnosis and effective intervention. While deep learning approaches have achieved high accuracy, most existing methods analyze each eye independently and inconsistently apply preprocessing, limiting their ability to capture inter-eye asymmetries and subtle pathological features. In this study, we propose a bilateral ensemble framework for multi-class retinal disease classification using ConvNeXt-XLarge as the backbone. Fundus images were preprocessed with color normalization, a color correction technique to enhance feature consistency. Experiments were conducted on the ODIR-5K dataset, focusing on five clinically relevant categories: Normal, Diabetic Retinopathy, Cataract, Age-Related Macular Degeneration, and Myopia. We evaluated both single-eye and bilateral ensemble settings, with and without color correction, using Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1 Score, Specificity. The bilateral ensemble with color-corrected images achieved the highest overall accuracy of 74.5 %, demonstrating that bilateral feature fusion and systematic preprocessing improve the model's ability to capture inter-eye asymmetries and subtle retinal patterns. These results underscore the potential of ensemble-based approaches to provide more reliable and clinically meaningful automated retinal disease detection.

title: Performance Optimization of Lead-Free Double Absorber Perovskite Solar Cells through ETL and HTL Engineering: A SCAPS-1D Study
authors: Md. Mahmudul Hasan Tonmoy, Mohammad Farhan Tanvir, Mst. Arifa Khatun, Md. Humayun Kabir, Md Abir Hasan Sizan, Jinia Shaila Jahan, Zahid Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: The growing demand for sustainable energy urges for advanced solar technologies. Since single-junction solar cells have efficiency limits, multi-junction cells with stacked absorber layers of different band gaps can capture a broader range of the solar spectrum, thereby achieving higher efficiency. In this paper, we conducted a study where two different perovskite materials namely <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CH}_{3} \text{NH}_{3} \text{SnI}_{3}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Cs}_{2} \text{BiAgI}_{6}$</tex> were used as absorber layers on top of each other. Initially, we calculated the photovoltaic characteristics of the solar cell structure <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{N i} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{I T O} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{Z n O} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{C H}_{\mathbf{3}} \mathbf{N H}_{\mathbf{3}} \mathbf{S n I}_{\mathbf{3}} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{C s}_{\mathbf{2}} \mathbf{B i A g I}_{\mathbf{6}} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{S p i r o -}$</tex> OMeTAD/AI (structure A) by optimizing various input parameters of absorber materials such as thickness, acceptor density, band gap etc in SCAPS 1-D software. Thus, the final photovoltaic properties were found to be a maximum power conversion efficiency (PCE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 2. 4 4 \%}$</tex>, along with Voc, Jsc, and FF of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.4025 ~\mathrm{V}, 26.420173 ~\text{mA} / \text{cm}^{2}$</tex>, and 87.55 %, respectively. Later, we varied the materials in the electron transport layer (ETL) and the hole transport layer (HTL) to investigate their impact and improve efficiency. Due to the variation of the ETL and the HTL, three more structures were built. Those structures are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{N i} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{I T O} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{T i O}_{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{C H}_{3} \mathbf{N H}_{3} \mathbf{S n I}_{3} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{C s}_{2} \mathbf{B i A g I}_{6} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{C u}_{2} \mathbf{O} / \mathbf{A l}$</tex> (structure B) <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{N i} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{I T O} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{Z n O} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{C H}_{3} \mathbf{N H}_{3} \mathbf{S n I}_{3} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{C s}_{2} \mathbf{B i A g I}_{6} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{C u I} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{A l}$</tex> (structure C), and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{N i} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{I T O} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{T i O}_{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{C H}_{\mathbf{3}} \mathbf{N H}_{\mathbf{3}} \mathbf{S n I}_{\mathbf{3}} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{C s}_{\mathbf{2}} \mathbf{B i A g I}_{\mathbf{6}} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{C u I} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{A l}$</tex> (structure D). All the structures outperformed our initial structure, while Structure B has the highest PCE of 33.82%. The promising results suggest strong potential for large-scale manufacturing, calling for further experiments to evaluate stability and longterm durability.

title: Semi-Supervised Context-Aware Emotion Classification for Noisy Bangla Text Using Advanced Learning Strategies
authors: Abdullah Al Shafi, Sumaiya Rahim Suma
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Acquiring high-quality labeled Bangla text for emotion classification is expensive and error-prone, particularly for noisy social media content containing code-mixing, emojis, transliteration, and frequent spelling variations. Purely supervised models struggle to generalize under these conditions, as limited labeled data cannot capture the full diversity of expressions. To address this, we propose two complementary semi-supervised learning (SSL) strategies that leverage abundant unlabeled data while mitigating noise. Firstly, a Teacher-Student framework with an Enhanced Pseudo-Labeling Framework (EPLF) generates high-confidence pseudo-labels using calibration-aware thresholds, multiple pseudo-labeling strategies, and topic-emotion balancing, improving label efficiency, dataset coverage, and mitigate class imbalance. Secondly, GAN-BERT employs adversarial representation learning to regularize BERT embeddings, encouraging context-aware feature alignment and reducing overfitting on noisy inputs. We evaluate these methods on a manually curated Bangla social media dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 2, 6 9 8}$</tex> comments across multiple domains and six fine-grained emotions. The Teacher-Student + EPLF approach achieves a weighted F1-score of 66.2 %, while GAN-BERT achieves 66.8 %, both performed very well against supervised baselines trained on the same limited labeled data. These results demonstrate that combining context-aware pseudo-labeling and adversarial learning provides a scalable, effective, and practical solution for low-resource, noisy Bangla emotion classification, enabling models to leverage unlabeled data without compromising reliability or semantic understanding.

title: Bilingual Fake News Detection Using Transformer Models: A Comparative Study
authors: Tasnia Sharmin, Muhammad Nazrul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: With the proliferation of misinformation, the detection of fake news has become increasingly critical, establishing it as an emerging research area gaining significant attention. The increase in false information on multilingual platforms presents a challenge for the automated identification of misinformation. This research examines how well multilingual transformer-based models, such as mBERT (both cased and uncased), XLM-RoBERTa, mDeBERTa, and DistilBERT, perform in identifying fake news within a bilingual dataset that includes equal data in English and Bengali, which ensures models remain neutral in a multilingual context and do not favor a predominant language. Experimental findings indicate that XLM-RoBERTa and mDeBERTa deliver the best overall results, with both attaining an accuracy of 0.98 and F1-scores of 0.99, showing minimal misclassifications, and demonstrating excellent generalization. DistilBERT, while being more efficient in terms of computation, shows a minor reduction in accuracy. The analysis of the confusion matrix, training and validation loss curves validates the best-performing model's robustness, while comparisons of runtime demonstrate the trade-offs between efficiency and performance. The results highlight the effectiveness of transformer models in multilingual settings with limited resources, facilitating the development of scalable, language-independent systems for detecting fake news.

title: An Etiquette-Compliant Airport Customer Service Automation Framework Based on Efficient Fine-Tuning of Open-Source LLMs
authors: Sudha Bhattacharjee, Muhammad Shakil Pervez, Atia Sanjida Talukder, Md. Kamrul Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: This paper presents the development of an enhanced, resource-efficient artificial intelligence (AI) tool for generating domain-specific, etiquette-compliant responses to automate airport customer service operations. While pretrained large language models (LLMs) can generate fluent text, their direct application often produces generic, unstructured, and domain-misaligned outputs lacking the politeness and professionalism required in service contexts. To address these limitations, we curated a labeled dataset from online resources and synthetic generations, ensuring balanced category representation and clear separation between high- and low-quality responses, and used it for supervised fine-tuning of the LLM to enhance the generation. Our approach employs the open-source GPT-2 to ensure transparency, cost-effectiveness, and adaptability, fine-tuned through Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) for parameter-efficient, resource-friendly training. The fine-tuned model was evaluated against its pretrained counterpart using three complementary methods: (1) lexical and semantic similarity with reference answers via ROUGE and BLEU scores, (2) label-consistency verification using four independent classifiers—ModernBERT-base, e5-small-v2, DeBERTa-v3-large, and RoBERTa-base, and (3) visual evaluation through an interactive demonstration interface for qualitative comparison. Results demonstrate that the fine-tuned model significantly surpasses the pretrained model in contextual alignment, clarity, politeness, and label accuracy. Beyond airport operations, this framework can be applied to any customer service or agentic environment where response generation must balance technical accuracy, domain expertise, and alignment with human values.

title: Transformer Models for Combating Cyberbullying: Comparative Evaluation on Racism and Sexism Detection
authors: Ashiqur Rahman Saron, Hasin Almas Sifat, Foysal Ahmed Neloy, Soumodip Madhu, Chayti Rani Mondol, Md. Mortuza Ahmmed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Racism and sexism's constitute the majority of online abuse and still remains one of the main instigator for cyberbullying on the internet. Online bullying inflict severe mental harm and also affect the safety of women expressing their views online which otherwise always considered welcoming in nature. This research tries to investigates the current transformer based state of the art NLP models and their capabilities toward identifying racist and sexist conversation in social media discourse. More specifically, BERT, XLNet, and RoBERTa are compared against each other on multiple datasets accumulated from social media websites. Following the normalization and balancing operations, the final datasets had 3,940 samples related to racism and 6,746 samples related to sexism. Each transformer model was fine tuned and assessed based on these performance indicators: accuracy, recall, precision, F1 score, and AUC-ROC. The findings revealed that, in all evaluated datasets, RoBERTa topped BERT and XLNet. RoBERTa in the racism dataset achieved 91.88% accuracy and an AUC of 0.959, also acquired an AUC-ROC of 0.906 and 83.11% accuracy on the sexism dataset. This shows it's outstanding ability to recognize and capture abusive words in subtle and context sensitive uses of the given literature. Insights gathered from this work may drive future investigations and the help us find feasible solutions to this issue of cyberbullying in virtual environment.

title: Enhanced Semi-Supervised Breast Cancer Histopathology Classification Using Fixmatch with Domain-Specific Augmentation
authors: Nishat Sultana Nuha, Tanjim Mahmud, Abubokor Hanip, Mohammad Shahadat Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Histopathological images are essential for diagnosing breast cancer, but they present difficulties because of the absence of labeled data and variations in tissue staining and structure. FixMatch and a ResNet50 backbone are combined in this study to create a novel semi-supervised learning architecture. It makes advantage of particular histopathology-specific augmentations. The model efficiently generalizes across different samples by mimicking stain variations and tissue structure alterations through precise color modifications and geometric transformations. The BreaKHis dataset, which contains high-resolution images of breast histology classified into two classes—benign and malignant—is used for the experiments. When compared to conventional techniques, the experimental results demonstrate notable improvements in classification accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity. Grad-CAM visuals also aid by emphasizing the tissue regions that affect the model's predictions. This method addresses variations in breast cancer histopathology classification while providing a useful means of utilizing unlabeled data.

title: Domain-Specific NLP for Personalized Radiation Treatment Pathways with LLM Fine-Tuning
authors: Apurbo Biswas, Md. Owafeeuzzaman Patwary, Morzia Momo, Sumaiya Tasnim, Tofayet Sultan, Abhijit Bhowmik
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: General-purpose large language models (LLMs) such as GPT-4 and LLaMA-2 exhibit strong general reasoning capabilities but struggle to interpret domain-specific biomedical terminology and complex oncological semantics, often producing factual inaccuracies or hallucinations. To address these limitations, this study introduces the Radiation Oncology Model (ROM) a domain-adapted NLP system for personalized radiation treatment planning. ROM is built upon BioGPT and finetuned using the Radiation Oncology NLP Database (ROND) through a Retrieval-Augmented Fine-Tuning (RAG-FT) approach employing Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). The model integrates multimodal patient data, including clinical narratives, genomic information, and imaging features, to generate individualized radiotherapy pathways. Experimental results demonstrate significant performance improvements, achieving an average F1-score of 84.09%, surpassing baseline models such as RadOnc-GPT <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\approx 79.06 \%)$</tex>. ROM also exhibits reduced hallucination rates and improved contextual grounding aligned with clinical guidelines. Qualitative analyses confirm enhanced interpretability, factual consistency, and entity recognition across biomedical categories. These findings highlight that domain-specific fine-tuning and retrieval grounding substantially enhance clinical reliability and reasoning in AI-driven oncology. The proposed ROM framework establishes a pathway toward clinically interpretable and trustworthy LLMs for precision radiotherapy and broader personalized oncology applications.

title: AI-Driven Prediction of Students’ Academic Performance in Higher Education
authors: Mohamad Fayed, Ziad Doughan, Soubhi Abou Chahine
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: The influence of new technologies on many facets of our everyday life is growing especially in the area of education. Higher education's growth depends on advanced technologies such as Machine Learning (ML) and Artificial Intelligence (AI). With the goal of raising educational levels, raising student participation, simplifying processes, and improving general learning process, their usage in this field is quickly growing. Universities have to correctly forecast students' academic success so as to guarantee student achievement, find at-risk people, and carry out effective interventions. This study explores the application of effective machine learning and ensemble learning methods to anticipate the academic performance of students. Using a large dataset including behavioral patterns, academic achievement, and demographic information, we taught several machine learning algorithms including Naïve Bayes (NB), Decision Trees (DT), Logistic Regression (LR), and Support Vector Machines (SVM). Ensemble methods including Random Forests (RF), Gradient Boosting Machines, Bagging, Stacking, and Voting Classifiers were used to improve the accuracy and usability of predictions in more situations. Important parts of this study were preprocessing the data, creating features, and tuning hyperparameters to guarantee the model's performance. The experimental data reveal that single algorithms were less precise, accurate, recalled, and F1-scored than ensemble learning models, hence making them very dependable at predicting outcomes for several student populations. Moreover, feature importance analysis gave insightful knowledge of the main factors affecting academic performance, including earned credits, probation history, Grade Point Average (GPA), admissions scores, core averages, and elective averages. The results of the study illustrate how effectively ensemble learning techniques tackle various challenges related to forecasting issues in educational institutions. This study offers universities the groundwork to utilize data-driven techniques for early detection of academic difficulties, hence facilitating personal student assistance and enhancement overall higher education institution.

title: RetinaVision: XAI-Driven Augmented Regulation for Precise Retinal Disease Classification Using Deep Learning Framework
authors: Mohammad Tahmid Noor, Shayan Abrar, Jannatul Adan Mahi, Md Parvez Mia, Asaduzzaman Hridoy, Samanta Ghosh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Early and accurate classification of retinal diseases is critical to counter vision loss and for guiding clinical management of retinal diseases. In this study, we proposed a deep learning method for retinal disease classification utilizing optical coherence tomography (OCT) images from the Retinal OCT Image Classification - C8 dataset (comprising 24,000 labeled images spanning eight conditions). Images were resized to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$224 \times 224 \text{px}$</tex> and tested on convolutional neural network (CNN) architectures: Xception and InceptionV3. Data augmentation techniques (CutMix, MixUp) were employed to enhance model generalization. Additionally, we applied GradCAM and LIME for interpretability evaluation. We implemented this in a real-world scenario via our web application named RetinaVision. This study found that Xception was the most accurate network (95.25%), followed closely by InceptionV3 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(94.82 \%)$</tex>. These results suggest that deep learning methods allow effective OCT retinal disease classification and highlight the importance of implementing accuracy and interpretability for clinical applications.

title: MentalLLM: A Transformer-Based Large Language Model Framework for Depression Detection
authors: Fateha Jannat Ayrin, Md Mizanur Rahman, Montaser Abdul Quader, Arpita Dass, Md Maruf Hasan Khondaker, Madhusodan Chakraborty, Partha Pratim Saha, Md Rezaul Karim Khan, Vishwanath Akuthota, Ahmed Selim Anwar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Depression has emerged as a critical global mental health challenge, affecting over 300 million people worldwide. Traditional screening methods face limitations in scalability, accessibility, and early detection capabilities. Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown remarkable potential for understanding nuanced linguistic patterns associated with mental health conditions. This paper introduces MentalLLM, a novel transformer-based framework specifically designed for depression detection in social media text. Our approach leverages a fine-tuned RoBERTa architecture enhanced with emotional attention mechanisms and multi-task learning capabilities. We evaluate our model on multiple benchmark datasets including the Reddit Self-reported Depression Diagnosis (RSDD) dataset, CLPsych datasets, and a newly collected social media corpus. MentallLM achieves state-of-the-art performance with 96.8% accuracy, 94.2% precision, 95.7% recall, and 94.9% F1-score on the primary evaluation dataset. Our multi-task learning approach simultaneously predicts depression severity levels and identifies key emotional indicators, providing interpretable insights for mental health professionals. Comprehensive ablation studies demonstrate the effectiveness of our emotional attention mechanism and domain-specific pre-training. The model shows significant improvements over traditional machine learning approaches (SVM: 92.8%, XGBoost: 92.25%) and outperforms existing transformer-based methods by 3–7% across all metrics.

title: Design and Analysis of a Smart Solar-Powered Portable Charging System with IoT-Based Monitoring
authors: Riad Mollik Babu, Sakhawat Hossen Rakib, Md Hasibur Rahman, Md Shahidul Islam, Md. Shofiqul Islam, Atika Farhana
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: The rise in the use of portable electronic devices in fields like healthcare, calamity management, and communication has sparked greater requirement for sustainable and deployable energy alternatives, especially in remote areas and poor environments. There are many conventional sources of power such as diesel generators and the conventional power banks experience difficulties regarding the efficiency, scalability, and environmental sustainability. In this research, a Smart Solar Powered Portable Charging System is designed and evaluated by using simulation, the system has the ability to integrate the real time monitoring and predictive maintenance features which are based on Internet of Things (IoT). The designed system has combined the lightweight thin film photovoltaic (PV) panels, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{LiFePO}_{4}$</tex> battery energy storage system (BESS), and an adaptive charge controller, all of them simulated and validated in MATLAB Simulink. The device has the state of thermal management, and it can offer an increase in battery life of up to 20% for high temperatures operating environments. The economic analysis signifies that the payback period is about seven years and each unit can save about $ 1,500 per year. According to the environmental assessment, this process includes the reduction of consumption by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2-3$</tex> metric tons of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> per year. This study assists to promote strong, budget-friendly energy solutions aligned with global goals on decarbonization and fair access to energy.

title: A Home Energy Management System Using Alternating Operation and Temperature-Controlled AC
authors: Mohsen Nasri
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Energy is one of the biosphere's most vital resources. However, the meager resources that are currently accessible are becoming scarce due to the population's rapid rise and increased dependency on energy for daily use as a result of smart technologies and the Internet of Things (IoT) applications. Therefore, new methods are being developed and applied in the smart grid applications energy optimization process to ensure that users are making the best use of the available energy resources. To make the electrical grid more intelligent and adaptable, the new techniques must consider every facet of the system. In This paper we propose a Home Energy Management System (HEMS) that improves household energy consumption by integrating a temperature-controlled Air Conditioner (AC) with an Alternating Operation (AO) technique. The HEMS dynamically schedules appliances to reduce peak load while maintaining indoor temperatures within comfort thresholds, as shown by simulation results for a hot day. The per-appliance power charts and stacked scheduling demonstrate the coordinated functioning of multiple devices, including managing emergency loads like dishwashers and water pumps, without going against the AO approach. The system efficiently transfers loads to lower-cost periods, decreasing energy expenditure, according to the dynamic electricity price curves and total power usage. The suggested strategy strikes a compromise between user comfort, energy efficiency, and cost reduction, according to performance evaluation in terms of power consumption and electricity pricing.

title: MD-AGC: A Multi-Descriptor Adaptive Gamma Correction for Enhanced Urban Mapping and Smart City Analysis
authors: Chaima Ben Fraj, Dorsaf Omri, Tarek Bejaoui
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Radiometric inconsistencies caused by highly heterogeneous illumination conditions remain a major challenge in the processing of satellite imagery for urban analysis and smart-city applications. Shadows, overexposed surfaces, and uneven luminance distributions degrade both visual interpretability and the reliability of downstream tasks such as segmentation, object extraction, and change detection. Existing gamma correction strategies either rely on fixed parameters or depend on a single global descriptor, limiting their ability to generalize across complex urban scenes. To address these limitations, we propose a novel Multi-Descriptor Adaptive Gamma Correction (MD-AGC) algorithm that jointly integrates luminance, histogram dispersion, and structural contrast into a unified illumination model. The fused descriptor guides the estimation of an adaptive gamma value specifically tailored to the radiometric characteristics of each image region. Extensive experiments conducted on diverse satellite datasets demonstrate that MD-AGC provides superior enhancement quality, improved radiometric normalization, and consistent gains in downstream smart-city pipelines. The proposed method offers a lightweight yet robust solution suitable for large-scale operational processing.

title: Unigaussian: Driving Scene Reconstruction From Multiple Camera Models Via Unified Gaussian Representations
authors: Yuan Ren, Guile Wu, Runhao Li, Zheyuan Yang, Yibo Liu, Tongtong Cao, Xingxin Chen, Bingbing Liu
abstract: Urban scene reconstruction is crucial for real-world autonomous driving simulators. Although existing methods have achieved photorealistic reconstruction, they mostly focus on pinhole cameras and neglect fisheye cameras. In fact, how to effectively simulate fisheye cameras in driving scenes remains an unsolved problem. In this work, we propose UniGaussian, a novel approach that learns unified 3D Gaussian representations from multiple camera models for urban scene reconstruction in autonomous driving. Our contributions are two-fold. First, we propose a new differentiable rendering method that distorts 3D Gaussians using a series of affine transformations tailored to fisheye camera models. This addresses the compatibility issue of 3D Gaussian splatting with fisheye cameras, which is hindered by light ray distortion caused by lenses or mirrors. Besides, our method maintains real-time rendering while ensuring differentiability. Second, built on the differentiable rendering method, we design a new framework that learns unified Gaussian representations from multiple camera models. By applying affine transformations to adapt different camera models and regularizing the shared Gaussians with supervision from different modalities, our framework learns unified 3D Gaussian representations with input data from multiple sources and achieves holistic driving scene understanding. As a result, our approach models multiple sensors (pinhole and fisheye cameras) and modalities (depth, semantic, normal, and LiDAR point clouds). Our experiments show that our method achieves superior rendering quality and fast rendering speed for driving scene simulation.

title: Self-Supervised Implicit Attention Priors for Point Cloud Reconstruction
authors: Kyle Fogarty, Chenyue Cai, Jing Yang, Zhilin Guo, Cengiz Öztireli
abstract: Recovering high-quality surfaces from irregular point cloud is ill-posed unless strong geometric priors are avail able. We introduce an implicit self-prior approach that distills a shape-specific prior directly from the input point cloud itself and embeds it within an implicit neural representation. This is achieved by jointly training a small dictionary of learnable embeddings with an implicit distance field; at every query location, the field attends to the dictionary via cross-attention, enabling the network to capture and reuse repeating structures and long-range correlations inherent to the shape. Optimized solely with self-supervised point cloud reconstruction losses, our approach requires no external training data. To effectively integrate this learned prior while preserving input fidelity, the trained field is then sampled to extract densely distributed points and analytic normals via automatic differentiation. We integrate the resulting dense point cloud and corresponding normals into a robust implicit moving least squares (RIMLS) formulation. We show this hybrid strategy preserves fine geometric details in the input data, while leveraging the learned prior to regularize sparse regions. Experiments show that our method outperforms both classical and learning-based approaches in generating high-fidelity surfaces with superior detail preservation and robustness to common data degradations.

title: CTR3D: Cross-View Token Reduction for Dense Multi-View Generation
authors: Kunming Luo, Hongyu Yan, Yuan Liu, Zihao Zhang, Manyuan Zhang, Wenping Wang, Ping Tan
abstract: Recent multi-view diffusion (MVD) methods have utilized the generative capabilities of 2D image diffusion models to produce multi-view images from a single-view input. However, existing approaches often depend on dense crossview attention layers, which hinder scalability and fidelity due to their high computational costs. In this paper, we propose CTR3D, a novel method that incorporates token reduction in multi-view attention layers to efficiently generate dense, high-resolution multi-view images without restricting the camera viewpoints of the generated views. Our approach is designed into three key steps: redundancy removal, attention interaction, and token recovery. These steps leverage lightweight, projection-based techniques for multi-view token reduction and recovery, significantly improving the computational efficiency of MVD. By reducing the number of tokens in attention layers while preserving multi-view consistency, our model achieves state-of-the-art performance in novel view synthesis and 3D reconstruction while keeping efficiency for generation of dense high-resolution images and normals. Experimental results demonstrate that our method surpasses existing approaches, providing a more efficient and effective solution for multi-view generation. https://github.com/HKUST-SAIL/CTR3D

title: PhysMotion: Physics-Grounded Dynamics From a Single Image
authors: Xiyang Tan, Ying Jiang, Xuan Li, Zeshun Zong, Tianyi Xie, Yin Yang, Chenfanfu Jiang
abstract: We introduce PhysMotion, a novel framework that leverages principled physics-based simulations to guide intermediate 3D representations generated from a single image and input conditions (e.g., applied force and torque), producing high-quality, physically plausible video generation. By utilizing continuum mechanics-based simulations as a prior knowledge, our approach addresses the limitations of traditional data-driven generative models and results in more consistent physically plausible motions. Our framework begins by reconstructing a feed-forward 3D Gaussian from a single image through geometry optimization. This representation is then time-stepped using Material Point Method (MPM) with continuum mechanics-based elastoplasticity models, which provides a strong foundation for realistic <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d y$</tex> namics, albeit at a coarse level of detail. To enhance the geometry, appearance, and ensure spatiotemporal consistency, we refine the initial simulation using a text-to-image (T2I) diffusion model with cross-frame attention, resulting in a physically plausible video that retains intricate details comparable to the input image. We conduct comprehensive qualitative and quantitative evaluations to validate the efficacy of our method.

title: Design and Simulation of a 5-DOF Anthropomorphic Robot for Shoulder Rehabilitation Due to Osteoarthritis
authors: Guillermo Andrés Sifuentes Silva, Manuel Fernando Guzmán Castillo, Ayrton Nieves Acosta, Eddie Angel Sobrado Malpartida, Jose Luis Becerra Felipe, Nain Máximo Ramos Álvarez
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: This project focuses on the design and development of an automatic rehabilitation machine for patients with grade II osteoarthritis, based on a five-degree-of-freedom (5DOF) anthropomorphic robot intended to assist shoulder mobility therapies. The system is conceived to reproduce natural movements with high precision and efficiency, ensuring reliable mechanical support during rehabilitation routines. The structural design aims to optimize the mechanism's resistance to the torques and moments generated along various motion trajectories, ensuring durability and operational stability. Additionally, the design process incorporates anthropometric parameters based on the average height of Peruvian men and women, enabling the mechanism to provide an adjustable height range that accommodates individual user dimensions. This research seeks to validate the robot's capability to maintain efficient performance under variable loads by considering both static and dynamic behavior, delivering a robust and precise tool that contributes to improving the quality and consistency of rehabilitation treatments.

title: Mechanical Design and Testing of an Expandable Rover Chassis for Space-Constrained Payloads
authors: Miguel Angel Silva Plata, Dylan Ramiro Gutierrez Huallpara, Nayara Carminia Lara Ramos, Laura Letizia Choque Mamani, Raul Alejandro Giraldo Escalier, Franco Milan Morales Albarracín, Gabriel Antonio Rojas Silva
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: Planetary rover missions are subject to strict mass and storage volume constraints, which drive the development of compact systems capable of expanding their operational footprint. This work proposes a compact, expandable chassis for rovers that folds into a small configuration for transport and deploys a transformable locomotion mechanism combining wheeled and legged modes. The proposed design addresses the relationship between the volume occupied during storage and the effective footprint after deployment, with the goal of improving mobility and terrain adaptability without increasing launch volume. A kinematic model has been developed to evaluate the expansion behavior of the mechanism, and the concept has been validated through simulation and a low-cost experimental prototype. The main result is a quantified expansion ratio of 1.70 between the deployed and stored configurations, demonstrating a significant increase in the operational envelope. These results highlight the relevance of deployable rover prototypes as platforms for optimizing new mobility architectures under missiondriven constraints.

title: 6-DOF Robotic Arm Trajectory Optimization Model Based on Human Back Physiotherapy
authors: Yichao Liu, Huaizhi Wang, Jianjun Zhou, Yijia Li
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: In the context of physiotherapy on the human back, the operating range and singularities of a 6-DOF robotic arm significantly affect the range of trajectory planning. The human back has a relatively large area and diverse postures for therapy. Currently, using a mobile robot base or multiple robotic arms greatly increases the cost of the equipment, while using multiple short trajectories negatively affects the user experience. To address this scenario, which involves a large area, varied postures, and the need to maintain continuous trajectories, this paper designs an end-effector offset structure. By adding control over the sixth-axis, the impact of the first three axes on reach and singularities is reduced, thereby increasing the operational range and improving the stability and smoothness of the robotic arm. At the same time, the end-effector's periodicity issues are addressed to ensure continuous trajectory execution. The implementation demonstrates the effectiveness of our method.

title: Learning-Based Statistical Refinement for Denoising_supp1-3691286.pdf
authors: Rihuan Ke
abstract: This work proposes a learning-based statistical refinement method for improving the denoising results of a given denoiser without knowing the precise noise distribution or accessing clean images or calibration data. While there are many existing successful denoising approaches for handling different kinds of noise, they typically require accurate modeling of the images and the noise (implicitly or explicitly), and hence the denoising results can be suboptimal due to different practical factors such as imperfect models, unreliable noise assumptions, or low-quality data. In particular, when clean image samples are not available and there is a lack of knowledge of the underlying noise distribution, which is the case in various practical situations, the results may not align well with the noise statistics. The unawareness of useful statistical information leads to suboptimal results. This work aims to make the best use of the statistical information to improve the consistency between the given denoising results and the noise statistics, under the assumption that the noise is conditionally pixel-wise independent given the clean signal. A method, based on a Bayesian formulation of an auxiliary signal in the noisy data, is proposed for evaluating the consistency of the denoising results, without precise information on noise distribution. By leveraging the statistical information from noisy data, the method enhances the statistical noise consistency and improves denoising quality.

title: Bit Efficient Toeplitz Covariance Estimation
authors: Hongwei Xu, Zai Yang
abstract: This paper addresses the problem of estimating Toeplitz covariance matrices from partial entries of randomly quantized samples. To balance the trade-offs among the number of samples, the number of observed entries per sample, and the data resolution, we propose a ruler-based quantized Toeplitz covariance estimator. We derive non-asymptotic upper and lower bounds for the proposed estimator, and analyze the corresponding convergence rates. Our results characterize how sparse observation and coarse quantization affect the performance of the proposed estimator and suggest that reducing data resolution within a certain range has limited impact on estimation accuracy. Numerical experiments are provided to validate the theoretical findings.

title: Online Outcome-Driven Configuration Selection With Adaptive Grouping for Live Video Streams_supp1-3690872.pdf
authors: Xiangxiang Dai
abstract: Optimizing live video stream configurations, such as resolution, is essential for balancing inference accuracy and bitrate, thereby reducing data volume and conserving network resources in intelligent video analytics. Existing systems predominantly rely on offline or periodic strategies using historical data to determine optimal configurations. However, these methods may fall short in managing bitrate, which is highly sensitive to dynamic variations in video content and can benefit from real-time adjustments. To address these limitations, we propose the <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">O <inline-formula><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$_{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> MEGA</monospace> (Online Outcome-driven Grouping Configuration Adaptation) framework. Motivated by unique experimental observations, <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">O <inline-formula><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$_{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> MEGA</monospace> adapts configurations in an “outcome-driven” manner by directly utilizing real-time online bitrate outcomes from encoded video streams. This approach allows efficient and lightweight integration of outcome feedback into configuration decisions without incurring significant analysis overhead that could negate the benefits of adaptation. Moreover, <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">O <inline-formula><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$_{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> MEGA</monospace> introduces a dual grouping mechanism that enables cross-camera collaboration on configuration sharing, expediting configuration selection in densely deployed camera networks. Unlike existing methods that rely solely on spatio-temporal correlations, our framework groups cameras based on real-time outcome feedback, thus adaptively optimizing group-level performance online. We provide theoretical guarantees to ensure the interpretability and effectiveness of the framework. Experimental results on a testbed with four NVIDIA development kits and an edge server demonstrate up to 6.8% accuracy improvement or 13.2% data volume reduction compared to the state-of-the-art under four typical vision queries.

title: Robustness of Prompting: Enhancing Robustness of Large Language Models Against Prompt Attacks
authors: Lin Mu, Guowei Chu, Li Ni, Lei Sang, Yiwen Zhang
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable performance across various tasks by effectively utilizing a prompting strategy. However, they are highly sensitive to input perturbations, such as typographical errors or slight character order errors, which can significantly impair their performance. Despite advances in prompting techniques such as Chain-of-Thought and automatic prompt generation, developing a prompting strategy that explicitly mitigates the negative impact of such perturbations remains an open challenge. To bridge this gap, we propose Robustness of Prompting (RoP), a novel prompting strategy aimed at enhancing the robustness of LLMs. RoP consists of two stages: Error Correction and Guidance. In the Error Correction stage, RoP applies diverse perturbation methods to generate adversarial examples, which are used to generate prompts that correct input errors automatically. In the Guidance stage, RoP generates an optimal guidance prompt based on the corrected input, guiding the model to generate more robust and accurate inferences. Through comprehensive experiments spanning arithmetic, commonsense, and logical reasoning tasks, we demonstrate that RoP significantly improves LLMs’ robustness against adversarial perturbations. Crucially, it preserves model accuracy with only minimal degradation compared to clean input scenarios, thereby establishing RoP as a practical and effective approach for enhancing LLM robustness in real-world applications.1

title: TTM-KCOSBLS: Open-Set Fault Diagnosis and Few-Shot Class Incremental Learning Framework of Wind Turbine Transmission System with Thermal Twin Model
authors: Shiyu Lin, Hongshan Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Due to the complex and dynamic operating conditions, the wind power transmission system (WTTS) faces new faults in the long-term industrial operation. This will lead to frequent misdiagnosis of traditional fault diagnosis (FD) methods. It is a key challenge to accurately diagnosis the emerging new faults and realize incremental learning by using limited new fault samples. This paper proposed a thermal twin model and Kolmogorov-Arnold network enhanced causal open-set broad learning system (TTM-KCOSBLS) for WTTS open-set fault diagnosis and few-shot class incremental learning (OSFD-FSCIL). First, a TTM fault feature construction method is proposed to construct high-quality fault features of WTTS from temperature monitoring data. Second, the proposed KCOSBLS includes multiple sparse causal attention and unknown fault discriminator based on extreme value theory to realize interpretable OSFD. Third, a FSCIL method based on TTM generation is proposed, which solves the problem of insufficient new fault samples in CIL. An orthogonal loss function is designed to balance the old and new knowledge, and the CIL of unknown faults in WTTS is realized. Finally, a large number of experiments were carried out with the data of 2MW wind turbines in northern China. The results show that the OSFD and CIL average diagnostic accuracy of TTM-KCOSBLS can reach 0.9425 and 0.9344, respectively, which are greatly improved compared with the comparison method. The research provides an effective new method for long-term FD of WTTS.

title: Different Damp-Heat-Induced Series Resistance Degradation Behaviors in Fielded Crystalline Silicon Photovoltaic Modules Due to Difference in Bill of Materials_supp1-3691199.docx
authors: Elizabeth Palmiotti
abstract: This case study investigates mono-crystalline silicon modules from underperforming portions of a utility-scale photovoltaic power plant. Field-collected I-V curves and electroluminescence imaging suggested that increased series resistance was a primary factor driving module degradation. Selected modules were removed from the field for further analysis, including incremental damp heat accelerated testing, which confirmed a progression in series resistance degradation. Two distinct cell degradation behaviors became apparent during the investigation. Cross-sectional scanning electron microscopy (with elemental analysis) and scanning spreading resistance microscopy identified key differences between the two degradation mechanisms, primarily grid finger width and contact resistance. Additionally, the study highlights the reliability implications of retest requirements in International Electrotechnical Commission 61215 for material changes and how they may have mitigated the degradation observed at this site.

title: Enhanced Integrated Decision and Control for High-Level Automated Vehicles and Its Experiment Verification_supp2-3695533.mp4
authors: Shengbo Li
abstract: Learning through experience is essential for high-level autonomous driving systems, as it has the potential to enhance driving performance in corner cases. However, current decision and control modules adopt an empirical design paradigm for engineering efficiency, relying heavily on expert rules or real-vehicle data, failing to fully cover and optimize edge scenarios. To address this gap, we propose an enhanced integrated decision and control method that leverages reinforcement learning as the optimal control problem solver, endowing high-level automated vehicles with experience data usage. Specifically, a constrained mixed policy gradient algorithm is developed, which combines and dynamically adjusts the application ratio of experience data and the environmental model during training. This approach achieves fast convergence while maintaining high performance even with inaccurate analytic models. Furthermore, an attention based encoding network is designed to accommodate diverse driving states in urban traffic, integrating an embedding network for feature extraction and a weighting network for feature fusion, realizing order-insensitive encoding and importance differentiation of road users. The trained policy is deployed on a fully functional autonomous vehicle. Experiments at a signalized intersection show that the proposed method can accurately identify critical surrounding obstacles and execute safe, efficient, and intelligent driving behaviors across 32 scenarios.

title: DNN Obfuscation with Contrastive Learning for Efficient Defense against Side-Channel Attacks
authors: Yidan Sun, Guiyuan Jiang, Jiayang Liu, Qian Sun, Peilan He, Siew-Kei Lam
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) have achieved no table advancements across various domains. Yet, they remain vulnerable to Side-Channel Attacks (SCAs) which expose them to model theft, posing substantial security threats. Existing countermeasures for mitigating SCAs using DNN obfuscation often involve impractical hardware modifications or suffer from poor scalability due to the need for time-consuming deployment on target platforms. We introduce CLOb-DNN (Contrastive Learning Obfuscation for DNNs), a novel contrastive learning based framework that improves both the efficiency and robust ness of DNN obfuscation against SCAs. By leveraging triplet networks, we effectively reduce the need for repeated physical deployments, substantially improving obfuscation efficiency while ensuring that the obfuscated DNNs meet latency constraints. Our framework integrates architecture-level and scheduling-level obfuscation to enhance stability and avoid costly modifications to backend source codes. We perform extensive evaluations using multiple state-of-the-art attack models to demonstrate the robustness of our approach against diverse threats. Experiments show that our method reduces obfuscation time by approximately 90%, achieves superior defense capability, and successfully meets various latency requirements, thereby offering a practical and effective solution for protecting DNN IPs against SCAs.

title: Accurate phase modulation of Φ-OTDR system based on arbitrary phase-shifting technique
authors: Xianglei Pan, Ke Cui, Haojie Huang, Zhongjie Ren, Rihong Zhu
abstract: Distributed acoustic sensing (DAS) based on phase-sensitive optical time-domain reflectometry (Φ-OTDR) enables the continuous, real-time detection of vibrations and acoustic fields along optical fibers. The phase extraction method plays a pivotal role in determining the performance of the Φ-OTDR system. In this study, we propose a novel three-step phase-shifting demodulation model that is suitable for arbitrary phase-shifting angles. Building upon this model, a piezoelectric ceramic transducer (PZT) within an unbalanced Michelson interferometer (MI) is employed as the phase modulation carrier. A sinusoidal signal serves as the phase modulation source. By controlling the relative timing between the probe pulses and the modulation signal, three adjacent Rayleigh backscattering (RBS) traces are encoded with distinct phase-shifting angles. Phase recovery is then performed using the proposed demodulation model. Experimental results demonstrate the successful demodulation of signals across a frequency range from 0.1 Hz to 1 kHz over a sensing length of 9.85 km, achieving a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of 41.6 dB. This approach provides a cost-effective system configuration and high computational efficiency, offering a promising solution for the large-scale engineering application of DAS.

title: RD-PCN: A Secure Role-Differentiated Payment Channel Network for Heterogeneous Nodes_supp1-3692826.pdf
authors: Ning Lu
abstract: Scalability is a critical challenge for blockchain-based cryptocurrencies and has become a significant bottleneck in large-scale applications such as retail. Payment channel networks (PCNs) are among the most promising solutions to this challenge. However, nodes in real-world PCNs exhibit heterogeneous behaviors and capabilities, causing existing schemes to perform poorly when deployed in practical environments. In this paper, we first demonstrate, through a measurement study, the existence of node heterogeneity in real-world PCNs; and then propose RD-PCN, a role-differentiated PCN that accommodates such heterogeneity by explicitly separating node roles and responsibilities. We address two key challenges in realizing RD-PCN. To improve channel fund utilization, RD-PCN introduces multi-party payment channels (MPCs) to selectively connect nodes at the network edge. We accordingly design a PCN-compatible MPC, termed PC-MPC, representing the first practical solution for deploying MPCs in PCNs. To securely outsource routing tasks to resource-rich nodes, we design a privacy-preserving and fair routing scheme based on trusted execution environments and the proposed topology synchronization mechanism. We prove the security of PC-MPC and the routing scheme under the universally composable framework. Experiments show that RD-PCN improves the payment success ratio by at least 18.9% compared to representative schemes, while increasing fund utilization by 60%.

title: Tailoring of Magnetic Properties and Giant Magnetoimpedance Effect in Co-Fe-Si-B-Ni-Mo Amorphous Microwires
authors: Mohamed Salaheldeen, Valentina Zhukova, Pablo A. Rodríguez Jiménez, Julian Gonzalez, Arcady Zhukov
abstract: We studied the impact of heat treatment on the magnetic properties and Giant Magnetoimpedance (GMI) effect of Co-Fe-Si-B glasscoated microwires doped by Ni-and Mo. A substantial change in the hysteresis loop shape from linear to rectangular, a significant increase in the GMI ratio, ΔZ/Z, from ~370% up to 470% and a modification in the ΔZ/Z dependence on magnetic field are observed. The observed dependencies are explained by a change in the magnetization reversal mechanism from magnetization rotation towards magnetization switching through the domain wall, DW, propagation. Such change in the magnetization reversal mechanism induced by heat treatment is confirmed experimentally by Sixtus-Tonks-like method, revealing fast DW propagation with velocities up to 850 m/s. The observed results are discussed in terms of internal stress relaxation and a domain structure modification upon heat treatment of studied microwire.

title: Slot-Rotated Dual-Patch Circularly Polarized Microstrip Antenna for 5.8 GHz WLAN Applications
authors: Fariha Tasnim, Syed Md Hasin Tasnim, Md Faruk Muntasir Banna, Md Aminul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: A dual-patch circularly polarized microstrip antenna is introduced for WLAN applications, operating at 5.8 GHz. The design includes a patch and a parasitic patch. Circular polarization is achieved through the rotated slot perturbation method, connecting two orthogonal modes with a 90° phase difference. Full-wave simulations show excellent impedance matching and clear polarization, with a return loss of −27.91 dB, an axial ratio of 1.13, a VSWR of 1.083, and a gain of about 7.03 dBi. The compact single-layer design, based on Rogers RT5880, ensures a simple structure. This makes the antenna ideal for indoor WLAN environments that face multipath propagation and random device orientations.

title: Enhancing Dry Eye Disease Classification Using Hybrid Ensemble Learning with XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost
authors: Nusrat Jahan, Refah Tasnia, Md Rakib Mahmud, Ashutosh Roy, Jasmin Akter, Md Mahabube Alahi Atik, Abdul Ahad
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Dry Eye Disease (DED) occurs frequently among patients as it causes substantial life quality deterioration. The management process requires both early diagnosis together with precise disease classification to be successful. The authors developed a multibox ensemble algorithm that utilizes XGBoost and LightGBM and CatBoost to advance DED classification accuracy levels. The database analyzed contains multiple numerical and categorical features which include data on patient age alongside stress scales and symptoms related to eye health. The preprocessing steps included encoding categorical data while standardizing numerical variables in addition to correcting class distribution using balancing techniques. The ensemble classifier utilized multiple single model strengths to generate predictions about DED. The metrics for performance assessment included accuracy, precision and recall and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score. The combination model yielded much better prediction outcomes than when using models separately which indicated better predictive capability and robust performance. The results demonstrate that joining these advanced machine learning algorithms produces stronger DED classification systems which face down challenges from class imbalance and feature complexity. The proposed approach shows great potential for early detection of Dry Eye Disease because it leads to improved clinical management and customized therapies.

title: Harnessing AI for Sustainable Agriculture: Predicting Plant Growth in Greenhouse Systems Under Climate Change Pressures
authors: Arka Dutta, Ashim Dey, Annesha Das
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: In the modern era, climate change is a significant worry for nearly every nation today. This global warming poses a threat to crop production, which is expected to impact food security in the future. Hence, greenhouse cultivation has emerged as a viable solution. It is essential to forecast plant growth rates based on plants' physiological characteristics in a greenhouse setting. To achieve this, A publicly available dataset of 30,000 plant growth rates in greenhouse conditions is analyzed and utilized here. Nine machine learning methods were explored to forecast the growth rate of plants in a greenhouse setting. This research revealed that the highest <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> value of approximately 99% was obtained from several models, including SVR, Random Forest, CatBoost, KNN, XGBoost, and Decision Tree. However, based on other evaluation metrics, the KNN model achieved the best overall performance, achieving the lowest error values among all models. Rigorous validation confirmed the robustness of the models, and feature analysis highlighted the dominant role of traits in driving plant growth. The results indicate a strong correlation between plant height growth and variables such as temperature, leaf area, and chlorophyll content. This method improves model performance and offers insights for precision farming techniques. It is anticipated that this study will aid the area of greenhouse farming and support the agricultural sector in addressing the issue of global warming. This study also uncovers the relationship between the rate of plant growth and various physiological traits, which may positively impact the solution to the global food scarcity problem.

title: IoT-Enabled Insulator Crack Detection Using a YOLOv11n Algorithm on Raspberry Pi 5
authors: Fauzia Fariha Trona, Farhan Ferdous, Ahanaf Tahmid Romit, Riad Bin Islam, Bishwajit Banik Pathik
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Insulators are essential for reliable power transmission. They often develop cracks due to stress, weather, or aging. If these fissures are not found in time, they may spread and cause failures. Traditional inspection techniques are less efficient because they are primarily manual and take a long time. In this research, a lightweight YOLOv11n deep learning model on a Raspberry Pi 5 is used to create an Internet of Things-enabled system for real-time insulator crack detection. This uses a Pi camera Module to take pictures, detects cracks on the gadget, and uses lowpower wireless communication to send out alarms. According to experimental findings, the method offers precise, quick, and contact-free detection, which makes it an effective choice for condition-based tracking and improving grid reliability.

title: Integrating Deep Feature Extraction with ML for Robust Lung Cancer Classification on CT Images
authors: Sayeeda Khan, Md. Ibne Shihab Shad, Md. Imran Hossain, Akif Mahdi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) are highly effective for image analysis but it requires large amounts of labeled data to get better, generalized, stable and reproducible result. In medical imaging, like lung cancer CT scans for multi-class cancerous image classification, annotated data is usually limited. As a result, training a DNN directly often leads to overfitting and poor generalization. To address this, pre-trained DNNs on medical images were used as feature extractors, and the extracted features were analyzed using principal component analysis (PCA). EfficientNet-B0, pre-trained on medical images, exhibited the highest PCA1 and PCA2 values, indicating strong feature representation. Due to the relatively high PCA2, the third principal component (PCA3) was also included to enhance discriminative power. Classical machine learning (ML) classifiers including support vector machines (SVM), random forests (RF), logistic regression (LR), and gradient boosting (GB) were trained on the reduced features. SVM achieved the best performance with top three PCA, with a validation accuracy of 93 % and crossvalidation accuracy of 91.5 %, validation F 1 -score of 93.4 % and cross-validation validation F1-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 1. 9 \%}$</tex>. Five-fold crossvalidation confirmed consistent performance without overfitting. Grad-CAM was applied to visualize the most influential regions, enhancing interpretability. The framework enables six-class lung cancer classification, demonstrating reproducibility and practical utility for limited medical image datasets.

title: An Interpretable Ensemble CNN Framework for MRI-Based Diagnosis of Neurodegenerative Diseases
authors: Ayesha Siddiqua, Atib Mohammad Oni, Abu Saleh Musa Miah, Najmul Hassan, Md Maruf Al Hossain Prince, Jungpil Shin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: In medical imaging, convolutional neural networks have made major advances, particularly in the classification of neurodegenerative disorders like Alzheimer's and Parkinson's disease. But their “black-box” nature makes model interpretability difficult, which is crucial in healthcare for confidence and informed decision-making. This research introduces a multiclass multiclass transfer learning-based ensemble classification system that classifies brain MRI images into AD, PD, and healthy controls using EfficientNetB7, ResNetV2-50, and DenseNet121. Soft voting determines categorisation by averaging predictions from all three models and selecting the class with the highest likelihood. Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) visualises brain areas impacting model predictions to improve interpretability. This method increases classification performance and provides transparent insight into decision making, increasing trust in AI-assisted neuroimaging applications.

title: LabEquipVis: Automated Detection of Computer Laboratory Equipment Using YOLOv8 and Transfer Learning
authors: B. M. Shahria Alam, Omar Faruq Shikdar, Fahad Ahammed, Md Parvez Mia, Jannatul Adan Mahi, Nishat Tasnim Niloy
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Computer labs are a crucial component of the modern education system and an essential facility for research. Monitoring these labs to inventory the number and type of digital equipment, especially in large facilities, presents a significant challenge. An automated monitoring system offers an effective solution to this problem. In this research, we created a novel dataset of computer lab environments consisting of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2, 5 8 4}$</tex> images, annotating all present equipment. We deployed deep learning architectures utilizing transfer learning, employing three versions of the YOLO algorithm: YOLOv8, YOLOv9, and YOLOv11.
