YOLOv8 emerged as the best-performing model, achieving an accuracy of 87.89%. Its mean average precision (mAP) was also highly impressive, exceeding 87% for all but one class. A web app has been developed to showcase the effectiveness of our system.

title: A Simulation Framework for Pre-Deployment Optimization of Digital Twin Architectures in Resource-Constrained Precision Agriculture
authors: Sourour Meddeb, Selma Batti, Khaled Bousetta, Habib Fathallah
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Physical deployment of IoT-based precision agriculture systems in remote regions incurs substantial costs ($400-600/tree) with uncertain performance outcomes. This paper presents a simulation-driven framework for systematic evaluation and optimization of digital twin (DT) architectures before hardware investment. We propose a methodological approach integrating: (1) a five-layer DT architecture adapted for oasis agroecosystems addressing coupled groundwater-salinity-microclimate dynamics; (2) a simulation methodology combining NS-3 network modeling with crop physiological models; (3) standardized test scenarios for comparative architectural analysis across energy management, network topology, and sampling strategies; (4) multi-objective evaluation criteria balancing autonomy, reliability, and decision quality. Applied to olive cultivation in North African oases, the framework enables systematic comparison of configuration alternatives through scenarios spanning heat waves, extended cloud cover, and infrastructure failures. This methodology provides a reproducible approach for agricultural IoT design in resource-constrained contexts, with framework validation through case study application and physical deployment identified as essential future work.

title: IEEE Guide for the Selection, Testing, Application, and Installation of 300 V to 500 kV Cables Having Radial-Moisture Barriers and/or Longitudinal Water Blocking
authors: N/A
abstract: Detailed information relating to the design, testing, application, and installation of various types of electrical cables to reduce the deleterious effect of moisture and chemical ingress and resultant failures in service is provided in this guide. This includes single and multiconductor cables over a complete range of voltage ratings. Testing criteria and installation methods are also covered along with many technical references.

title: Vibration-Based Automatic Fault Detection for Rotary-Percussive Drilling Via IT2 T-S Fuzzy Modeling and Adaptive Observer
authors: Satyam Paul, Magnus Löfstrand
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: This paper presents a vibration–based, observer–driven fault detection scheme for rotary–percussive drilling that is both physics–grounded and robust to operating–regime variability. A compact axial–torsional plant with single–cutter bit–rock interaction captures loading/unloading asymmetry and frictional torque coupling. To compensate for salient nonlinearities and premise uncertainty (e.g., intermittent contact, rate effects), we embed the dynamics in an interval type–2 Takagi–Sugeno (IT2–T–S) fuzzy framework with explicitly defined IF–THEN rules and type reduction, yielding a convex blend of local linear models suitable for analysis and synthesis. An adaptive Luenberger observer is then designed to (i) reconstruct the nominal vibration response, (ii) generate a residual sensitive to faults yet tolerant to modelling errors and measurement noise, and (iii) deliver an online estimate of an unknown axial fault input. A Lyapunov function with vertex LMI conditions guarantees exponential convergence in the fault–free case and uniform ultimate boundedness under bounded faults; the fault–estimation update law is derived to ensure closed–loop stability. Simulations with percussion–style axial forcing demonstrate three key outcomes on a short time horizon: residuals remain within a noise–based threshold pre–fault and cross the band at the fault onset; the estimated fault rapidly converges to the true magnitude with negligible steady bias; and the state–error norm decays quickly pre–fault and exhibits a bounded transient post–fault. The results indicate that the proposed IT2–T–S adaptive observer provides an implementation–ready path to reliable, vibration–based fault detection for drilling systems.

title: UniLiPs: Unified LiDAR Pseudo-Labeling with Geometry-Grounded Dynamic Scene Decomposition
authors: Filippo Ghilotti, Samuel Brucker, Nahku Saidy, Matteo Matteucci, Mario Bijelic, Felix Heide
abstract: Unlabeled LiDAR logs, in autonomous driving applications, are inherently a gold mine of dense 3D geometry hiding in plain sight - yet they are almost useless without human labels, highlighting a dominant cost barrier for autonomous-perception research. In this work we tackle this bottleneck by leveraging temporal-geometric consistency across LiDAR sweeps to lift and fuse cues from text and 2 Dvision foundation models directly into 3D, without any manual input. We introduce an unsupervised multimodal pseudo-labeling method relying on strong geometric priors learned from temporally accumulated LiDAR maps, alongside with a novel iterative update rule that enforces joint geometric-semantic consistency, and vice-versa detecting moving objects from inconsistencies. Our method simultaneously produces 3D semantic labels, 3D bounding boxes, and dense LiDAR scans, demonstrating robust generalization across three datasets. We experimentally validate that our method compares favorably to existing semantic segmentation and object detection pseudo-labeling methods, which often require additional manual supervision. We confirm that even a small fraction of our geometrically consistent, densified LiDAR improves depth prediction by 51.5 % and 22.0 % MAE in the 80-150 and 150-250 meters range, respectively.

title: SAIL-Recon: Large SfM by Augmenting Scene Regression with Localization
authors: Junyuan Deng, Heng Li, Tao Xie, Weiqiang Ren, Qian Zhang, Ping Tan, Xiaoyang Guo
abstract: Scene regression methods, such as VGGT [86], solve the Structure-from-Motion (SfM) problem by directly regressing camera poses and 3D scene structures from input images. They demonstrate impressive performance in handling images under extreme viewpoint changes. However, these methods struggle to handle a large number of input images. To address this problem, we introduce SAIL-Recon, a feed-forward Transformer for large scale SfM, by augmenting the scene regression network with visual localization capabilities. Specifically, our method first computes a neural scene representation tokens from a subset of anchor images. The regression network is then fine-tuned to reconstruct all input images conditioned on this neural scene representation. Comprehensive experiments show that our method not only scales efficiently to large-scale scenes, but also achieves state-of-the-art results on both camera pose estimation and novel view synthesis benchmarks, including TUM-RGBD, CO3Dv2, and Tanks & Temples. Code and models are publicly available here.

title: FastMap: Revisiting Structure from Motion Through First-Order Optimization
authors: Jiahao Li, Haochen Wang, Muhammad Zubair Irshad, Igor Vasiljevic, Matthew R. Walter, Vitor Campagnolo Guizilini, Greg Shakhnarovich
abstract: We propose FastMap, a new global structure from motion method focused on speed and simplicity. Previous methods like COLMAP and GLOMAP are able to estimate high-precision camera poses, but suffer from poor scalability when the number of matched keypoint pairs becomes large, mainly due to the time-consuming process of secondorder Gauss-Newton optimization. Instead, we design our method solely based on first-order optimizers. To obtain maximal speedup, we identify and eliminate two key performance bottlenecks: computational complexity and the kernel implementation of each optimization step. Through extensive experiments, we show that FastMap is up to 10 × faster than COLMAP and GLOMAP with GPU acceleration and achieves comparable pose accuracy. Project webpage: https://jiahao.ai/fastmap.

title: SpotLight: Shadow-Guided Object Relighting via Diffusion
authors: Frédéric Fortier-Chouinard, Zitian Zhang, Louis-Etienne Messier, Mathieu Garon, Anand Bhattad, Jean-François Lalonde
abstract: Recent work has shown that diffusion models can serve as powerful neural rendering engines that can be leveraged for inserting virtual objects into images. However, unlike typical physics-based renderers, these neural rendering engines are limited by the lack of manual control over the lighting, which is often essential for improving or personalizing the desired image outcome. In this paper, we show that precise and controllable lighting can be achieved without any additional training, simply by supplying a coarse shadow hint for the object. Indeed, we show that injecting only the desired shadow of the object into a pre-trained diffusionbased neural renderer enables it to accurately shade the object according to the desired light position, while properly harmonizing the object (and its shadow) within the target background image. Our method, SpotLight, is entirely training-free and leverages existing neural rendering approaches to achieve controllable relighting. We show that SpotLight achieves superior object compositing results, both quantitatively and perceptually, as confirmed by a user study, outperforming existing diffusion-based models specifically designed for relighting. We also demonstrate other applications, such as hand-scribbling shadows and full-image relighting, demonstrating its versatility.

title: MoAngelo: Motion-Aware Neural Surface Reconstruction for Dynamic Scenes
authors: Mohamed Ebbed, Zorah Lähner
abstract: Dynamic scene reconstruction from multi-view videos remains a fundamental challenge in computer vision. While recent neural surface reconstruction methods have achieved remarkable results in static 3D reconstruction, extending these approaches with comparable quality for dynamic scenes introduces significant computational and representational challenges. Existing dynamic methods focus on novel-view synthesis, therefore, their extracted meshes tend to be noisy. Even approaches aiming for geometric fidelity often result in too smooth meshes due to the ill-posedness of the problem. We present a novel framework for highly detailed dynamic reconstruction that extends the static 3D reconstruction method NeuralAngelo to work in dynamic settings. To that end, we start with a high-quality template scene reconstruction from the initial frame using NeuralAngelo, and then jointly optimize deformation fields that track the template and refine it based on the temporal sequence. This flexible template allows updating the geometry to include changes that cannot be modeled with the deformation field, for instance occluded parts or the changes in the topology. We show superior reconstruction accuracy in comparison to previous state-of-the-art methods on the ActorsHQ dataset.

title: Feature-Based Image Selection (FBIS) for Enhancing 3D Reconstruction in Agriculture
authors: Jomana A. Abdelmoaty, Zoltán Istenes
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: The future of technology increasingly relies on artificial intelligence systems, which must be capable of perceiving visual scenes like humans to interpret and understand their surroundings, making 3D reconstruction a fundamental component of this capability. This paper focuses on 3D reconstruction of an apple plantation for monitoring purposes using a mobile robot equipped with an RGB-D RealSense D435 camera. It presents a comparative analysis of two widely used reconstruction tools, COLMAP and Open3D, which utilise different input data types: RGB for COLMAP and RGB-D for Open3D, to identify the most suitable approach for outdoor environments exposed to sunlight. To reduce processing overhead and improve reconstruction efficiency, a Feature-Based Image Selection (FBIS) algorithm is proposed. FBIS leverages feature extraction techniques to eliminate redundant images that lack novel features. By evaluating the performance of COLMAP and Open3D, this work offers insights into their suitability for field conditions and proposes an effective strategy to optimise 3D reconstruction in agricultural settings. The apple plantation datasets are available at https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17183457.

title: Artificial Neural Networks for Solving Forward and Inverse Kinematics on a 7 Degree-of-Freedom Robotic Manipulator
authors: Aanchal Save
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: Traditionally, forward or direct kinematics refers to computing the end-effector pose of a robotic arm given its joint angles, while conversely, inverse kinematics is used to find the joint angles needed to reach a desired end-effector pose. Unlike forward kinematics, inverse kinematics lacks a general deterministic solution and becomes increasingly complex as the number of degrees of freedom (DoF) grows. While Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) have shown promise in solving inverse kinematics for low-DoF systems, their accuracy diminishes in higher-DoF configurations. This study presents a neural network-based approach for both forward and inverse kinematics on a 7-DoF robotic arm, detailing the data generation process, model architecture, and training methodology. The forward kinematics model is used to iteratively validate the inverse kinematics model, a unique solution that allows the model to achieve a median endeffector position error of 9.95 centimeters.

title: Family Privacy Perceptions of Robots in the Home: A Multi-Site Convergence across USA, Spain, and Ukraine
authors: Leigh Levinson, Gonzalo A. Garcia, Levko Ivanchuk, Guillermo Perez, Gloria Alvarez, J. Gabriel Amores, Manuel Castro-Malet, Randy Gomez, Selma Sabanovic
abstract: None

title: Transmitter IQ Skew and Imbalance Estimation for Alamouti Coded Simplified Coherent Optical System
authors: Na Shan, Weidong Ma
abstract: The Alamouti-coded simplified coherent receiver utilizing a single balanced photodetector (BPD) is a compelling candidate for future low-cost coherent optical systems. However, Alamouti coded scheme is inherently sensitive to transmitter impairments, particularly in-phase/quadrature (IQ) time skew and power imbalance, due to its strict orthogonality requirements. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes a 4×4 MIMO channel estimation method. Using standard training symbols, the technique estimates both IQ time skew and power imbalance for an Alamouti-coded OFDM signal in a simplified coherent receiver. The proposed method directly derives these impairments from the estimated frequency-domain channel coefficients. The experimental results show that the estimation errors of IQ time skew and power imbalance are within ±0.5 ps and ±0.4 dB when the two polarizations are equally mixed.

title: A 9.0–20.0-GHz CMOS Variable-Gain Amplifier With Wide Tuning Range and Minimized Phase Errors Using Current Reuse and Reactance Compensation
authors: Seongick Jo, Geonwoo Lee, Juntaek Oh
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a 9.0–20.0-GHz CMOS wideband variable-gain amplifier (VGA) with minimized gain and phase errors in a 65-nm RF CMOS process. The VGA employs a cross-coupled variable-gain stage comprising main and auxiliary gain cells using a current steering topology, where a single control voltage inversely modulates their currents to maximize the gain tuning range. To suppress phase distortions, a varactor-based adaptive reactance compensation (ARC) technique is introduced by placing varactors between the gates of the auxiliary and main cells, thereby automatically canceling the auxiliary input-capacitance variation during tuning. The prototype occupies 0.096 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and consumes 24 mW from a 1.5-V supply. Measurements show a 10.7-dB peak gain and a 23.3-dB tuning range over 9.0–20.0 GHz, with root-mean-square (rms) gain and phase errors below 0.17 dB and 0.79°, respectively.

title: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters Information for Authors
authors: N/A

title: SciNumQA: A Benchmark for Complex Numerical Reasoning over Scientific Tables
authors: Sohyun Son, Yubin Kim, Gangwoo Kim, Donghee Choi, Mujeen Sung
abstract: Numerical reasoning is crucial for extracting meaningful information from scientific tables, enabling deeper analysis beyond simple lookup. As automated analysis of structured data becomes increasingly important not only in scientific research but also in industrial applications such as technical decisionmaking and compliance, the demand for robust numerical reasoning has grown. While recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have improved tabular reasoning, the ability to perform advanced numerical reasoning over scientific tables remains underexplored. Most prior work has focused on simple lookup or basic numerical tasks, often using small or trivial tables. In this work, we introduce SciNumQA, a benchmark dataset comprising 1,607 question–answer pairs based on 572 scientific tables, specifically designed to evaluate complex, multi-step numerical reasoning. Our benchmark systematically filters out trivial cases, ensuring that every question requires compositional reasoning and explicit numerical operations. Experiments show that even recent LLMs (e.g., GPT-4.1 mini) struggle to perform accurate reasoning on these challenging tasks, particularly when table structure becomes more complex. This highlights the need for more robust and generalizable models for scientific table understanding and advanced numerical reasoning. The SciNumQA dataset is available at this link.

title: Data-driven Convex Relaxation for Sustainable Optimal Power Flow with Feasibility Guarantees_supp1-3688521.pdf
authors: Junfei Wang
abstract: The sustainable optimal power flow (SOPF) problem is formulated by grid operators to jointly optimize cost efficiency and carbon emissions, while adhering to the operational limits and constraints of the underlying electrical infrastructure. With the proliferation of intermittent generation sources (e.g. wind and solar power) and diverse demands (e.g. electric vehicles), SOPF must be solved at high granularity to account for the variabilities introduced by these. However, due to the nonconvex nature of SOPF, solving it directly is NP-hard. In this paper, we introduce a data-driven convex relaxation framework that approximates the decision boundary separating feasible and infeasible points of the SOPF problem. This convex boundary replaces all nonlinear constraints in the formulation. This transformed problem is then solved iteratively via a gradient-based approach for which the solution is globally optimal. Then, in order to guarantee feasibility for the original problem, a simplified physics-based refinement is introduced and its global convergence properties are proven using theoretical constructs from fixed point iteration. Practical and comparative case studies on standard benchmarks, including IEEE 14-bus, IEEE 118-bus, and PEGASE 1354-bus systems, demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in terms of optimality, feasibility, and sustainability.

title: A Systematic Failure Analysis of Vision Foundation Models for Open Set Iris Presentation Attack Detection_supp1-3694899.pdf
authors: Raghavendra Ramachandra
abstract: Vision foundation models have demonstrated strong transferability across diverse visual recognition tasks and are increasingly considered for biometric applications. Their suitability for iris Presentation Attack Detection (PAD), particularly under realistic open-set operating conditions, remains insufficiently examined. This work presents a systematic failure analysis of general-purpose vision foundation models for open-set iris PAD using periocular imagery. Five representative foundation models are evaluated under three open-set protocols that explicitly separate different sources of distribution shift: (i) unseen Presentation Attack Instruments (PAIs), (ii) unseen datasets captured with different sensors, and (iii) cross-spectral transfer from near-infrared (NIR) to visible spectrum (VIS) imagery. Both frozen feature representations and parameter-efficient task adaptation using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) are assessed within a unified experimental framework. The results indicate that foundation models can transfer across datasets with similar sensing characteristics, but fail to generalise reliably to unseen attack instruments and degrade sharply under cross-spectral evaluation. While LoRA improves performance in certain cross-dataset settings, it frequently amplifies failure under attack-level and spectral shifts. Additional validation experiments using segmented iris inputs, full backbone fine-tuning, joint cross-dataset and cross-PAI shifts, and reverse VIS→NIR transfer further confirm that these failures are not simply artefacts of periocular input, weak adaptation, or one-directional spectral evaluation. These findings show that strong closed-set or cross-dataset performance should not be treated as evidence of robust open-set security, and highlight the need for PAD representations that maintain sensitivity to presentation artefacts while remaining stable under realistic deployment variation.

title: An Improved Lower Bound on Cardinality of Support of the Amplitude-Constrained AWGN Channel
authors: Haiyang Wang, Luca Barletta, Alex Dytso
abstract: We study the amplitude-constrained additive white Gaussian noise channel. It is well known that the capacity-achieving input distribution for this channel is discrete and supported on finitely many points. The best known bounds show that the support size of the capacity-achieving distribution is lower-bounded by a term of order A and upper-bounded by a term of order A2, where A denotes the amplitude constraint. It was conjectured in [2] that the linear scaling is optimal. In this work, we establish a new lower bound of order A √ log A, improving the known bound and ruling out the conjectured linear scaling. To obtain this result, we quantify the fact that the capacity-achieving output distribution is close to the uniform distribution in relative entropy. Next, we introduce a wrapping operation that maps the problem to a compact domain and develop a theory of best approximation of the uniform distribution by finite Gaussian mixtures. These approximation bounds are then combined with stability properties of capacity-achieving distributions to yield the final support-size lower bound.

title: Title Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)

title: PGD-UNet++: A Combining Direction-Awareness and Skeletal Connectivity Loss Segmentation Network for Ground Fissures Detection
authors: Sunwen Du, Xiaowei Hu, Baocheng Li, Guorui Feng, Jianmin Wang, Yanwei Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Ground fissures will damage the ecological environment and endanger infrastructure. However, due to slender shape, discontinuity and complex background, the precise detection of these data remains quite challenging. To address this issue, an efficient Deep Learning (DL) network named PGD-UNet++ is proposed to detect ground fissures in Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) imagery from the coal mining area automatically and accurately. Our novel network consists of four key components: Position Prior Generator (PPG)module, Enhanced UNet3+, Global-Local Feature Decoupling (GLFD) module, and Skeleton Connectivity Loss. PPG module captures the spatial orientation of fissures using a prior generation approach with multi-directional convolution kernels and spatial pooling. GLFD module decouples contextual information from multi-scale features, using global representations from spatial pooling and local details from hybrid deformable convolutions. Skeleton Connectivity Loss uses morphological operations to compare predicted and ground truth skeletons, explicitly preserving the topological structure of fissures. We also establish a standardized dataset of 8,564 mining-area images, featuring fissure masks annotated at an experimentally validated optimal width of 7 pixels. Experimental results show that PGD-UNet++ outperforms state-of-the-art methods on both the full dataset (F1=85.93%, mIoU=86.69%) and a mining subset (F1=88.93%, mIoU=89.51%), while remaining lightweight with only 10.53 M parameters. This research brings a new network for ground fissures detection and a high-quality dataset, advancing the tools for automated geological hazard monitoring and mining safety.

title: Movable Antenna-Assisted MIMO SWIPT: Joint Precoding and Antenna Position Optimization
authors: Zihao Huang, Nianzu Li, Peiran Wu
abstract: Movable antenna (MA) technology has recently emerged as a promising paradigm to flexibly reshape wireless channels, thereby enhancing system performance beyond the limits of traditional fixed-position antennas (FPAs). In this paper, we investigate an MA-assisted multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) network, where an access point (AP) equipped with multiple MAs serves both multi-FPA information decoding users (IDUs) and multi-FPA energy harvesting users (EHUs). Our objective is to maximize the weighted sum harvested power of all EHUs by jointly optimizing the transmit precoding matrices and the positions of MAs, subject to the constraints on the maximum transmit power of the AP, quality of service of each IDU, confined MA moving region, and minimum inter-MA distance. To tackle the non-convexity of this problem, we first transform the original optimization problem into a more tractable equivalent form by leveraging the weighted minimum mean square error (WMMSE) approach. Next, we start with the single-IDU single-EHU scenario and propose an efficient block coordinate descent (BCD) algorithm. Specifically, the transmit precoding matrix and MA positions are iteratively optimized based on the successive convex approximation (SCA) technique. The proposed algorithm is then extended to the general multi-IDU multi-EHU scenario. Finally, simulation results show that the proposed MA-assisted MIMO SWIPT system significantly outperforms conventional FPA systems and other benchmark schemes in terms of the weighted sum harvested power, validating the superiority of MAs in proactively reconfiguring channel conditions to boost energy transfer efficiency.

title: IEEE Computer Society Career Center
authors: N/A

title: Influence of Sine-Type Rotor Design on Electromagnetic Vibration Characteristics of Surface-Mounted Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machines
authors: Rupeng Zhao, Yuhan Cheng, Zhonghe Xu, Yongxiang Xu, Jibin Zou
abstract: The sinusoidal rotor field design alters the harmonic distribution of electromagnetic force waves in surface-mounted PMSMs, exerting a complex influence on electromagnetic vibration. First, the analytical expressions for electromagnetic force waves are derived theoretically, revealing the harmonic coupling mechanism and their phase relationships. Second, finite element models of both tile-shaped and sine type permanent magnet motors are established, and a comparative analysis is performed on the harmonic characteristics of the no-load back EMF and the frequency spectrum of electromagnetic force waves. Subsequently, a vibration calculation model is constructed to clarify the differential effects of the sine type design on the 0th-mode vibration and the minimum non-zero mode vibration. The study shows that the sine type design makes the electromagnetic force waves more sinusoidal, but it increases the amplitude of the second temporal harmonic of the electromagnetic force, significantly amplifies the minimum non-zero mode vibration, and makes it the dominant component of the overall motor vibration. Finally, the correctness of the theoretical analysis and simulation results is verified through experiments on four prototypes.

title: Design and Simulation Investigation of MIG and RF-Interaction Cavity of Sub-THz Tunable Gyrotron
authors: Vaidehi Meshram, Akash Akash, Prabhakar Tripathi, Rajanish Kumar Singh
abstract: A radio frequency (RF)-interaction cavity for a sub-THz gyrotron is designed and simulated to observe its beam–wave interaction behavior and operate in whispering-gallery mode TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7,2</sub> for DNP/NMR spectroscopy application. Amagnetron-injection-gun (MIG) is designed to generate an annular electron beam with a transverse velocity ratio (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha $</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of approximately ~1.11, a Larmor radius of ~0.025mm, a beam radius of around ~1.075mm, and a velocity spread of about ~2%. The particle-in-cell (PIC) simulation is performed to observe beam–wave interactions, which gives an output power of ~215W at a beam voltage of 15.5kV and a beam current of 0.1 A. The magnetic and thermal tuning schemes achieve a tunable bandwidth of ~3.4GHz, with the magnetic tuning providing ~1.5GHz and the thermal tuning providing ~1.9GHz, an enhancement of ~126.60%. The results obtained with the gyrotron indicate that it is suitable for DNP/NMR spectroscopy applications.

title: A dual-port, smartphone-linked, pocket-size fluorimeter for rapid molecular diagnostic assays at point-of-care_supp2-3693175.pdf
authors: Brian T Cunningham

title: A High-Speed Multi-Channel Magnetic Isolated Driver Circuit With Overcurrent Protection for Nanosecond Marx Generators
authors: Jinlin Gong, Kai Zhu, Rongrong Liu, Fukun Shi, Jinsong Guo, Zhenyu Ji, Xiaodong Wu, Jie Zhuang, Junfeng Rao
abstract: Solid-state pulse generators (SSPGs) require drivers with high-voltage isolation, synchronization, and electromagnetic interference (EMI) immunity. Multi-channel magnetic isolated driver circuits (MCMIDCs) are widely used in SSPGs due to the advantages of simple structure and high-voltage insulation. However, limited driving capability hinders the rapid switching of semiconductor devices, fundamentally limiting the minimum pulse width to submicrosecond range. This study proposes a high-speed MCMIDC for pulse generators to generate nanosecond pulses with fast edges and short widths. Snubber circuits incorporating metal–oxide–semiconductor field-effect transistors (MOSFETs) are employed to enhance the source current and driver synchronization. The auxiliary precharged inductor branch strengthens the sink current. A desaturation circuit employs current limiting to suppress turn-<sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</small> overvoltage, protecting MOSFETs from overcurrent/overvoltage breakdown. The operating principles are presented, and a 24-stage Marx generator prototype is developed. Simulation and experimental results show that the proposed driver circuit provides multichannel high drive currents above ±5 A and synchronization error within 4 ns. The prototype can generate 20-kV pulses with a minimum pulse width of 36 ns and a maximum current amplitude of 250 A over different resistive loads. Collectively, this proposed MCMIDC features ultrafast gate voltage transitions, high sink/source currents, and integrated overcurrent protection.

title: An 820uW Low Jitter Digital Injection-Locked PLL Using Differential Multi-Phase Injection and Self-Calibration for Biosensing Applications
authors: Zhen Li, Mingxuan Zheng, Fan Yang, Xinyuan Zhang, Jialuo Huang, Xiaodong Zhao, Yuanyuan Cui, Xunying Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Circuits and Systems
abstract: This paper presents a highly energy-efficient, low-jitter digital injection-locked phase-locked loop (ILPLL) tailored for the 2.4 GHz industrial scientific medical band. The proposed digital injection-locking architecture overcomes the limitations of conventional analog approaches regarding power, area, and robustness, and its incorporated differential multi-phase injection technique simultaneously suppresses jitter and further reduces power consumption. The misaligned injection pulse timing and non-optimized injection pulsewidth are digitally calibrated in background to maintain low power and low jitter across process, voltage, and temperature (PVT) variations. Fabricated in a 28-nm CMOS process, the proposed digital ILPLL achieves 542 fs RMS jitter with -65.8 dBc spur at 2.4 GHz working frequency. It consumes 820 ¼W power from a 0.6 V supply voltage, demonstrating the state-of-the-art energy efficiency along with a competitive jitter. In vitro experiment is conducted in a hospital for clinical trials, where the proposed digital ILPLL integrated into an SoC provides a local oscillator signal for the radio frequency front-end, enabling the system to wirelessly transmit electrocardiogram data in a battery-free manner.

title: The Synergistic Electro-Thermal-Electrochemical Mechanism of Water-blocking Buffer Layer Ablation in High-Voltage Cable
authors: Mengyao Zhu, Jianwei Gao, Yang Guo, Zhuobin Xi, Huan Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Ablation failure of the water-blocking buffer layer (BFL) in high-voltage cross-linked polyethylene (XLPE) cables remains a longstanding challenge. However, a comprehensive understanding of the key factors and critical conditions governing its occurrence is lacking, which hinders accurate condition assessment and increases failure risk. This study proposes a quantitative electro-thermal-electrochemical coupling framework to elucidate the multiphysics ablation mechanism. Through 3D asymmetric finite element simulation and constant-current ablation tests, we demonstrate that both incomplete contact and moisture ingress are necessary conditions for ablation initiation. Under incomplete contact between the corrugated aluminum sheath and BFL, electric field concentration develops in interfacial voids, leading to partial discharge when the moisture content of BFL reaches or exceeds 5%. Thermal ablation initiates at the moisture content of 25% or higher, when localized temperature in sufficiently contacted regions surpasses the pyrolysis threshold of polyester fibers. The ablation failure evolves through a synergistic process: electrochemical corrosion generates high-resistance white powder, which distorts the electric field and accelerates heating, leading to partial discharge and resultant partial polyester fiber fusing. The accumulation of corrosion products and fiber breakdown mutually reinforce, intensifying current concentration and forming a positive feedback loop that culminates in irreversible thermal breakdown.

title: Can Meter-scale Optical Imagery Enable Species-Level Vegetation Monitoring in Hydrologically Dynamic Riparian Zones?
authors: Xu Zhou, Shengjun Wu, Yutao Gao, Zhaofei Wen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Monitoring species-level vegetation dynamics within mountainous riparian zones presents significant challenges due to pronounced hydrological variability, intricate terrain, and the presence of narrow, fragmented vegetation corridors. These spatial configurations intensify mixed-pixel effects, giving rise to the common view that species-level monitoring in such complex riparian environments necessitates the use of sub-meter or ultra-high-resolution remote sensing imagery. However, the high costs and limited routine availability of such data constrain their application for temporally continuous monitoring under dynamic hydrological conditions. This study investigates the feasibility of using meter-scale optical satellite imagery for species-level vegetation monitoring in narrow and hydrologically dynamic mountainous riparian zones. By employing PlanetScope imagery as a representative source of meter-scale, high-temporal-resolution data, we investigate the feasibility of mapping dominant species coverage (CDS) through the integration of multiple machine learning models. To address the pronounced temporal variability characteristic of riparian environments, this study further investigates the efficacy of a multi-temporal integration (MTI) strategy in stabilizing species-level vegetation signals under fluctuating hydrological conditions. The findings indicate that CDS estimation based on single-date imagery is highly susceptible to the timing of image acquisition, with prediction performance showing considerable variation across acquisition dates (R² ranging from approximately 0.53 to 0.90). This variability reflects the combined effects of meteorological conditions, water-level fluctuations, and vegetation phenology on image quality and background interference. By integrating predictions from multiple acquisition dates, the MTI strategy markedly enhanced both prediction accuracy and temporal consistency. For the best-performing model, MTI increased the R² to approximately 0.91 and reduced the RMSE to about 0.09, while models with initially lower performance demonstrated greater relative improvements following integration. Overall, the results demonstrate that meter-scale satellite imagery, when integrated with frequent temporal sampling, can yield reliable species-level data in narrow and heterogeneous riparian environments, which have traditionally been thought to necessitate sub-meter resolution. This underscores the practical applicability of meter-scale data for ecological monitoring in complex riparian systems.

title: Structural Feature Modulation for Day–Night Cross-Domain Object Re-Identification
authors: Simin Zhan, Yifan Shi, Pudu Liu, Liu Liu, Jianqing Zhu, Huanqiang Zeng, Zhen Lei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Day-night cross-domain object re-identification presents significant challenges due to severe illumination-induced domain gaps. Unlike conventional attention mechanisms that suffer from limited dimensional coverage and rely on single-type normalization strategies, we propose a structural feature modulation (SFM) approach that operates from a modulation perspective. Our SFM approach incorporates a gated batch-layer normalization strategy within the modulation architecture, resulting in the construction of a gated normalization-based modulation (GNM) module. This module effectively leverages the complementary advantages of both batch normalization and layer normalization to balance intra-domain discriminability and cross-domain generalization. Furthermore, we develop a multi-granularity modulation (MGM) module that recalibrates features across both edge-granularity and area-granularity pathways, enabling comprehensive structural modulation. Extensive experiments on the DN-348 and LLCM benchmark datasets demonstrate that our SFM approach consistently outperforms state-of-the-art approaches, achieving notable improvements including a 2.51% mAP increase on the DN-348 dataset. Our codes are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/SimiiZ/SFM</uri>

title: RadarSim: Simulating Single-Chip Radar Via Multimodal Neural Fields
authors: Chuhan Chen, Tianshu Huang, Akarsh Prabhakara, Chaithanya Kumar Mummadi, Zhongxiao Cong, Anthony Rowe, Matthew O'Toole, Deva Ramanan
abstract: Radars are an ideal complement to cameras: both are inexpensive, solid-state sensors, with cameras offering fine angular resolution, while radars provide metric depth and robustness under adverse weather. However, radar data is more difficult to interpret than camera images and varies significantly between sensors, necessitating increased reliance on simulation for prototyping sensors and processing pipelines. Recent work treating radar reconstruction as a novel view synthesis problem has shown great promise in reconstructing radar-relevant geometry and simulating lowlevel radar data. However, such methods are constrained by the low spatial resolution of the underlying radar. To address this, we propose a unified differentiable renderer, RadarSim, which leverages the high angular resolution of RGB cameras to generate Doppler radar range images from a camera-initialized neural field. Using a novel data set of calibrated radar camera recordings from a custom handheld rig, we demonstrate that RadarSim produces sharper geometry and Doppler range frames than radar-only reconstructions.

title: L4P: Towards Unified Low-Level 4D Vision Perception
authors: Abhishek Badki, Hang Su, Bowen Wen, Orazio Gallo
abstract: The spatio-temporal relationship between the pixels of a video carries critical information for low-level 4D perception tasks. A single model that reasons about it should be able to solve several such tasks well. Yet, most state-of-theart methods rely on architectures specialized for the task at hand. We present L4P, a feedforward, general-purpose architecture that solves low-level 4D perception tasks in a unified framework. LAP leverages a pre-trained ViT-based video encoder and combines it with per-task heads that are lightweight and therefore do not require extensive training. Despite its general and feedforward formulation, our method is competitive with existing specialized methods on both dense tasks, such as depth or optical flow estimation, and sparse tasks, such as 2D/3D tracking. Moreover, it solves all tasks at once in a time comparable to that of single-task methods.

title: An Industry 4.0-Based System for Automated Calibration of Multi-Channel Force Sensors in CNC Grinding Machines
authors: Mohamad Wael Kheshfeh, Tobias Tute, Sebastian Linke, Rüdiger Mecke
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: Multi-channel force sensor calibration in CNC precision grinding traditionally requires manual, expert-driven procedures susceptible to human error and inefficiency. This paper presents a fully automated calibration framework that eliminates manual intervention throughout the entire workflow-from initiating measurement sequences via OPC UA, through autonomous coefficient computation using specialized AAS submodels, to automated deployment of validated correction matrices into production systems with ISO-compliant documentation gener-ation- requiring human oversight only for final approval. The framework integrates OPC UA for SINUMERIK 840D sl controller connectivity, Asset Administration Shell (AAS) for semantic data modeling, and RevolutionPi Core 3+ edge computing for distributed processing. Three algorithmic approaches were comparatively evaluated across <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5}$</tex> multi-channel force measurements <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(100-1500 \mathrm{N})$</tex>: manual spreadsheet analysis (baseline), Multiple Linear Regression (MLR), and Inverse Matrix (IM) decomposition. While IM achieved marginally higher accuracy (1.5 % improvement), MLR was selected for deployment owing to its optimal balance of accuracy (23 % error reduction vs. manual baseline), computational efficiency (2760 -fold speedup), and industrial practicality. System validation showed matrix condition numbers below 50 and OPC UA latency stable at 100 ms, confirming robustness and scalability for autonomous metrological infrastructure in Industry 4.0.

title: Minimal Solvers for Full DoF Motion Estimation from Asynchronous Tracks
authors: Petr Hruby, Marc Pollefeys
abstract: We address the problem of estimating both translational and angular velocity of a camera from asynchronous point tracks, a formulation relevant to rolling shutter and event cameras. Since the original problem is non-polynomial, we propose a polynomial approximation, classify the resulting minimal problems, and determine their algebraic degrees. Furthermore, we develop minimal solvers for several problems with low degrees and evaluate them on synthetic and real datasets. The code is publicly available.

title: Contact4D: A Video Dataset for Whole-Body Human Motion and Finger Contact in Dexterous Operations
authors: Jyun-Ting Song, JungEun Kim, Jinkun Cao, Yu Lei, Takuma Yagi, Kris Kitani
abstract: Understanding how humans interact with objects is key to building robust human-centric artificial intelligence. However, this area remains relatively unexplored due to the lack of large-scale datasets. Recent datasets focusing on this issue mainly consist of activities captured entirely in controlled lab environments, and contact annotations are mostly estimated using threshold clips. We introduce Contact4D, a multi-view video dataset for humanobject interaction that provides detailed body poses and accurate contact annotations. We use a flexible multiview capture system to record individuals performing furniture assembly tasks and provide annotations for human detection, tracking, 2D/3D pose estimation, and groundtruth contact. Additionally, we propose a novel processing pipeline to extract accurate hand poses even when they are severely occluded. Contact4D consists of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 M$</tex> images captured from 19 synchronized cameras across 350 video sequences, spanning diverse environments, varioius furniture types, and unique subjects. We evaluate existing methods for human pose estimation and human-centric contact estimation, demonstrating their inability to generalize to our dataset. Lastly, we fine-tune a pretrained MultiHMR model on Contact4D and observe an improved performance of 56.6% body MPJPE and 26.4% hand MPJPE in scenarios under severe self-occlusion and object occlusion. Code and data are available at https://jyuntins.github.io/Contact4D.

title: TwoSquared: 4D Generation from 2D Image Pairs
authors: Lu Sang, Zehranaz Canfes, Dongliang Cao, Riccardo Marin, Florian Bernard, Daniel Cremers
abstract: Recovering a 4D motion from sparse visual information (such as two temporal frames of a subject) is a significant challenge. While humans are able to hallucinate the missing information in a plausible way, generative AI struggles due to a lack of high-quality training data and heavy computing requirements. To overcome these limitations, we propose TwoSquared, a method that obtains a 4D plausible sequence from just two 2D RGB images corresponding to the beginning and the end of the action. We propose to solve the problem in two steps: 1) first, obtaining a 3D reconstruction of the initial and final status, and 2) model the intermediate sequence as a physically plausible deformation. Our method does not require templates or class-specific prior knowledge, and can operate with arbitrary in-the-wild examples. We demonstrate our capabilities in a number of different objects, diverse in terms of nature, class, and deformation, surpassing video-based alternatives, which cannot achieve the same level of consistency.

title: Improving 3D Foot Motion Reconstruction in Markerless Monocular Human Motion Capture
authors: Tom Wehrbein, Bodo Rosenhahn
abstract: State-of-the-art methods can recover accurate overall 3D human body motion from in-the-wild videos. However, they often fail to capture fine-grained articulations, especially in the feet, which are critical for applications such as gait analysis and animation. This limitation results from training datasets with inaccurate foot annotations and limited foot motion diversity. We address this gap with FootMR, a Foot Motion Refinement method that refines foot motion estimated by an existing human recovery model through lifting 2D foot keypoint sequences to 3D. By avoiding direct image input, FootMR circumvents inaccurate image-3D annotation pairs and can instead leverage large-scale motion capture data. To resolve ambiguities of 2D-to-3D lifting, FootMR incorporates knee and foot motion as context and predicts only residual foot motion. Generalization to extreme foot poses is further improved by representing joints in global rather than parent-relative rotations and applying extensive data augmentation. To support evaluation of foot motion reconstruction, we introduce MOOF, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2D$</tex> dataset of complex foot movements. Experiments on MOOF, MOYO, and RICH show that FootMR outperforms state-of-the-art methods, reducing ankle joint angle error on MOYO by up to 30 % over the best video-based approach. Our code and dataset are available for research purposes at twehrbein.github.io/footmr-website/.

title: RosLightNet: A Lightweight DCNN Framework for Automated Rose Leaf Disease Classification with Interpretable Visual Explanations
authors: Montakir Emtiaz Ahmed Rafid, Sanzida Islam Smriti, Navid Tasbir Shuvo, Foujia Afrose Tanha, Tanvir Parvez Prottoy, Most. Tanjila Ahmmed Meem
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Foliar diseases in roses (Rosa spp.) may include black spot, insect-induced damages, and mosaic virus which have an adverse impact on the plant vigor, flower quality as well as commercial yield. In order to aid in early and confident disease identification in resource-restricted farming environments, this paper presents a proposal of RosLightNet that is an ultra- lightweight Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) that is tuned to a real-time rose leaf disease detection application. The model combines depthwise separable convolutions, residual learning, and a small squeeze-and-excitation attention, which meets a high level of discriminative power with a small footprint of less than 95 K parameters. RosLightNet, with 5-fold stratified evaluation protocol and using RoseLeafInsight dataset, achieves an accuracy of 99.33, mean AUC of 0.9999 and an F1-score of 0.993, surpassing the accuracy of larger architectures like VGG16, DenseNet121, ResNet152V2, Xception and MobileNetV2. Explainable AI methods, such as LIME and Grad-CAMs, affirm that the model pays appropriate attention to disease-specific lesion appearances like, necrotic textures, discoloration, and structural distortions. Nonetheless, the great level of accuracy is partly due to the controlled conditions of acquisition of the data and the lack of external validation, which could be an obstacle to extrapolation to field settings. Irrespective of these limitations, RosLightNet is an attractive platform in terms of accuracy, efficiency, and interpretability that makes it a viable platform to implement on mobile and edge computers in order to facilitate real-time and accessible rose disease detection in precision agriculture.

title: Robust and Explainable Facial Expression Recognition Using YOLOv10 Under Real-World Visual Degradations
authors: Firoze Maliha, Gazi Tahsina Sharmin Jahin, Tasnia Fahrin Choity, Umme Habiba, M. Jamshed Alam Patwary
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Facial Expression Recognition (FER) is essential in affective computing though existing deep learning models struggle under real-world distortions such as occlusion, blur, and noise. This study presents a unified robust-ness-explainability-calibration framework built on YOLOv10 model that achieves Precision 0.90, Recall 0.89, mAP@50 0.93, and mAP @ 50-950.77, outperforming YOLOv8 and Faster RCNN, evaluated on a balanced AffectNet subset. A dual-stream preprocessing design combining clean and perturbed inputs enhances robustness and stability. For interpretability, RISE, SHAP-CAM, and sliding-patch occlusion visualizations, along with saliency divergence metrics, quantify decision transparency. Temperature scaling and Risk-Coverage evaluation further improve calibration reliability. The proposed model establishes a state-of-the-art, interpretable, and trustworthy FER framework, resilient to visual uncertainty and suitable for real-world affective systems.

title: The Optimization of Violence Detection and Classification with Vision Transformer and ResNet18 Hybrid Model
authors: Naimur Rahman Tusher, Mahedi Hasan, Mazharul Islam, Jakaria Habib, Jarin Tasnim, Touhedour Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Violence in Bangladesh, including street clashes, mob attacks, and weaponized crimes, is a real threat to public safety in heavily populated areas. Real-time detection of such events is still a challenge for most conventional surveillance systems, and thus, there is strong demand for smart automated solutions. This study proposed a deep learning based violence detection and classification framework that integrates a Hybrid Vision Transformer (ViT) and Residual Network (ResNet18) approach. To validate its robustness and effectiveness, the proposed model is benchmarked against standalone ViT and ResNet18 architectures. A custom dataset was developed comprising 2,140 images, which includes 750 non-violent, 730 violent, and 660 weaponized violence images, collected from Kaggle's Real-Life Violence Situations dataset, and augmented with recent Bangladeshi incidents by CCTV footage and social media sources. These models were trained and tested on this dataset to be practical for the Bangladeshi context. Experimental results show that the proposed hybrid model outperforms its standalone counterparts, with ViT achieving 98.36% accuracy and ResNet18 achieving 97.66%, while the Hybrid ViT-ResNet18 model attains 98.60% accuracy along with superior sensitivity, F1-score, and AUC scores. These results indicate the potential of hybrid Transformer and CNN-based architectures to build reliable and context-aware surveillance systems that could enhance violence mitigation and public security in Bangladesh.

title: Enhancement of Perovskite Photovoltaic Cell Performance with Various Kinds of Layer Configuration Using Oghmanano Software
authors: Rubayat Chowdhury
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Renewable energy technology is one of the most advanced fields of research in the world. A solar photovoltaic system is a major part of renewable energy. Perovskite photovoltaic cells are increasingly favored for their exceptional efficiency and performance. The perovskite and top electrode layers greatly influence the overall efficiency of a basic perovskite-based photovoltaic cell. This research examines the performance of a perovskite photovoltaic cell model using various layer configurations. Out of 14 various perovskite material thicknesses, power conversion efficiency for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$24 \times 10^{-7} ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> thick perovskite material was found to be 21.83% using a standard perovskite solar cell setup. For the top electrode layer, ten different kinds of materials were employed. The usage of lithium as the top electrode and absorber layer of perovskite <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$24 \times 10^{-7} ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> has demonstrated the highest efficiency of 32.08% out of ten varieties of top electrode layer materials.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning and Deep Learning Framework for Bangla Hate Speech Detection
authors: Sumaiya Afroze, Muhammad Nazrul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Detecting Bangla hate speech on social media poses significant challenges due to overlapping abusive expressions, frequent code-mixing, and the inherent linguistic complexity of the language. Therefore, the objective of this research is to develop an effective hybrid model for multi-label hate speech detection and to evaluate its performance against baseline deep learning models. To achieve this, we prepared a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 5, 0 0 1}$</tex> entries, annotated with a primary binary classification of Hate and No Hate, where the Hate class is further divided into five fine-grained categories: Insult, Violence, Sexual, Racism, and Religious. We explored several deep learning and hybrid models and performed a detailed performance comparison. The proposed hybrid model achieved the best results, consistently outperforming the deep learning models. Experimental results show that it delivers higher accuracy and robustness in identifying multiple hate categories simultaneously, with a label-based accuracy of 95 %, Hamming loss of 0.05, and micro and weighted <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> scores of 0.85. As such, this study contributes by introducing a hybrid model and demonstrating its effectiveness in advancing Bangla hate speech detection on social media.

title: Machine Learning-Optimized Rectenna Design for Battery-Free Biocompatible Implants
authors: Raisul Ismail Apon, Zarin Nushiba, Md. Azad Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: The integration of radio frequency (RF)-based wireless power transfer (WPT) systems into implantable medical devices (IMDs) represents a critical step toward battery-free, autonomous biomedical implants. This study presents the design, simulation, and optimization of a miniaturized rectenna operating at the 433 MHz ISM band, addressing challenges related to power delivery, biocompatibility, and electromagnetic safety. A multi-layer human tissue phantom, incorporating realistic dielectric parameters, was developed to evaluate antenna performance and ensure compliance with specific absorption rate (SAR) guidelines. The antenna was optimized for compact geometry, low return loss, and high energy harvesting efficiency under physiological conditions. Simultaneously, a rectifier circuit was designed in ADS using harmonic balance analysis and tested with various diode models to maximize RF-to-DC conversion. The proposed system achieved a peak efficiency of 84.816%, with SAR levels remaining within safety limits across multiple implantation scenarios. Additionally, ensemble machine learning models were used to predict SAR, return loss (S11), and conversion efficiency. The Stacking Regressor achieved the highest accuracy with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> values of 0.9915 (SAR), 0.9763 (S11), and 0.9891 (efficiency), demonstrating the robustness of the predictive framework. These findings confirm the potential of the proposed rectenna system to enable safe and efficient wireless powering of future IMDs, supporting the advancement of implantable electronics and biomedical telemetry.

title: Energy-Efficient IoT Localization with Uncertainty-Aware EKF and Hybrid Markov-CNN-PCA Models
authors: Maher Jabberi, Bassem Sellami, Akram Hakiri, Abderrazak JEMAI
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Accurate and energy-efficient localization remains a fundamental challenge in large-scale IoT networks, where resource constraints and dynamic signal conditions limit the effectiveness of conventional approaches. Purely stochastic models often fail to capture complex spatial signal patterns, while deep learning models alone typically ignore temporal structure and incur high computational cost. We propose a hybrid localization framework that unifies stochastic temporal modeling with deep neural feature extraction. Specifically, we introduce Markov-CNN, which integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for spatial feature learning with Markov-based temporal state modeling, and Markov-CNN-PCA, which further incorporates principal component analysis (PCA) to reduce feature dimensionality and computational overhead. The architecture enables structured temporal smoothing over learned embeddings while preserving scalability for resource-constrained IoT deployments. We conduct extensive simulations comparing our models against standalone Markov and CNN baselines across localization accuracy, energy consumption, and end-to-end latency. Results demonstrate that hybridization yields consistent and substantial improvements across all metrics. In particular, Markov-CNN-PCA achieves the best performance-efficiency trade-off, delivering superior localization accuracy while reducing energy usage and inference latency, thereby enabling real-time deployment.

title: Adaptive Hyperheuristic Framework for Dynamic Network Defense Optimization
authors: Kassem Danach, Samir Haddad, Jomana Al Haj Hassan, Jinane Sayah, Merhej Joseph, Kallab Chadi
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: Network defense optimization in dynamic and adversarial environments poses significant challenges to traditional metaheuristic approaches due to their limited adaptability to non-stationary conditions. This paper proposes an Adaptive Hyperheuristic (AHH) framework that integrates reinforcement learning for intelligent heuristic selection across constructive, improvement, perturbation, and reconstructive operators, combined with adaptive acceptance criteria including Simulated Annealing, Great Deluge, and Late Acceptance. The problem is formulated as a multi-objective optimization model minimizing attack surface, response time, and operational cost while maximizing network resilience. Extensive numerical experiments conducted on simulated network topologies (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 - 5 0 0}$</tex> nodes) under multiple attack scenarios demonstrate that AHH achieves superior performance, improving hypervolume by up to 12.5 %, reducing IGD by 38 %, and decreasing convergence time by approximately 15 % compared to Genetic Algorithm and Scatter Search baselines. Furthermore, the proposed method reduces attack success rate by 40 % and improves network resilience by over 13 %, while maintaining low variance across 30 independent runs. These results confirm the robustness, efficiency, and practical applicability of the proposed framework for real-world network defense optimization.

title: 3D-GENERALIST: Vision-Language-Action Models for Crafting 3D Worlds
authors: Fan-Yun Sun, Shengguang Wu, Christian Jacobsen, Thomas Yim, Haoming Zou, Alex Zook, Shangru Li, Yu-Hsin Chou, Ethem Can, Xunlei Wu, Clemens Eppner, Valts Blukis, Jonathan Tremblay, Jiajun Wu, Stan Birchfield, Nick Haber
abstract: Creating 3D graphics content for immersive and interactive worlds remains labor-intensive, limiting our ability to create large-scale synthetic data for training foundation models. Recent methods aim to alleviate this, but they often focus on a single aspect (e.g., layout) and do not improve generation quality by simply scaling computational resources. We recast 3D environment generation as a sequential decision-making problem, using Vision-Language Models (VLMs) as policies that output actions to jointly craft a 3D environment's layout, materials, lighting, and assets. Our framework, 3D-Generalist, trains VLMs to generate more prompt-aligned 3D environments via self-improvement fine-tuning. We demonstrate the effectiveness of 3D-Generalist and our training strategy in generating simulation-ready 3D environments. We also demonstrate its quality and scalability for synthetic data generation by pretraining a vision foundation model on the generated data. After fine-tuning on downstream tasks, we show that it surpasses models pre-trained on meticulously human-crafted synthetic data and approaches results achieved when training with orders of magnitude larger real data.

title: Enhancing Accessibility in Retail Banking: A Voice-Enabled RAG Framework with Performance Evaluation
authors: Mahbuba Tabassum, Tasnima Hamid, Sadat Hasnat Sabbir, Alina Akter, Mst. Arifa Azmary, Md. Ashiqussalehin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Conversational AI has emerged as a transformative tool in retail banking, yet traditional text-based systems often fail to accommodate diverse user needs, particularly in spoken interactions. This paper introduces a voice-enabled Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework designed to enhance accessibility and inclusivity in retail banking services. Integrating Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR), semantic embedding with SentenceTransformers and FAISS indexing, Large Language Model (LLM)-based response generation via Cerebras, and Text-to-Speech (TTS) synthesis, the proposed modular pipeline leverages the Bitext Retail Banking LLM Chatbot Training Dataset to ensure domain-specific, compliant, and contextually accurate responses. A comparative evaluation across six configurations—varying embedding models, vector databases, and augmentation frameworks—assessed performance on subsets of 10,30, and 50 samples using metrics such as intent accuracy, entity F1-score, ROUGE, BLEU, relevance, latency, and robustness. Results demonstrate that the SentenceTransformer+FAISS+LangChain configuration achieves superior performance, with intent accuracy up to 0.89, ROUGE-1 scores of 0.91, and latency below 0.71 seconds on larger datasets, outperforming baselines in semantic fidelity and multi-turn dialogue coherence. This framework advances voice-driven banking assistants by mitigating hallucinations, optimizing real-time interaction, and promoting regulatory compliance, thereby fostering greater user trust and operational efficiency in financial services.

title: A Lightweight Ensemble Model for IoT Intrusion Detection on Resource-Constrained Edge Devices
authors: Radiul Walee, Ashim Dey, Annesha Das
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Network intrusion detection in IoT environments is challenging due to cyberattack complexity and edge device limitations. This study proposes an improved machine learning framework using feature optimization and ensemble learning on the RT-IoT2022 dataset. The method employs correlation-based feature selection to identify attack-correlated features—forward packet payload statistics, flow FIN flag counts, and forward URG flag counts. The Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) addresses class imbalance in network traffic data during training. The framework evaluates multiple algorithms: XGBoost, Cat-Boost, Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbors, Naive Bayes, Gradient Boosting, Decision Tree, and LightGBM. LightGBM achieves 99.87% accuracy on the full feature set. XGBoost reaches 97.51% accuracy using only the top 10 features—a 75% reduction in dimensionality. The feature-reduced XGBoost model maintains high precision (0.85), recall (0.83), and F1-score (0.83) across 12 attack categories, making it ideal for resource-constrained IoT deployment. Payload-related features show the strongest correlation with attack detection, offering insights for IoT security feature engineering. The lightweight approach delivers computational efficiency and robust detection, enabling practical deployment in edge computing scenarios with limited resources.

title: Capturing Dialectal Variation in Code-Mixed Banglish through Multi-Task Transformers
authors: Hasin Almas Sifat, Koushik Biswas Arko, Abida Afrin, K. M. Tahsin Kabir, Abdullah Rakib Akand, Md. Mortuza Ahmmed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Code-mixed languages, especially Banglish (a mix of Bengali and English), are extremely difficult for natural language processing (NLP) due to various syntactic irregularities, lexical borrowing, and regional variation. Current methods generally only address isolated tasks like translation or region classification and frequently do not incorporate features of regional speech that impact model capabilities. In this work, we present a RegionAware Multi-Task Transformer that jointly performs region classification and translation quality prediction on BanglishEnglish parallel data. The model combines separate Banglish and English BERT encoders, pooling via attention, and cross-attention fusion to represent both intra-lingual and cross-lingual contextual dependencies. Our model achieves 83 % accuracy and a macro F1 score of 0.84 for region classification, while the translation quality prediction task achieves a Pearson correlation of 0.78. Both models significantly outperform traditional baselines based on machine learning (TF-IDF + Logistic Regression) and neural sequence models (BiLSTM). The results illustrate that regionaware multi-task learning improves representation learning and enhances generalization through the region's dialectal variation in Banglish. The research presented in this paper takes one step closer towards building contextualized and robust NLP systems for low-resource, code-mixed languages.

title: A Machine Learning and Bioinformatics Approach for Identifying Key Biomarkers in Bipolar Disorder and Schizophrenia
authors: Mst. Mostary Khatun, Tanvir Hasan, Mst. Amina Khatun, Md. Faruk Hosen, Monir Morshed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Bipolar disorder (BD) and schizophrenia (SCZ) are serious psychiatric conditions that share similar clinical characteristics and genetic predispositions. Nevertheless, the fundamental molecular mechanisms linking these disorders have not been fully explored. This research seeks to clarify the common molecular pathways and potential therapeutic targets in BD and SCZ by employing an integrated bioinformatics and machine learning methodology. Gene expression datasets were sourced from public databases, and an analysis of differential gene expression was performed using the Limma package. A feature selection method based on filtering, specifically Fisher Score, was utilized to pinpoint the most significant genes common to both disorders. We detected 30 shared differentially expressed genes (DEGs) in BD and SCZ. Functional enrichment analysis through Gene Ontology (GO) demonstrated a significant connection to mitochondrial activity and cellular respiration. KEGG pathway analysis additionally indicated links to oxidative phosphorylation and pathways associated with neurodegenerative diseases. The development and clustering of the protein-protein interaction (PPI) network revealed five pivotal hub genes-NDUFS2, NDUFB2, UQCRQ, COX7C, and NDUFA13-all of which are integral components of the mitochondrial respiratory chain, implying a shared metabolic dysfunction in both conditions. Furthermore, regulatory network analysis revealed complex interactions involving transcription factors (TFs), microRNAs (miRNAs), and the common DEGs, highlighting intricate gene regulatory mechanisms. Predictive drug analysis pinpointed potential therapeutic agents aimed at these common molecular signatures. In summary, our results offer new insights into the shared biological foundations of BD and SCZ, providing encouraging pathways for future diagnostic and therapeutic approaches.

title: Hyperparameter Tuning for GNN-Based Graph Coloring: A Case Study on PI-SAGE
authors: Alisha Khan, Israth Jahan Eshika, Ahsanur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have recently emerged as promising tools for solving NP-hard graph coloring problems. However, their effectiveness is often sensitive to the selection of key hyperparameters, particularly the number of colors, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$q$</tex>, and the learning rate. These hyperparameters are typically chosen arbitrarily, with limited understanding of their impact on the performance of graph coloring models. This paper presents an in-depth analysis of these two hyperparameters within the context of PI-SAGE, a state-of-the-art GNN-based graph coloring model. Two novel algorithms, Optim- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$q$</tex> and Optim-LR, are introduced to efficiently identify optimal values for <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$q$</tex> and the learning rate. These algorithms do not require prior knowledge of the graph's chromatic number or its bounds, making them more widely applicable. The methods are designed to minimize color conflicts while maintaining computational efficiency. Extensive experiments on benchmark graphs demonstrate that Optim- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$q$</tex> and Optim-LR consistently generate near-optimal colorings, with minimal color conflicts, outperforming a strong binary-searchbased baseline in both accuracy and runtime. We have also compared our method with the Randomized search and Optuna tuning methods, where our method, Optim-q, has yielded the lowest number of colors, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$q$</tex>, while keeping conflicts number 0, which is not ensured in our other competitors. The findings offer a practical and efficient framework for hyperparameter tuning in GNN-based graph coloring models, making them more reliable and robust for real-world applications.

title: A Certification Scheme for Large Language Models-Based Applications
authors: Nicola Bena, Marco Anisetti, Ernesto Damiani, Alex Della Bruna, Chan Yeob Yeun, Claudio A. Ardagna
publish: ACM Transactions on Intelligent Systems and Technology
abstract: None

title: Front Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)

title: A Highly Efficient Zero-Shot Image Denoiser
authors: Ke Gu, Hongyan Liu, Yubin Gao, Kunpeng Mai, Kai Chen, Shu Jin, Guangtao Zhai
abstract: Currently, there is an increasingly high demand for high-quality images across various streaming media applications, but visual signals are impaired by composite noise. The existing dataset-based image denoisers, requiring a huge number of clean-noisy image pairs from diverse scenes to well train a large-size deep network, often perform unwell in the real-world noisy image restoration due to the complexity and variability of scenes, limited computing resources and the difficulty in obtaining clean images. So this paper tries to propose a dataset-free highly efficient zero-shot image denoiser with a lightweight CNN, called HEZID, to achieve the effective real-time real-world noisy image restoration. First, for the problem of the BSN strategy that discards the center pixel to avoid identity mapping for image denoising, we propose a new solution by devising a dual-mode pixel-realignment strategy, which sequentially develops a ‘rough’ pixel-realignment method for smooth regions and a ‘fine’ pixel-realignment method for edge regions, therefore fully exploiting the center pixel and preventing identity mapping. Second, we develop a super-lightweight CNN-based denoiser based on the strip convolution, which is motivated by the high sensitivity of human eyes to horizontal and vertical stimuli for visual inputs, followed by introducing the residual learning and separable convolutions to severally facilitate feature propagation and reduce the computational burden. Experiments on multiple real-world noisy image datasets confirmed that our HEZID outperformed a dozen state-of-the-art peers in terms of restoration quality and running efficiency.

title: IEEE Tech RXIV filler
authors: N/A

title: Covering radius of generalized Zetterberg codes of even characteristic
authors: Minjia Shi, Tor Helleseth, Ferruh Özbudak
abstract: For integers u ≥ 2 and s ≥ 1, let q0 = 2u, and let Cs(q0) be the generalized Zetterberg code of length n = qs 0 + 1 over the finite field Fq0 of characteristic 2. For odd characteristic, the covering radius of Cs(q0) was determined recently, whereas the case of even characteristic remained open. In this paper, we determine the covering radius of generalized Zetterberg codes over finite fields of characteristic 2, thereby solving this open problem. Our approach uses methods from the theory of algebraic curves over finite fields. As an application, we obtain an infinite family of quasi-perfect codes.

title: Multi-level Collaborative Distillation Meets Global Workspace Model: A Unified Framework for OCIL
authors: Shibin Su, Guoqiang Liang, De Cheng, Shizhou Zhang, Lingyan Ran
abstract: Online Class-Incremental Learning (OCIL) enables models to learn continuously from non-i.i.d. data streams. Since samples of the data streams can be seen only once, it is more suitable for real-world scenarios compared to offline learning. However, this constraint intensifies the challenge for OCIL in maintaining an appropriate balance between stability and plasticity. Moreover, under stricter memory buffer constraints in real world, current replay-based methods are less effective. While ensemble methods improve plasticity, they often struggle with stability. Inspired by the Global Workspace Theory (GWT), we propose a novel approach that enhances ensemble learning through a Global Workspace Model (GWM)—a shared, implicit memory that guides the learning of multiple student models. The GWM is formed by fusing the parameters of all students within each training batch, capturing the historical learning trajectory and serving as a dynamic anchor for knowledge consolidation. Like the broadcasting mechanism of GWT, the GWM is redistributed periodically to students, stabilizing learning and promoting cross-task consistency. In addition, we introduce a multi-level collaborative distillation mechanism. It enforces peer-to-peer consistency among students and preserves historical knowledge by aligning each student with the GWM. As a result, student models remain adaptable to new tasks while maintaining previously learned knowledge, striking a better balance between stability and plasticity. Extensive experiments on three standard OCIL benchmarks show that our method delivers significant performance improvement for several OCIL models across various memory budgets. The code is available at https://github.com/susususushi/GWM.

title: Near-Optimal Control of Linear Time-Delayed Systems Using Truncated State History
authors: Mainak Pal, Mostafa M. Shibl, Vijay Gupta
