abstract: Optimal linear quadratic regulator (LQR) synthesis for time-delayed systems yields controllers requiring full access to the delay history. For systems with large delays, this imposes significant storage and communication overhead. We investigate the performance of finite-memory suboptimal controllers utilizing truncated state feedback. Under a mild stabilizability assumption, we prove that the suboptimality gap in terms of the cost difference between the optimal and truncated controllers decays exponentially with the truncation horizon. Crucially, our results do not require strong decay properties on the system matrices.

title: Analysis and Performance Limits of Si/SiGe Bipolar Transistor Collector Profiles at Reduced Temperatures
authors: Justin P. Heimerl, Jeffrey W. Teng, John D. Cressler
abstract: We investigate silicon-germanium heterojunction bipolar transistor (SiGe HBT) collector region impact ionization and transit time performance limits at 300 and 100 K using a commercially available Boltzmann transport equation (BTE) solver based on the Monte Carlo (MC) method. We demonstrate that the carrier energies computed by the solver can be used to accurately predict measured device multiplication factors (M-1) at both temperatures using the simple Okuto-Crowell model. We then use computed electron velocity and energy profiles to automatically discover a Pareto front using Bayesian optimization techniques. Finally, we investigate collector profiles along the Pareto front to provide insight into optimal collector profile design for both 300 and 100-K operation. The results suggest that collector profiles designed along the Pareto front at 300 K will likely lie along the Pareto front at 100 K.

title: VulKnow: Enhancing Vulnerability Detection with Structured Knowledge and Large Language Models
authors: Guixiang Liao, Yanli Chen, Wei Ke, Hanzhou Wu, Zhicheng Dong
abstract: The rapid proliferation of Internet-of-Things (IoT) systems has led to increasingly heterogeneous, resource-constrained, and security-sensitive software deployments. In this context, detecting vulnerabilities in embedded and system-level IoT code has become particularly critical, as even a single exploitable flaw may compromise entire networks or devices. Vulnerability detection in real-world software continues to pose significant challenges due to the intricate nature of program semantics, the diversity of vulnerability patterns, and the limited explainability offered by current machine learning models. Although Large Language Models (LLMs) have exhibited remarkable capabilities in comprehending and reasoning about source code, their effectiveness is frequently compromised by inadequate domain knowledge and instances of hallucinated outputs. To address these limitations, we propose VulKnow, a retrieval-augmented framework for vulnerability detection that harnesses structured vulnerability knowledge alongside multi-stage prompt-based reasoning. Specifically, VulKnow first constructs a structured knowledge base derived from authoritative sources such as CWE/CVE reports and standard library specifications. Sub-sequently, it conducts context-aware knowledge retrieval and integrates the retrieved items into an LLM-based initial filtering module. To ensure high-confidence and verifiable results, we design a multi-stage reasoning pipeline that progressively validates vulnerabilities through semantic prompts and consistency checks. Experiments conducted on multiple real-world datasets (Linux, Qemu, Big-Vul) demonstrate that VulKnow significantly enhances precision, recall, and explainability compared to existing static analysis tools as well as LLM-only baselines. This positions VulKnow as a reliable solution for practical vulnerability auditing scenarios.

title: Design and Signal Integrity Analysis of Meander Coupled Microstrip Lines with an Embedded Interdigital Structure for FEXT Suppression_supp1-3697190.pdf
authors: Yevgeniy S. Zhechev
abstract: This paper presents an area-efficient method to suppress far-end crosstalk (FEXT) in high-density meandered coupled microstrip lines by integrating an interdigital stub structure within the inter-trace gap. The proposed topology operates by selectively enhancing the mutual capacitance to equalize the even- and odd-mode phase velocities in inhomogeneous media. Unlike conventional guard traces or periodic structures that require additional routing area or ground vias, the integrated stubs provide FEXT mitigation while maintaining the meander’s original footprint and characteristic impedance stability (50 ± 1.5 Ω). Full-wave simulations and measurements up to 20 GHz demonstrate broadband FEXT suppression, with ∣S41∣ maintained below –20 dB up to 3 GHz. Time-domain analysis at 5 Gbps confirms a 1.46× reduction in peak FEXT and significant jitter improvement. The design achieves a 1.9× improvement in area efficiency compared to the 3W spacing rule, offering a scalable solution for signal integrity in area-constrained PCB designs.

title: A Domain Generalization Framework for Online Aero-Engine Rub-Impact Detection Using Adversarial Learning and Multi-Source Label Aggregation
authors: Haotian Peng, Jie Gao, Yafeng Shen, Wei Wang, Chunyan Hu, Jinsong Du
abstract: Online monitoring of rub-impact faults is essential for aero-engine safety, yet existing methods struggle with asynchronous multi-modal data, diverse sensor configurations, and the scarcity of reliable fault annotations. To address these challenges, this paper proposes RubNet, a domain generalization framework that first employs a physics-guided temporal alignment strategy to precisely align high-frequency vibration signals with low-frequency state parameters based on spectral centroid correlations. A multi-scale channel attention convolutional neural network is subsequently utilized to extract robust features, leveraging adversarial domain generalization to learn representations that remain invariant to shifts in sensor position and orientation. Furthermore, to mitigate label noise during temporal fine-tuning, the framework integrates an uncertainty-aware soft label aggregation mechanism that fuses conflicting annotations from human experts, vision language models, and pre-trained model predictions using the Dawid-Skene algorithm. Experimental validation on real-world ground test data demonstrates that RubNet achieves state-of-the-art performance with an F1-score of 93.26% and superior generalization capabilities across unseen sensor domains, while maintaining low computational complexity suitable for real-time deployment.

title: HDEdit: Editing Videos and 3D Scenes with Video Diffusion Through Hierarchical Task Decomposition
authors: Yanming Zhang, Jun-Kun Chen, Jipeng Lyu, Yu-Xiong Wang
abstract: We introduce HDEdit, a training-free framework for instruction-guided video and 3D scene editing that resolves the fundamental tension between instruction fulfillment and original content preservation through Hierarchical task Decomposition. Our key insight is to progressively decompose complex edits into simpler subtasks. This hierarchical strategy aligns with dual objectives: an LLMguided planner structures high-level subgoals for reliable instruction fulfillment, while embedding-space interpolation further refines each subgoal to preserve unedited content. Two tailored control mechanisms - word-level attention map propagation and parallel denoising synchronization - ensure temporally consistent, hyperparameter tuning-free execution. Beyond video, we extend HDEdit to 3D editing via a simple yet effective render-edit-reconstruct process that maintains strong geometric consistency. Extensive experiments demonstrate our state-of-the-art results across diverse and challenging edits, including longduration videos, fast camera motion, and significant 3D geometric changes.

title: CropCraft: Complete Structural Characterization of Crop Plants from Images
authors: Albert J. Zhai, Xinlei Wang, Kaiyuan Li, Zhao Jiang, Junxiong Zhou, Sheng Wang, Zhenong Jin, Kaiyu Guan, Shenlong Wang
abstract: The ability to automatically build 3D digital twins of plants from images has countless applications in agriculture, environmental science, robotics, and other fields. However, current 3D reconstruction methods fail to recover complete shapes of plants due to heavy occlusion and complex geometries. In this work, we present a novel method for 3D modeling of agricultural crops based on optimizing a parametric model of plant morphology via inverse procedural modeling. Our method first estimates depth maps by fitting a neural radiance field and then optimizes a specialized loss to estimate morphological parameters that result in consistent depth renderings. The resulting 3D model is complete and biologically plausible. We validate our method on a dataset of real images of agricultural fields, and demonstrate that the reconstructed canopies can be used for a variety of monitoring and simulation applications. Project page: https://ajzhai.github.io/CropCraft

title: Neural 4D Scene Reconstruction with Multiple One-Shot Scanning Systems
authors: Ryusuke Sagawa, Kota Nishihara, Takafumi Iwaguchi, Hiroshi Kawasaki
abstract: Recently, 3D reconstruction from multiview stereo (MVS) has advanced significantly with the introduction of neural implicit representation methods, which estimate voxel densities or signed distance fields (SDFs) to describe the 3D structure of a scene. Although such neural-based methods typically require a large number of captured images to estimate dense volumetric information during training, developing systems that can recover the 3D shape of moving objects using only a small number of stationary cameras remains highly demanding and challenging. To address the issue of sparse views, various active lighting techniques have been proposed. However, the problem remains inherently difficult, particularly when attempting to capture the complete shape of an object with a wide baseline. In this paper, we propose a novel approach that combines active lighting with photometric stereo (PS) using neural representations. Additionally, we introduce a multiplexed illumination technique that captures the entire shape of an object in a single shot. Although this results in a low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), our method also addresses this issue. The advantages of our technique are demonstrated through real-world experiments, showcasing its ability to capture a 4D scene.

title: Robotic Arm with Artificial Intelligence for Subsurface Irrigation of Onion in Urban Gardens of Lima
authors: Rommel Anampa Huachaca, William Alexander Pacherres Serquen, Ayrton Krickst Nieves Acosta, Jose Alberto Carrizales Navarro, Joel Jesus Figueroa Vilcarromero, Eddie Angel Sobrado Malpartida
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Mechatronics and Robotics Engineering (ICMRE)
abstract: Urban community gardens in arid cities such as Lima often irrigate uniformly over the soil surface, ignoring the actual root zone and wasting water. This paper presents a vision guided robotic subsurface irrigation system that combines an RGB camera, a Raspberry Pi and a four degree of freedom manipulator equipped with a water injector. A U Net Small network segments the wetted bulb from red onion images; trained on one hundred and seventy six samples, it achieved an intersection over union score of about zero point sixty five and an F1 score of about zero point seventy four, sufficient to localize the active root region. On top of this perception layer, three visual servoing schemes are implemented and compared: image based, pose based and a hybrid controller. The pose based scheme provided the best tracking performance, with complete convergence in roughly four seconds and negligible residual position error. Using standard crop coefficients for onion, the automated subsurface strategy yielded a theoretical application efficiency between ninety two and ninety five percent and an estimated reduction of up to fifty percent in water use compared with gravity irrigation, applying between fifteen and twenty millilitres per plant directly in the root zone. These results indicate that the proposed architecture is a promising low cost platform for precision irrigation in urban gardens of shallow rooted crops such as onion.

title: Cost-Effective XL-MIMO Communication With Cylinder Directly Connected Antenna Array
authors: Xuancheng Zhu, Zhiwen Zhou, Zhenjun Dong, Yong Zeng
abstract: Extremely-large scale MIMO (XL-MIMO) is a key technology for 6G networks, thanks to its superior spatial resolution and beamforming gain. While the recently proposed ray antenna array (RAA) with directly-connected ULAs enables cost-effective beamforming without phase shifters, it may suffer from signal blockage due to coplanar element placement. To address this issue, we propose a cylinder directly-connected antenna array (DCAA) composed of multiple simple uniform circular arrays (sUCAs) in a layered three-dimensional (3D) structure. Each sub-array in sUCA forms a beam aligned with its orientation to achieve full spatial coverage. We characterize the structural design, provide an overall design guideline, and analytically analyze its performance. An input-output signal model is developed for uplink and downlink, along with optimization algorithms to maximize the achievable sum rate. Simulations under 3GPP channels validate the superior performance of the proposed structure, showing that the cylinder DCAA achieves uniform spatial resolution, higher achievable rate, lower hardware cost and power consumption than the conventional ULA with hybrid beamforming (HBF), highlighting its potential for XL-MIMO in millimeter wave (mmWave) and Terahertz (THz) systems.

title: Prism: Semi-Supervised Multi-View Stereo with Monocular Structure Priors
authors: Alex Rich, Noah Stier, Pradeep Sen, Tobias HÖllerer
abstract: The promise of unsupervised multi-view stereo (MVS) is to leverage large unlabeled datasets, yet current methods underperform when training on difficult data, such as handheld smartphone videos of indoor scenes. Meanwhile, high-quality synthetic datasets are available but MVS networks trained on these datasets fail to generalize to realworld examples. To bridge this gap, we propose a semisupervised learning framework that allows us to train on real and rendered images jointly, capturing structural priors from synthetic data while ensuring parity with the realworld domain. Central to our framework is a novel set of losses that leverages powerful existing monocular relativedepth estimators trained on the synthetic dataset, transferring the rich structure of this relative depth to the MVS predictions on unlabeled data. Inspired by perceptual image metrics, we compare the MVS and monocular predictions via a deep feature loss and a multi-scale statistical loss. Our full framework, which we call Prism, achieves large quantitative and qualitative improvements over current unsupervised and synthetic-supervised MVS networks. This is quite a useful result, opening the door to using both unlabeled smartphone videos and photorealistic synthetic datasets for training MVS networks.

title: Hyper Diffusion Avatars: Dynamic Human Avatar Generation Using Network Weight Space Diffusion
authors: Dongliang Cao, Guoxing Sun, Marc Habermann, Florian Bernard
abstract: Creating human avatars is a highly desirable yet challenging task. Recent advancements in radiance field rendering have achieved unprecedented photorealism and realtime performance for personalized dynamic human avatars. However, these approaches are typically limited to personspecific rendering models trained on multi-view video data for a single individual, limiting their ability to generalize across different identities. On the other hand, generative approaches leveraging prior knowledge from pre-trained 2D diffusion models can produce cartoonish, static human avatars, which are animated through simple skeleton-based articulation. Therefore, the avatars generated by these methods suffer from lower rendering quality compared to person-specific rendering methods and fail to capture pose- dependent deformations such as cloth wrinkles. In this paper, we propose a novel approach that unites the strengths of person-specific rendering and diffusion-based generative modeling to enable dynamic human avatar generation with both high photorealism and realistic pose-dependent deformations. Our method follows a two-stage pipeline: first, we optimize a set of person-specific UNets, with each network representing a dynamic human avatar that captures intricate pose-dependent deformations. In the second stage, we train a hyper diffusion model over the optimized network weights. During inference, our method generates network weights for real-time, controllable rendering of dynamic human avatars. Using a large-scale, cross-identity, multi-view video dataset, we demonstrate that our approach outperforms state-of-the-art human avatar generation methods.

title: A Remeshing Method via Adaptive Multiple Original-Facet-Clipping and Centroidal Voronoi Tessellation
authors: Yue Fei, Jingjing Liu, Yuyou Yao, Yusheng Peng, Liping Zheng
abstract: CVT (Centroidal Voronoi Tessellation)-based remeshing optimizes mesh quality via the Voronoi-Delaunay framework, optimizing vertex distribution and generating regular triangles. Current CVT-based approaches can fall into two categories. The former are exact methods, such as Geodesic CVT and Restricted Voronoi Diagrams(RVD), which ensure high quality but require significant computation. The latter are approximate methods, which reduce computational complexity yet compromise quality. To address this tradeoff, we propose a CVT-based surface remeshing method that balances optimization between quality and efficiency via curvature-adaptive multi-clipping of 3D Centroidal Voronoi cells using original surface facets. The core idea of the method is that we adaptively adjust the number of clipping times according to local curvature, and use the angular relationship between the normal vectors of neighboring facets to represent the magnitude of local curvature. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our method.

title: Numerical Design of a Lead-Free Double Absorber Solar Cell Exceeding 35% Efficiency: A SCAPS-1D Simulation
authors: Rabbi Al Nahiyan, Sheikh Hasib Cheragee, Md. Samiul Islam, Jannatul Afroj Akhi, Md. Shoumik Tarafdar, Abu Zafor Md. Touhidul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Perovskite solar cells (PSCs) without lead offer broad light absorption, low weight, stability, and environmental safety, making them strong candidates for next-generation photovoltaics (PV). Although the efficiency of PSC has greatly improved, commercialization of the technology on large industrial scale has not been effective, mainly because of the toxicity of the lead-based perovskites, which are damaging to the environment and shorten the operating duration of the cells. Here, a lead-free double absorber PSC is proposed with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Cs}_{2} \text{TiBr}_{6}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CsSnI}_{3}$</tex> as absorber materials. The thicknesses of these absorbers which had different bandgap characteristics were varied systematically in order to get optimal current matching. Here, the AM 1.5 G solar spectrum was used to compute device performance in SCAPS-1D simulations. After careful investigation, the optimized high-performance structure is found <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F T O} / \mathbf{T i O}_{\mathbf{2}} / \mathbf{C s}_{\mathbf{2}} \mathbf{T i B r}_{\mathbf{6}} / \mathbf{C s S n I}_{\mathbf{3}} / \mathbf{N i O} / \mathbf{A u}$</tex>. The current work also examines the influence of changing in the electron transport layers (ETLs) and absorber-layer thickness on the PV efficacy of the proposed solar cells with two absorbers. The optimized device under normal operating conditions (300 K) had a short-circuit current density <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{J}_{\text{sc}})$</tex> of 34.36 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mA} / \text{cm}^{2}$</tex>, power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 35.15 percent, open-circuit voltage <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{V}_{\text{oc}})$</tex> of 1.1462 V, and fill factor (FF) of 89.25 percent. These findings underscore the high potential of double absorber solar cell (DASC) as effective, stable, and environmentally friendly alternatives to traditional PV technologies.

title: Techno-Economic and Environmental Evaluation of a Floating PV Plant Integrated with Aquaculture for Sustainable Energy and Food Production
authors: Nurul Amin, Mohammad Asif Ul Haq, Rumki Akther Akhi, Aparna Sarker Saku, Md. Sabbir Alam, Ahmed Al Mansur
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: The rapid increase in global energy demand, coupled with the scarcity of usable land, necessitates innovative solutions for renewable energy deployment. This study proposes a 7.26 MWp floating solar photovoltaic (PV) power plant integrated with fish farming, enabling dual benefits of renewable energy generation and aquaculture production. The proposed site is situated in an area surrounded by industrial, educational, and residential zones, ensuring widespread socio-economic benefits. The system was modeled and simulated using PVsyst 7.4 to evaluate energy yield, performance ratio, payback period, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emission reduction. The proposed system is projected to generate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10,174 \text{MWh}$</tex> annually at a Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.0467 / \text{kWh}$</tex>, and achieve full investment payback in 6.1 years. Environmentally, the system could offset 193,303.25 tons of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> from Diesel, 254,346 tons from Coal, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$114,455.87$</tex> tons from natural gas over 25 years, in cases of fish selected at mid and bottom water levels for FPV. Results demonstrate that the hybrid floating solar-fish farm system significantly improves land utilization efficiency, reduces greenhouse gas emissions, and provides reliable energy while supporting local food production. The payback period analysis indicates strong economic feasibility, while the integration of aquaculture enhances community sustainability. This research highlights floating PV-aquaculture hybridization as a viable approach for future sustainable development.

title: A Unified Neural Framework for Fact Verification Using LLM-GNN-LSTM Hybrid Model
authors: Ashraful Goni, Rubiat Jahan Momtahina
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: The rapid expansion of misinformation across online platforms has highlighted the necessity of reliable automated fact verification systems. Existing approaches have often relied on either semantic understanding through pretrained language models or structural reasoning using graph-based methods, but few have successfully integrated complementary paradigms within a unified framework. This study has introduced a hybrid architecture that combined a lightweight language model (DistilBERT) for contextual representation, graph neural networks (GNNs) for relational reasoning between claims and evidence, and bidirectional LSTMs for sequential feature refinement. A gated fusion mechanism was employed to adaptively integrate linguistic and structural information streams, ensuring that the model captured semantic content, relational dependencies, and temporal patterns in a coherent manner. The framework had been trained and evaluated on the Triple- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}$</tex> dataset, achieving 99.95 % test accuracy and 99.97 % F1-score, outperforming established baselines such as GEAR, ProoFVer, and MRR-FV. These results have demonstrated the effectiveness of combining multi-modal neural paradigms for fact verification, providing a methodological foundation for building interpretable, high-precision systems applicable to diverse misinformation detection domains.

title: A Novel Cost-Effective Flexible Denim-Based Wearable Sensor for Tumor and Skin Abnormality Detection
authors: Chowdhury Tasmia Israt, S.M.Ibtisam Ruhan, Aminur Rahman, Jannat Jahan Afifa
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: The development of small, bendable and efficient wearable sensors that can monitor numerous physiological parameters has been accelerated due to the growing demand for wearable healthcare equipment. This research introduces a wearable antenna sensor that uses ring resonator and is embedded on a denim textile substrate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\epsilon=1.605, \tan \delta=0.028)$</tex> with overall dimension <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$47 \text{mm} \times 36 \text{mm} \times 0.56 \text{mm}$</tex>. The suggested design allows for non-invasive monitoring and identification of abnormalities at tissue-level, while keeping the structure mechanically flexible and having a low profile, making it suitable for continuous usage. An initial resonator prototype was created and built to characterize the substrate. Then the effective dielectric constant was determined through experimental validation to guarantee precise performance. The modified sensor shows resonance at 3.793 GHz and 6.782 GHz, with return losses of -30.23 dB and -19.434 dB respectively. The suggested design is confirmed to be feasible since the measured findings demonstrate good alignment with theoretical models and full-wave simulations. To validate the biological applicability of the manufactured sensor, practical evaluations were carried out through on-hand measurements, while bending analysis, tumor detection and SAR assessment were conducted exclusively through simulations. The sensor's appropriateness for wearable application is further validated by the fact that, when bent, it maintains almost no changes in reflection coefficient, bandwidth, gain or efficiency. The results show that the suggested antenna sensor is a strong, flexible and inexpensive option for the next generation of wearable biomedical monitoring systems.

title: Growth of $\varepsilon-\left(\text{In}_{\mathrm{x}} \text{Ga}_{1-\mathrm{x}}\right)_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$ on AlN via Pulsed Laser Deposition
authors: Maria Sultana, Brian Samuels, Pallab Sarkar, Ravi Droopad, Ariful Haque
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> and its alloys are some of the most desirable ultra-wide bandgap semiconductors for high-power and harsh environment electronics, solar blind deep UV photodetectors, deep UV LEDs, toxic gas sensors, and highpower Schottky barrier diodes. In this research, hexagonal <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon$</tex> (In0.15 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left.\text{Ga}_{0.85}\right)_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$</tex> was successfully deposited on AIN for the very first time. The epitaxial <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\varepsilon}$</tex>-(In0.15Ga0.85)2O3 was deposited on wurtzite c-plane (0002) AIN via pulsed laser deposition (PLD), using optimized PLD growth parameters, including the temperature and the oxygen partial pressure. It was observed that a moderate substrate temperature of 550 °C and a relatively high PLD oxygen partial pressure of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.25 \times 10^{-2} \text{Torr}$</tex>, a laser energy density of ∼1.38 Jcm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−2</sup>, and a laser repetition rate of 2 Hz were necessary to successfully grow <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\varepsilon}$</tex>-(In0.15Ga0.85)2O3. The attained bandgap was 4.53 eV. X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) high-resolution depth profile confirmed the uniform presence of indium throughout the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\varepsilon}$</tex>-(In0.15Ga0.85)2 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{O}_{3}$</tex> thin films. With <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 2500$</tex> laser pulses, the attained thickness of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon \left(\text{In}_{0.15} \text{Ga}_{0.85}\right)_{2} \mathrm{O}_{3}$</tex> was ∼ 80 nm, confirmed by ellipsometry.

title: Integrated Multi-Omics Network Pharmacology Uncovers Novel Biomarkers and Therapeutic Candidates in Human Metapneumovirus Infection
authors: Md Kawsar, Dilruba Shareen, Sajib Acharjee Dip, Ram Prosad Sarker, Bikash Kumar Paul, Touhid Bhuiyan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Background: Human Metapneumovirus (HMPV) is a major respiratory pathogen causing severe illness in vulnerable populations. Despite its clinical impact, molecular mechanisms underlying HMPV pathogenesis remain unclear, hindering therapeutic progress. This study applies an integrative multi-omics network pharmacology approach to identify key biomarkers, regulatory networks, and therapeutic targets in HMPV infection. Methods: Multiple transcriptomic datasets (GSE8961, microarray; GSE231788, RNA-seq) of HMPV-infected and control samples were analyzed. Differential expression was determined using platform-specific statistical models and validated fold-change thresholds. Protein-protein interaction networks were constructed and benchmarked against randomized networks. Hub genes were identified via multiple centrality measures with ROC/AUC validation. Comparative pathway analysis with RSV and influenza established HMPV-specific molecular signatures. Drug repurposing was performed using Connectivity Map with stringent statistical validation. Results: Integration of multi-platform data identified 423 significant genes (FDR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.05$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\vert \log 2 \text{FC}\vert >1.2$</tex>). Eight hub genes showed strong biomarker potential (AUC > 0.85): STAT1, IRF7, CXCL10, OAS1, ISG15, IFIT3, HLA-B, and GBP1. Beyond canonical interferon responses, novel pathways were discovered, including autophagy modulation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p=3.2 \times 10^{-9}$</tex> and metabolic reprogramming <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p=1.8 \times 10^{-8}$</tex>. Comparative analysis distinguished HMPVspecific profiles from RSV and influenza. Drug repurposing identified three validated candidates: ribavirin <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(0.82$</tex>, FDR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.001)$</tex>, mycophenolic acid (0.67, FDR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.01$</tex>), and azithromycin (0.54, FDR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.05$</tex>). Conclusions: This multi-platform study provides the first computationally validated network-based characterization of HMPV host responses, revealing novel therapeutic targets and drug candidates with strong translational potential.

title: A Multi-Agent LLM Defense Pipeline Against Prompt Injection Attacks
authors: S M Asif Hossain, Ruksat Khan Shayoni, Mohd Ruhul Ameen, Akif Islam, M. F. Mridha, Jungpil Shin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Prompt injection attacks represent a major vulnerability in Large Language Model (LLM) deployments, where malicious instructions embedded in user inputs can override system prompts and induce unintended behaviors. This paper presents a novel multi-agent defense framework that employs specialized LLM agents in coordinated pipelines to detect and neutralize prompt injection attacks in real-time. We evaluate our approach using two distinct architectures: a sequential chain-ofagents pipeline and a hierarchical coordinator-based system. Our comprehensive evaluation on 55 unique prompt injection attacks, grouped into 8 categories and totaling 400 attack instances across two LLM platforms (ChatGLM and Llama2), demonstrates significant security improvements. Without defense mechanisms, baseline Attack Success Rates (ASR) reached <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 \%}$</tex> for ChatGLM and 20 % for Llama2. Our multi-agent pipeline achieved 100 % mitigation, reducing ASR to 0 % across all tested scenarios. The framework demonstrates robustness across multiple attack categories including direct overrides, code execution attempts, data exfiltration, and obfuscation techniques, while maintaining system functionality for legitimate queries.

title: StrokeGuardian: Hybrid Ensemble ML for Real-Time Brain Stroke Prediction and Clinical Decision Support
authors: Mohammed Sakib Hasan, Abdul Jobayer, Jihadul Islam, Sanjida Islam Nuha, Golam Md. Muradul Bashir
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Stroke is a major cause of morbidity and mortality in the globe which has presented a lot of pressure to effective and comprehensible risk prediction models. My suggestion in this paper is StrokeGuardian, a hybrid ensemble model of the real-time stroke prediction and clinical decision support. It is a combination of nonlinear representation learning, optimization of the boundaries and gradient based boosting that encompasses Neural Networks, Support Vector Machines as well as XGBoost using a weighted-voting model. A pre-processing on a sample size of 35,000 clinical data consisting of seventeen demographic and medical characteristics including an outlier removal process, a feature scaling process and the SMOTE balancing process has been done. The 10-Fold Cross Validation showed the accuracy of the model 99.15, F1-score 99.31 and AUC of 0.9979 which was higher than the benchmark methods. To allow transparency, we implemented Explainable AI methods, e.g. SHAP and LIME which help in the exposure of significant predictors, e.g. age, BMI, average glucose level. It created a web system based on Fast API and React, which is a cloud-based system that can make real-time predictions which can be interpreted. In most cases, StrokeGuardian is a consistent, easy to understand, and implementable system of stroke early warning.

title: A Comprehensive Study on Harnessing Train-Induced Airflow for Wind Energy in Metro Rail System at Dhaka, Bangladesh
authors: Sourav Debnath, Md Abdur Rouf, Md Zahid Hasan, Md Akib Javed, Md Billal Hossen, Kanij Fatema
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: The modern world has prompted research into renewable technologies to reduce reliance on fossil fuel for sustainable future. Though wind energy is one of the nonconventional energy resources that has minimalistic environmental effect, it is much more challenging for setting up in a densely city like Dhaka. This study explores a new way to capture wind energy by using the airflow produced by metro trains. Small turbines placed on station rooftops were modeled for this purpose. Using data collected from smartphone-based GPS measurements along Dhaka Metro Line-6, turbine behavior was simulated in MATLAB Simulink. The findings indicate that, at an average train speed of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$13.42 \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>, a one-meter-diameter turbine with a power coefficient of 0.45 can generate approximately 0.52 kW of power. In our proposed system, around 505 kWh of renewable energy can be produced in a full day operation with cutting carbon emissions by about 171 tons per year. A realistic and sustainable solutions may be suggested to boost wind energy in urban areas at a cost of 117,100 BDT per turbine. It also focuses on incorporation of non-conventional energy system with public transport to lower greenhouse gas emissions and promote long-term urban sustainability.

title: Intelligent Healthcare Scheduling: A Multilingual Rasa Chatbot and Flutter-Based Doctor Appointment Framework
authors: Afia Tabassum, Afroza Sultana, Afrina Noor, Mohammed Shamsul Alam, Farzana Tasnim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Access to timely and organized healthcare services remains a persistent challenge, particularly in settings where manual appointment processes lead to patient dissatisfaction and operational inefficiency. This research presents a smart healthcare scheduling framework that integrates a Rasa-based conversational chatbot with a Flutter mobile platform and a Cloud Firestore database. The proposed system automates key appointment-related activities such as booking, rescheduling, cancellations, and reminders, while supporting both Bangla and English communication. In addition, Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech modules were incorporated to improve accessibility for elderly users and individuals with limited literacy. To optimize the Natural Language Understanding (NLU) component, two configurations were evaluated: the DIET Classifier and a simplified CRF + DIET-Lite model. Experimental evaluation using ten-fold cross-validation demonstrated that the CRF + DIET-Lite model achieved test F1-scores of 0.812 for intent recognition and 0.828 for entity extraction. The results indicate that combining natural language processing with real-time scheduling can significantly reduce missed appointments, simplify administrative workflows, and make healthcare systems more inclusive and responsive in resource-constrained environments.

title: AvatarTex: High-Fidelity Facial Texture Reconstruction from Single-Image Stylized Avatars
authors: Yuda Qiu, Zitong Xiao, Yiwei Zuo, Zisheng Ye, Weikai Chen, Xiaoguang Han
abstract: We present AvatarTex, a high-fidelity facial texture reconstruction framework capable of generating both stylized and photorealistic textures from a single image. Existing methods struggle with stylized avatars due to the lack of diverse multi-style datasets and challenges in maintaining geometric consistency in non-standard textures. To address these limitations, AvatarTex introduces a novel three-stage diffusion-to-GAN pipeline. Our key insight is that while diffusion models excel at generating diversified textures, they lack explicit UV constraints, whereas GANs provide a well-structured latent space that ensures style and topology consistency. By integrating these strengths, AvatarTex achieves high-quality topology-aligned texture synthesis with both artistic and geometric coherence. Specifically, our three-stage pipeline first completes missing texture regions via diffusion-based inpainting, refines style and structure consistency using GAN-based latent optimization, and enhances fine details through diffusion-based repainting. To address the need for a stylized texture dataset, we introduce TexHub, a high-resolution collection of 20,000 multi-style UV textures with precise UV-aligned layouts. By leveraging TexHub and our structured diffusion-to-GAN pipeline, AvatarTex establishes a new state-of-the-art in multi-style facial texture reconstruction. TexHub will be released upon publication to facilitate future research in this field.

title: Deep vs. Shallow Neural Networks for Power Control in Full-Duplex D2D Communication
authors: Bachar Chaabani, Oussama Bazzi
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: In this paper, we investigate power allocation for full-duplex (FD) device-to-device (D2D) communications, a key technology in fifth-generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(5 \mathrm{G})$</tex> and beyond-5G (B5G) networks. The power control problem is addressed using machine learning (ML) techniques trained on a dataset generated via conventional optimization methods. We compare two ML models: a deep neural network (DNN) and a shallow feedforward neural network (FNN). Simulation results demonstrate that the ML-based approach can significantly reduce computational complexity compared to traditional optimization. In terms of prediction accuracy, both methods achieve low average NRMSEs across scenarios, ranging from 1 % to 7 %, with the DNN slightly outperforming the FNN in some cases. However, the DNN incurs <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4}-\mathbf{5}$</tex> times higher inference latency. This highlights that the FNN can provide a practical solution with sufficient accuracy and significantly lower latency for real-time FD-D2D power allocation.

title: Publisher Presentations as Promotional Paratexts
authors: Ryan Stanton, Cassandra Barkman
abstract: None

title: Reducing Electrical Incidents Without Sacrificing Uptime: An IEEE IAS Road Map [Standards News]
authors: Keith Waters
abstract: Provides society information that may include news, reviews or technical notes that should be of interest to practitioners and researchers.

title: Spatiotemporal Deformation Monitoring and Failure–Runout Process Prediction of the Xieliupo landslide
authors: Hezhen Liu, Yongsheng Li, Pengqing Shi, Xiaolong Zhou, Bin Liu, Bingquan Li, Qiang Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The Bailong River Basin is characterized by active tectonics and numerous creeping landslides, with the Xieliupo landslide posing significant potential river-blocking hazards. Conventional interferometric synthetic aperture radar methods are often limited by decorrelation and large deformation gradients in steep terrain. This study utilizes Lutan-1 L-band synthetic aperture radar data using pixel offset tracking and time-series analysis to retrieve the four-dimensional deformation characteristics of the landslide, complemented and cross-validated by high-precision point observations from an artificial corner reflector (CR). Based on deformation partitioning and kinematic interpretation, two representative source-area scenarios were designed for numerical simulations to assess runout pathways and river-blocking risks under extreme conditions. Results indicate that the landslide persistently moves northwestward with marked spatial heterogeneity, exhibiting a partitioned kinematic structure characterized by rear thrust loading, central restraint, lower tensile decoupling, and frontal obstruction. CR monitoring reveals a step-like evolution with alternating acceleration and deceleration. Spring acceleration coincides with the transition of air temperatures from below-zero to above-zero, suggesting that freeze–thaw and snowmelt processes likely play an important role, while rainfall-deformation responses exhibit significant decoupling across stages. Numerical simulations demonstrate that hazard magnitude is significantly constrained by the source-area extent: failure restricted to the rear zone primarily deposits on the central platform, whereas failure involving the middle-to-lower zone may generate rapid long-runout movement and river blockage. These findings provide a useful reference for integrating remote sensing-derived deformation zoning with scenario-based landslide hazard assessment.

title: Adaptive Visual Servoing Control for Closed-Architecture Robotic Systems Without Image-Space Velocity and Joint Velocity
authors: Yu Zhang, Yanlin Yu, Wei Gao, Yafeng Li, Zebin Chen, Changchun Hua
abstract: This article introduces a velocity observer-based adaptive visual servoing control scheme for closed-architecture robotic systems. A novel dual-observer framework, consisting of an image-space observer and a joint-space sliding observer, is developed to avoid the usage of both image-space velocity and joint velocity. Leveraging this framework, an adaptive visual servoing loop control solution is designed, which dynamically generates velocities estimations-based joint position or velocity commands for the torque control loop. Rigorous Lyapunov-based stability analysis demonstrates the asymptotic convergence of both image-space tracking error and observation error, without necessitating modifications to the inner torque controller. This feature renders the proposed method particularly suitable for closed-architecture robotic systems, where the torque controller is unmodifiable due to safety/commercial strategy, and restricted to accepting only joint position or velocity commands. Experimental validation on a robotic manipulator demonstrates the superior performance of the proposed approach.

title: Cluster Fault-Tolerant Consensus Control for Heterogeneous Unmanned Systems With Prescribed Performance
authors: Mengna Li, Ziquan Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The coordination of air-sea heterogeneous unmanned systems (HUSs) can significantly improve the transportation efficiency of maritime transportation systems. However, various faults can affect the control performance and coordination of HUSs, leading to a decrease in the safety and reliability of maritime transportation. Therefore, this paper focuses on the problem of cluster fault-tolerant consensus control (FTCC) with prescribed performance for HUSs consisting of a group of unmanned surface vehicles (USVs) and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) under actuator faults. Firstly, based on pinning control and an error transformation with singularity avoidance, a distributed state estimator with prescribed performance is designed to estimate the output of the cluster leaders, and ensure that the estimation error converges to the predefined range within a prescribed time. Then, lumped fault term in HUSs is compensated by neural networks (NNs), and a new type of updating law for NN weights is designed by the immersion and invariance (I&I) adaptive theory. Finally, by using this error transformation and dynamic surface control, a cluster FTCC law with preset precision and prescribed time is proposed to achieve prescribed performance cluster consensus of HUSs under actuator faults. Lyapunov analysis proves that the formation error of HUSs can converge to the specified range within a prescribed time. Simulation test and comparative analysis verify the effectiveness of the proposed scheme.

title: Early Visual Pathway Origins of SSVEP Intermodulation Components Induced by Luminance Flicker
authors: Dalu Zheng, Weiren Lin, Songyun Xie, Xinzhou Xie, Hao Tang, Bohan Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Dual-frequency modulation strategies in SSVEP-BCIs not only elicit fundamental responses but also induce robust intermodulation components (IMCs) through nonlinear neural integration, significantly expanding the available coding space. While IMCs can arise from various levels of the visual hierarchy depending on the stimulus complexity, the fundamental physiological origins of IMCs induced by pure luminance flicker—specifically whether they arise from retinal nonlinearity or cortical integration—remain debated. To resolve this, we employed a head-mounted display to physically isolate visual fields, comparing dichoptic hemifields (cortical) and monocular hemifield (retinal) conditions across varying spatial densities. Our results provide evidence that IMCs in scalp EEG during luminance modulation originate predominantly from retinal nonlinearities. Furthermore, while the geometric shape of the stimulus exerts a significant influence, the total length of the spatial boundaries separating the two frequencies acts as the dominant determinant of nonlinear output intensity. To elucidate why high-frequency IMCs often appear attenuated despite this robust boundary-driven generation, we constructed a spectral synthesis model (A = β·ITC·Freq<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−α</sup>). This analysis demonstrated that the scalp-recorded amplitude is directly proportional to Inter-Trial Coherence (ITC) but is severely dampened by the brain’s intrinsic 1/f capacitive filtering, confirming that the apparent weakness of high-frequency components is an artifact of physical transmission.

title: Motion Stability of the Levitation With YBa$_{2}$Cu$_{3}$O$_{7-X}$ HTS Bulks and NdFeB Magnets Under Electromagnetic Compensation Subsystem
authors: Chong Lv, Yuxuan Lu, Zhihao Ke, Zigang Deng
abstract: The application of YBa<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$_{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>Cu<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$_{3}$</tex-math></inline-formula>O<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$_{7-x}$</tex-math></inline-formula> high-temperature superconducting (HTS) bulks in magnetic levitation (maglev) trains with NdFeB magnet arrays guideway still faces the problem of attenuation of the levitation force. Therefore, the electromagnetic compensation subsystem (EMCS) has been proposed for this train to enhance its levitation force. However, the introduction of electromagnetic subsystems has affected the self-stabilized environment of the original system. To clarify the boundary conditions for this influence, four levitation modes of the hybrid levitation system have been proposed. Research has shown that under levitation conditions, there is only one equilibrium solution for the hybrid system, and an upper boundary condition exists. Meanwhile, singularities will attract each other and degenerate into single singularities with changes in electromagnetic force, and the stable region will be further squeezed and eventually disappear. The oscillation between the saddle point and the stable point exists in the critical state of the hybrid system. According to the central manifold theorem, hybrid systems will have saddle-node bifurcations, but they are limited by the current intensity. These boundary conditions, combined with existing experiments, can stimulate the application of electromagnetic devices in conjunction with HTS bulks.

title: RBFNN-Assisted Decoupling Direct/Indirect Adaptive Robust Control for Pump-Valve Coordinated Steering System
authors: Jianchao Yu, Xizhe Wei, Jiawei Lin, Fuqi Li, Yunce Zhang, Shizhao Zhou
abstract: Traditional electro-hydraulic steering system (EHSS) in heavy-duty multiaxle vehicle suffers from high energy consumption, limited steering modes, and inadequate accuracy. To solve these challenges, a pump-valve coordinated (PVC) EHSS has been gradually proposed. However, existing control methods for this system still exhibit insufficient tracking accuracy, slow error convergence rate, and compromised safety under demanding conditions. Therefore, this article proposes a radial basis function neural network (RBFNN)-assisted decoupling direct/indirect adaptive robust controller. The direct/indirect adaptive robust control (DIARC) framework is introduced to effectively handle high-order nonlinearities of the system and time-varying damping parameters of the hydraulic cylinders. A decoupling strategy is proposed to address flow coupling between the pump and valve subsystems, ensuring precise flow distribution. Furthermore, an RBFNN is integrated to rapidly predict and compensate for unmodeled mechanical dynamics and external disturbances. Theoretically, the proposed control method achieves a comprehensive compensation for both parametric uncertainties and highly nonlinear unknown dynamics. Meanwhile comparative experiments demonstrate that the proposed controller substantially outperforms traditional methods under various steering conditions, particularly in high-frequency micro-adjustment scenarios, where it effectively suppresses mechanical high-frequency disturbances, achieves rapid error convergence, and delivers the best tracking performance.

title: VS-EXO: An Upper Limb Rehabilitation Exoskeleton Driven by Variable Stiffness Actuators
authors: Maozeng Zhang, Ke Shi, Jiejun Yan, Tongshu Chen, Huijun Li, Aiguo Song
abstract: Upper limb rehabilitation exoskeletons require both safety in physical human–robot interaction and effective assistive support. However, fixed-stiffness joint actuation offers limited adaptability to the varying interaction behaviors and assistance demands during rehabilitation. To address this limitation, this article presents a 4 degrees of freedom (DOFs) shoulder–elbow rehabilitation exoskeleton driven by variable stiffness actuators. Specifically, the shoulder's 3-DOF module employs active variable stiffness actuators, allowing for independent stiffness regulation and task-adaptive compliance. In contrast, the elbow flexion/extension joint incorporates a lightweight passive variable stiffness actuator to reduce distal mass and inertia. In addition, to mitigate singularity issues associated with the quasi-spherical shoulder, the initial joint-axis orientation is optimized to move singular configurations away from commonly used rehabilitation regions. Furthermore, a hierarchical control framework is developed, with the high-level control layer managing training-mode supervision and reference generation, while the low-level control layer implements dedicated position and torque controllers for both the active and passive variable stiffness actuators. Prototype experiments validate the feasibility of the proposed system in both robot-in-charge and human-in-charge training modes, demonstrating that the ability to modulate stiffness effectively supports various rehabilitation tasks by balancing assistance, compliance, and safety.

title: A Multi-Variable Parallel Flexible Switching Control for a Dual Active Bridge Converter Used in an Islanded Photovoltaic Nanogrid
authors: Bing Zhao, Fanrui Chang, Yong Li, Takeyoshi Kato, Christian Rehtanz
abstract: This article proposes a multi-variable parallel flexible switching control (MVP-FSC) for a dual active bridge (DAB) converter in an islanded nanogrid, aiming to stabilize the DC bus voltage and enable a seamless and smooth charge/discharge mode transition. First, eigenvalue analysis is employed to evaluate the stability of the DAB converter’s fourth-order model, and the underlying instability mechanism is investigated. By integrating state feedback based on a reduced order observer (ROO) with proportional-integral (PI) compensation, the unstable poles are eliminated. In this framework, the voltages on both sides and the maximum currents for charge/discharge are selected as control objectives. Based on the transmission characteristics, a dedicated symmetrization process and an objective identification method are developed to determine the instantaneous control variables, thereby achieving DC bus voltage control and seamless charge/discharge mode switching. Finally, an equivalent periodic update method is applied to all the control loops to improve dynamic performance and smooth transient waveforms. The feasibility and superiority of the MVP-FSC are validated through comparative simulations and experiments.

title: Rescue from the Air: Real-Time Data Fusion and Distributed Path Planning for Multi-UAV SAR Applications
authors: Millena Cavalcanti, Bruno José Olivieri de Souza, Thiago Lamenza, Tatiana Reimer, Laércio Lucchesi, Markus Endler
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are revolutionizing search and rescue (SAR) operations by offering advanced capabilities in dynamic environments. This study extends the collaborative multi-UAV MUDE approach to tackle the challenge of locating unknown moving targets under strict time constraints in large search areas. The algorithms operate in real time, allowing UAVs to cooperate within the communication range. This collaboration optimizes their paths, directing them to areas with the highest likelihood of target presence while adapting to the movement of the target. Through up to 10,000 simulations involving UAV swarms and moving ground targets, this study highlights the effectiveness of multi-UAV collaboration compared to traditional methods for static targets. The results show that as the number of UAVs increases, their accuracy improves significantly whereas the time required to find all targets diminishes. Following a sigmoid trend, the accuracy rate reaches a plateau, remaining above 95%, indicating that while more UAVs enhance efficiency, larger swarms have diminishing returns. This approach is particularly effective in environments with clustered targets, making it suitable for disaster response scenarios. This study offers a computationally efficient solution for multi-UAVcollaborative SAR missions, providing insights into adaptive path planning and information sharing strategies that can significantly enhance search and rescue operational capabilities.

title: Joint Communication and Computation Scheduling for MEC-enabled AIGC Services: A Game-Theoretic Stochastic Learning Approach_supp1-3696564.pdf
authors: Lin Gao
abstract: Artificial Intelligence Generated Content (AIGC) powered by Generative Diffusion Models (GDMs) has emerged as a transformative paradigm for automated content creation. To satisfy the stringent latency requirements of AIGC services in many edge intelligence scenarios (e.g., smart cities), Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) provides critical computational support by deploying GDMs at edge servers (ES) close to end users. This paper investigates an MEC-enabled AIGC network comprising multiple ES, wireless access points (APs), and mobile users (UEs) with heterogeneous latency and accuracy demands. We formulate a Joint Communication Association and Computation Offloading (JCACO) game, where each UE strategically selects its serving AP, ES, and inference steps to minimize the overall service completion time while meeting accuracy constraints. The problem is challenging due to the network dynamics and the incomplete information. We prove that the JCACO game is a potential game under both complete and stochastic information settings, ensuring the existence of Nash Equilibrium (NE) in both cases. To derive the NE efficiently, we develop a distributed Multi-Agent Stochastic Learning (MASL) algorithm that provably converges to the NE with strict performance guarantees. Unlike conventional best-response schemes, MASL requires neither the knowledge of other players’ strategies nor global network information, making it fully distributed and adaptive to dynamic environments. We further provide a strict theoretical convergence analysis for MASL by using Ordinary Differential Equations (ODEs). Simulation results demonstrate that MASL significantly reduces service completion time compared with benchmark methods while satisfying accuracy constraints, confirming its effectiveness and practicality for real-world MEC-enabled AIGC networks.

title: GZTrust: Topology-Aware Trust Management for IoT Networks under Zero Trust Principles
authors: Xingwu Wang, Dongqi Han, Jie Yuan, Xinghai Wei, Keji Miao
abstract: Due to the large-scale, dynamic topology and multi-hop communication characteristics of Internet of Things (IoT) systems, trust management has become a critical requirement for secure and reliable network operation. However, existing trust management schemes generally rely on endpoint-centric observations or centralized evidence aggregation, making them poorly suited to realistic IoT deployments with partial observability. In particular, performance degradations caused by compromised intermediate nodes or unstable links are often misattributed to benign devices, leading to distorted trust evaluation and inefficient trust-based control. To address these challenges, this study proposes a topology-aware trust management framework (GZTrust) for multi-hop IoT networks designed in accordance with Zero Trust principles. The GZTrust framework introduces a structured separation between trust evidence generation, trust reasoning, and trust-driven control. In the data plane, a TrustTrace mechanism is employed to incrementally collect hop-level trust evidence along forwarding paths with verifiable integrity and privacy-preserving pseudonyms. In the trust plane, dynamically selected policy enforcement points (PEPs) aggregate localized evidence in a topology-aware manner to avoid flat centralized processing. To accurately evaluate trust, this study designs an attribution-aware trust computation model that decomposes hop-level anomalies into node-centric and link-centric responsibility components. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that GZTrust consistently outperforms state-of-the-art trust management schemes, improving the F1-score by approximately 6–13% across varying malicious node ratios (5%–50%). These results indicate that GZTrust provides a practical and scalable trust management framework for dynamic multi-hop IoT environments, with a lightweight overhead profile that is well aligned with resource-constrained deployment settings.

title: Research on Influencing Factors of Airgap Windage Losses and Establishment of an Improved Windage Losses Prediction Model
authors: Bin Liu, Jia Fu, Zhongjun Yu
abstract: Airgap windage losses can account for over 30% of all losses in high-speed permanent magnet motors (HSPMM), highlighting the necessity of conducting a detailed investigation into the factors affecting the windage losses within the airgap. In this study, the shaft-type airgap—recognized as the most representative configuration—is selected as the research subject. First, an accurate numerical method based on computational fluid dynamics (CFD) is verified to calculate windage losses. Subsequently, a quantitative analysis is performed to evaluate the effects of geometric parameters (e.g., radius ratio η and aspect ratio χ), flow characteristics (e.g., tip Reynolds number Reδ and axial Reynolds number Rea), and surface roughness (e.g., roughness height Ks) on windage losses within the airgap. The results indicate that increases in η, Rea and Ks lead to a higher friction coefficient Cf, whereas an increase in Reδ results in a reduction in Cf. Moreover, Cf exhibits minimal sensitivity to variations in χ. The underlying mechanisms of these relationships are analyzed from the perspective of flow field details. Furthermore, based on the numerical results, an improved prediction model of Cf is developed. This improved model broadens the applicable flow range and demonstrates significantly enhanced accuracy compared to the original empirical models. The findings of this study provide a theoretical foundation for the structural and thermal management design of HSPMM.

title: CPCoCo: Localization-Free Collaborative Perception via Attentive Contrastive Consensus
authors: Langkun Xu, Chenyang Li, Zhongxiang Zhao, Lin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Collaborative perception compensates for the limitations of individual agents by facilitating information exchange among multiple agents, with relative pose transformations serving as the foundation for effective collaboration. Current collaborative perception systems rely heavily on precise localization information for relative pose transformations, but achieving robust localization in real-world scenarios remains a significant challenge. To overcome this, we introduce CPCoCo, a robust collaborative perception framework that eliminates the need for external localization. Specifically, we propose a feature-based attention contrastive learning module. It utilizes an attention mechanism to identify similar structures within features, thereby resolving cross-view feature inconsistencies. Concurrently, it employs a consistency consensus mechanism to filter for more reliable feature matching, ultimately achieving the alignment of multi-agent coordinate systems. The proposed approach can be seamlessly integrated into existing multi-agent collaborative object detection frameworks for end-to-end training. Extensive experiments on both real-world and simulated datasets demonstrate that CPCoCo achieves state-of-the-art detection performance in localization-free settings, with an average processing time of approximately 18 ms. Code is available on <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/saki-mobile/cpcoco</uri>

title: A New Isolated Series-Resonant AC-DC Converter for EV
authors: Rupesh Kumar Yadav, Jahangeer Ahmad Dar, Wajahat Khan, Mukesh Kumar Pathak
abstract: This article proposes an isolated series resonant (ISR) AC–DC converter with reduced voltage stress. The proposed topology employs a split-capacitor half-bridge (SC-HB) configuration with bidirectional AC-side switches and incorporates a semi-active rectifier (SAR) on the DC side to eliminate secondary-side circulating current and prevent reverse power flow. This effectively controls the power conversion through phase-shift control. The proposed converter eliminates transformer saturation and achieves zero-voltage-switching (ZVS) turn-on for all switches. The reduced voltage stress allows, using low-voltage devices with lower drain-to-source on-state resistance and output capacitance. The optimized operation, keeps conduction losses comparable while significantly reducing switching losses. As a result, total losses are reduced. This enables operation at higher switching frequencies while maintaining high efficiency. A self-regulated mechanism is introduced to suppress transformer saturation in the ISR AC–DC converter. A 1-kW experimental prototype is developed, achieving full ZVS over the entire line cycle with unity power factor (UPF) and high efficiency across a wide range of input voltage and load conditions; the results closely match the theoretical analysis, and validating the working of the converter.

title: InsoNet: Time Frequency Representation using Superlet Transform and CNN for Automated Insomnia Detection from ECG Signals
authors: Chaitanya Suman Mogadati, Chandan Nayak
abstract: Sleep is a vital physiological process essential for physical and mental restoration. Insufficient sleep is linked to various disorders, including narcolepsy, insomnia, sleep periodic limb movement disorder, and apnea. Insomnia is one of the most common sleep disorders, marked by problems falling asleep, staying asleep, or getting soothing rest, which can result in serious health issues. Traditional methods for insomnia detection, such as polysomnography, are costly, time-consuming, and require specialized clinical equipment. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes an automated and cost-effective approach for insomnia detection using ECG signals. Time-frequency representations in the form of spectrograms are generated using the Superlet transform (SLT) and fed into InsoNet, a custom-designed convolutional neural network (CNN) framework. InsoNet’s performance is evaluated against pretrained models such as AlexNet, MobileNetV2, and VGG19 on the sleep disorder research centre (SDRC) and cyclic alternating pattern (CAP) datasets. On the CAP dataset, InsoNet achieved 99.64% accuracy (Acc), 99.65% sensitivity (Sen), 99.64% specificity (Spe), 99.65% precision (Pre), 99.65% F1-score (F1s), a critical success index (CSI) of 0.99, and both false omission rate (FOR) and false discovery rate (FDR) of 0.36. On the SDRC dataset, InsoNet attained 99.3% across Acc, Sen, Spe, Pre, and F1s, with a CSI of 0.99 and FOR and FDR of 0.71. These results confirm that InsoNet outperforms existing models and demonstrates strong potential for clinical implementation.

title: Optimizing Hardware Acceleration for Learned Image Compression with Parallel Context Model
authors: Chen Chen, James Yen, Zhibai Huang, Weidong Qiu, Zhengwei Qi, Haibing Guan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Learned image compression (LIC) methods have approached or even surpassed advanced traditional methods (e.g., AVIF, JPEG-XL) in terms of compression bitrate and quality. Among them, autoregressive context models (ARCMs) have greatly improved the accuracy of entropy models, but strict Z-ordered serial decoding in ARCM leads to unacceptable latencies (∼ 50× latency overhead). Parallel context models achieve rate-distortion performance improvement close to ARCM while requiring only ∼ 10% − 40% of the latency overhead, but also introduce additional computational redundancy. In addition, most research ignore the en-/de-coding speed and only focus on the optimization of rate-distortion (RD). To tackle the above challenges, this paper optimizes the LIC model in both channel dimension and spatial dimension. In the channel dimension, considering the channel-wise priority characteristics in LIC models, slimmable neural network and variable bitrate optimizations are introduced to determine the execution of sub-models based on the dynamically available resources, achieving joint optimization of rate-distortion-complexity. In the spatial dimension, the computational redundancy of parallel context model (e.g., the structured redundancy of context features) is optimized, and the corresponding computation mode is unified with the existing modes (e.g., transposed convolution) in LIC models. Parameterized accelerator is designed for evaluation. Experimental results show that slimmable optimization realizes multiple RD trade-offs within single LIC model. Hardware/Software optimization of LIC model with Checkerboard context model achieve ∼ 1.97 − 4× performance improvement. Slimmable channel optimization achieves 1.52 − 2.07× gains to eliminate tail latency in computation module.

title: Classifying Indigenous Lifestyle Levels in Chattogram Hill Tracts Using Machine Learning and Ensemble Approaches
authors: Saikat Das Roky, Dhiman Sarma, Momotaz Jahan Moon, Ajit Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: The Chattogram Hill Tracts (CHT), located in southeastern Bangladesh, is inhabited by a variety of Indigenous communities, such as the Chakma, Marma, and Tripura, who encounter several socio-economic difficulties stemming from geographic isolation, climate change, and modernization. These elements hamper their traditional ways of life and restrict access to healthcare, education, and economic prospects. This study highlights the pressing requirement for a data-driven framework to classify lifestyle levels within these communities, establishing a basis for targeted interventions that safeguard cultural heritage while fostering sustainable development. The methodology incorporates a structured pipeline that utilizes a dataset consisting of 1,800 survey responses that include 36 attributes. Data preprocessing involved addressing missing values and encoding categorical variables, followed by the assignment of weights to the attributes to reflect factor significance through expert-weighted scores. The Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) was employed to balance the class distribution. Subsequently, models such as Random Forest, Logistic Regression, and Support Vector Machine were trained, with the most effective ones integrated into Weighted Average and Majority Voting ensemble frameworks. Performance was evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and confusion matrices. The Weighted Average ensemble model achieved a testing accuracy of 96.11 %, demonstrating exceptional capability in identifying lifestyle levels that are at risk. The findings provide valuable insights for policymakers to develop culturally sensitive interventions.

title: Optimized Boosting Approach for EEG-Based Emotion Classification with Feature Selection and Weighted Ensemble Learning
authors: Mohima Binte Rasel, Mst. Fatematuj Johora, Md. Abid Hasan Rafi, Pankaj Bhowmik, Md. Delower Hossain, Tangina Sultana
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Accurate recognition of human emotional states from electroencephalogram (EEG) signals is critical for applications in affective computing, brain-computer interfaces, and mental health monitoring. In this study, we present a comprehensive EEG-based emotion classification framework that integrates feature selection, hyperparameter optimization, and ensemble learning to maximize predictive performance. Raw EEG signals were preprocessed, standardized, and stratified into training and testing subsets to preserve class distributions. A baseline eXtreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) model was first trained to establish performance benchmarks and identify salient features. Using these selected features, an optimized XGBoost model was developed, with hyperparameters systematically tuned via cross-validation. To enhance robustness, a weighted ensemble combined predictions from the baseline and optimized models, with contributions weighted according to individual accuracies. The proposed approach achieved 99.53 % accuracy with near-perfect ROC AUC scores, outperforming existing methods while maintaining interpretability. Feature importance analysis with LIME and SHAP identified minimal and mean EEG values across key frequency bands as primary classification drivers, demonstrating a robust and scalable framework for EEG-based emotion recognition.

title: Hybrid EfficientNet-CBAM Deep Learning Framework for Automated Brain Tumor Classification from MRI Images
authors: Kazi Mubashir Tazwar, Anika Rahman Shapnil
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Early diagnosis of brain tumors is very crucial for initial screening and treatment planning of the diseased individuals. If achieved, it can play a significant role in improving survival chances in brain tumor cases. But manually interpreting MRI can be very time-consuming at times and even these results can differ among doctors. To address this issue, we developed a deep learning-based approach that incorporates EfficientNet-B3 and Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) to automatically classify brain tumors. We used CLAHE to enhance contrast so that tumor areas are easier to see before training. We also used data augmentation processes like rotation, flipping, color jitter and stratified <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5}$</tex>-fold cross-validation for testing. A mix of focal loss and cross-entropy loss helped balance the classes during training. Our proposed model achieved an accuracy of 94% and performed well across all the classes like glioma, meningioma, pituitary, and healthy samples. That is how it proves to be a useful tool for early screening and diagnosis of brain tumor in clinical applications.

title: Throughput Optimization in Cooperative Spectrum Sensing for Cognitive Radio Networks Using Deep Learning Based Models
authors: Laila Arjumand Zannat, Nawaz Talukder Sanglap, Khandaker Al Arafat, Sk. Shariful Alam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Women in Engineering (WIE) Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (WIECON-ECE)
abstract: Secondary users can make advantage of vacant frequency bands without interfering with major or primary users thanks to Cognitive Radio Networks (CRNs). To do this successfully, secondary users must determine a trade-off between sensing time and data transmission time. Several deep learning-based cooperative spectrum sensing models, including Transformer, CNN-BiLSTM-GRU, CNN-LSTM-MLP, CNNLSTM, and CNN-GRU, are used in this study to examine this tradeoff. The same system parameters are used to test all the models. Parameters are 100 kHz sampling frequency, a secondary user SNR of 20 dB, 100 ms frame duration and a primary user SNR of −14 dB. With a minimum detection probability value of 0.9 the throughput is computed with the help of conventional sensing throughput calculation. The results show that all models work best with an approximate sensing duration of 2.56 ms. Transformer model achieves the highest normalized throughput of 66.30 percent of the maximum throughputs. It has been discovered that spectrum sensing performance and throughput in cognitive radio networks can be greatly improved by using attention-based models.

title: Toward Sovereign Foundation Models for the Mena Region: Balancing Efficiency, Cultural Alignment, and Data Sovereignty
authors: Houssem Eddine Lassoued, Habib Fathallah, Anis Ben Aicha
publish: 2026 6th Middle East and North Africa Communications Conference (MENACOMM)
abstract: The rapid advancement of foundation models has created an urgent imperative for the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region to develop sovereign AI capabilities that align with local languages, cultural values, and regulatory frameworks.
